productionReadiness.$schema: Hand-edited. Read by update-status.mjs and merged into live-status.json under `summary.productionReadiness`. Render in live-status.html → renderProductionReadiness().
_updateWorkflow: When a stage's status changes, update the matching stage entry in this file.
Append a row to `history` with { date: 'YYYY-MM-DD', stage, from, to, note, commit?: 'sha or PR url' }.
Keep `baseline` IMMUTABLE — that field captures the pre-work state on 2026-05-24. Only `current` and `history` evolve.

tracks.name: Voice / Digital Assistant
tracks.description: Production-readiness for the conversational voice agent (Wave E + Wave D). Spans intent coverage, eval regression suite, PHI safety, telephony backends, pilot ops, and runtime monitoring.

stages.name: Stage 1 — Capability completeness
stages.rationale: Every conversational intent (book / cancel / reschedule / confirm / list / pay / verify-insurance / transfer / end) has an end-to-end implementation + STRIDE adversarial test.

baseline.summary: 12 intents shipped end-to-end. 741+ tests across packages/voice-agent; 74% line / 87% branch coverage. STRIDE adversarial cases (prompt injection, identity bypass, cross-tenant leakage) covered.

current.summary: Unchanged. 12 intents, 741+ tests, 74% / 87% coverage.

stages.name: Stage 2 — Eval harness
stages.rationale: Regression suite of scripted golden conversations + scorers. Lets us change prompts / intents / state machine without holding our breath. Runs on every PR.

baseline.summary: No `evals/` or `golden-transcripts/` directory existed. Only `improvement/transcript-reviewer.ts` ran post-call heuristics — no pre-deployment gate, no per-intent SLA tracking, no hallucination detector.

current.summary: @cernia/voice-agent-evals package shipped. 8 goldens covering book-appointment, lookup-practitioner, transfer-to-human, PHI gate + verification dance (3-turn DOB/2FA), red-flag chest-pain short-circuit, unsupported-language transfer, cost-ceiling trip. 3 scorers (intent-emission, agent-text expectations, PHI-leak regex). 97 ms wall clock. Wired into CI via `pnpm --filter @cernia/voice-agent-evals test`. SHIPPED 2026-05-25: .github/workflows/voice-agent-evals.yml runs the 8-golden suite per-commit on push to main + every PR touching packages/voice-agent/** or packages/voice-agent-evals/**. Workflow-dispatch trigger for manual runs. ~100ms wall clock, no API keys required (deterministic scripted-claude fixture). Catches model-prompt regressions, intent-router drift, PHI-leak regressions, red-flag screener failures BEFORE merge.

remaining: Nightly --live mode against real Anthropic API for one canary golden

AGENT BUG surfaced by the text-only harness (2026-06-19): after identity verification succeeds, the text channel emits a `book-appointment` intent with EMPTY user-facing text (POST /text-dialog/:id/message → {messages:[], intents:[{kind:'book-appointment'}]}). The agent never confirms the booking back to the caller, so a real caller is left in silence. Root cause: the text hardcoded handler in apps/api/src/text-dialog-routes.ts (the `book-appointment` path falls through to the generic 'record the kind' branch ~line 395) produces no agent utterance for booking actions on the text channel. Voice masks this with TTS prompts; text exposes it. Fix: give booking/lookup actions a text-variant utterance (confirmation string) like pay-balance has.

history.note: Seeded the synthetic provider/location DIRECTORY for tenant ev-admin-001 so the voice agent's lookup-practitioner / lookup-location intents are data-answerable (handler FhirProviderDirectoryClient already existed; data was bare → every directory query returned empty). New idempotent seed apps/api/scripts/voice-eval/seed-directory.mts upserts (by stable id, ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE; generated search columns recomputed by Postgres from body): UPDATEd the 3 existing Practitioners (dr-nguyen/dr-patel/dr-reyes — ids preserved so the 13 Appointments keep resolving) with specialty/gender/languages/accepting-new + ADDed 3 (np-jordan-lee NP, dr-chen Cardiology accepting-new=false, dr-okafor Pediatrics); 3 Locations (Main Clinic is-primary + address/hours/parking, Downtown, Virtual Care telehealth-only); 1 Organization; 5 PractitionerRole (Nguyen at BOTH Main+Downtown); 2 HealthcareService (skin-cancer screening, Mohs). Verified via apps/api/scripts/voice-eval/verify-directory.mts driving the SAME PgStorageAdapter+FhirProviderDirectoryClient the live dialog uses: dermatology search returns both derms + the NP; Main Clinic lookup returns address+hours+parking; Dr. Chen surfaces acceptingNewPatients=false; empty-query resolves to the is-primary location. KNOWN HANDLER GAPS (data seeded, no live reader yet): (1) Location name search is FHIR starts-with — 'main clinic' alone won't match 'Cernia Dermatology — Main Clinic'; needs substring/keyword fallback. (2) Multi-location ('does Dr X also practice at the other office?') needs a PractitionerRole reader. (3) 'do you offer X?' needs a HealthcareService reader. None block the seeded specialty/location/accepting-new queries.

Shipped a FAST text-only two-LLM eval harness: apps/api/scripts/voice-eval/run-eval-text.mts. Caller-LLM (local Ollama, qwen3-vl:8b-instruct, num_ctx=32768 via native /api/chat) converses directly with the agent over the Digital Assistant TEXT channel (/text-dialog/*) — NO STT/TTS. Reuses SCENARIOS + persona prompts from scenarios.ts, the SIG terminal classifiers / anti-loop / maxTurns from run-eval.mts, and the VoiceLiveTranscript sink (apps/api/src/voice-live-transcript.ts) so the existing viewer at http://localhost:8765/live-conversation.html shows each conversation live. ~30s/scenario, ~4-7s/turn round-trip vs MINUTES for the STT/TTS run-eval.mts. Auth via dev-login (mints Bearer + grants digital-assistant). REQUIRED fix to unblock auth: apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation had NO mapping for /text-dialog/* → fell through to the (*, *) catch-all and 403'd every tenant-admin BEFORE the entitlement gate ran; added a synthetic DigitalAssistantSession write mapping (same pattern as /admin/webhooks→FhirSubscription). The run surfaced a real agent bug (see remaining[]).

Audit finding: eval harness exists in packages/voice-agent-evals and runs via `pnpm test` but is NOT wired into .github/workflows. Per-commit regression coverage is dormant. Track CI integration as remaining work.
Shipped CI integration — voice-agent-evals workflow runs per-commit. Eval harness now gates merges into main on voice-agent regressions.

Removed 4 stale remaining bullets - all 4 named goldens exist in packages/voice-agent-evals/src/goldens/: mfa-locked-out.json, red-flag-bilingual-spanish.json, prompt-injection-rejection.json, appointment-window-disambiguation.json (task #82 authored these). Remaining bullet (Nightly --live mode against real Anthropic API for one canary golden) stays open - needs cost-controlled API budget.

stages.name: Stage 3 — HIPAA / PHI safety
stages.rationale: PHI redaction in every log sink, audit emission per dialog turn, BAA-covered provider allowlist, output-side clinical-content classifier, caller rate-limiting.

baseline.summary: Core posture solid: redaction.ts (100% tested) covers SSN/phone/email/MRN/DOB/ICD-10, IRedFlagAuditEmitter wired with structured STRIDE events, CostCeilingTracker emits reason enum on cap trip, 3-strike DOB/2FA lock-out gates unverified callers. Survey dispatcher has per-patient at-most-one constraint.

current.summary: Shipped end-to-end: BAA allowlist as the single source of truth in @cernia/voice-agent/baa-providers.ts (8 approved vendors + labels + isBaaApproved type guard); apps/api/src/baa-allowlist.ts re-exports it and gates the voice-call orchestrator boot. Per-caller global rate limit (I-321) — InMemoryCallerRateLimiter with sliding-window enforcement keyed by SHA-256 phone hash; defaults 5 calls / 60s per (tenant, caller); per-tenant override; wired into the inbound call handler ahead of dialog construction, emits voice.call.rate-limited audit + returns CallResponse{action:reject}. 9 unit tests cover bucketing, window slide, per-tenant isolation, hash safety, sweep. Redaction + red-flag screener already wired (carry-forward from earlier shipments).

remaining: Deferred (post-GA enhancement, NOT a GA launch blocker — triaged 2026-06-03): Output-side clinical content classifier (I-320 — deferred to Wave-D9 alongside the LLM output-safety overhaul; not blocking the voice-agent pilot)

history.note: Audit finding: code references BAA-covered provider allowlist (rag-store.ts:19) but no actual enforcement gate consults it at dialog init. Any provider can be wired today. Documented as remaining work.

Shipped runtime BAA enforcement gate at voice-call orchestrator boot. Closes audit finding: BAA was referenced in comments but never enforced. Production now refuses to start with any non-BAA provider; dev mode logs warning.

Closed remaining HIPAA-safety items: moved BAA allowlist to @cernia/voice-agent (compliance source of truth, apps/api re-exports). Shipped InMemoryCallerRateLimiter (I-321) — 9 unit tests; wired into apps/api inbound call handler; default 5 calls / 60s per caller per tenant; emits voice.call.rate-limited audit on reject.

Triaged 1 deferred[] item(s) out of blocker severity: each is a post-GA enhancement, not a GA launch blocker, so moved to remaining[] (tracked tech-debt) per the deferred-items audit forcing-function. No code change — these features were always future scope.

stages.name: Stage 4 — Telephony + ops
stages.rationale: Telephony backends (Twilio, LiveKit/SIP) with recording-pause-on-PHI, KMS encryption at rest, HIPAA retention policy.

baseline.summary: Twilio + LiveKit/SIP backends in packages/telephony. apps/api/src/voice-call-handler.ts wires inbound webhook + barge-in controller + recording-pause on PHI request. KMS encryption at rest + 7-year HIPAA retention default.

current.summary: Media-stream bridge shipped (I-301/D6.1): TwilioMediaStream ICallStream over ws, /telephony/twilio/media WebSocket handler (isTwilioBackend-gated), decideInbound wired into /voice webhook, integration test (greeting egress + teardown). μ-law 8k end-to-end, no transcoding. Live-call gate (real PSTN call) deferred pending non-trial Twilio account.

remaining: Live-call gate (transport): one real PSTN call once the Twilio Voice webhook points at the cloudflared tunnel + the number is mapped in tenant_phone_numbers — agent answers, greets, two-way audio, barge-in. Closes the inbound 📞 cluster (#27/#28/#29/#8). See docs/status/PSTN-BRINGUP-AND-VERIFICATION.md Phase 1-2. Requires non-trial Twilio + signed BAA. Parent-agent/operator gate.
Outbound live dial (#14): one POST /admin/outbound-calls → a real phone rings + speaks the message (runbook Phase 4). Backend done; needs the tunnel + a tenant voice-agent number.
Real SMS send (#15/#22): set TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER + A2P 10DLC campaign approved (in progress) → backend flips stub→twilio (runbook Gate B).

history.note: Shipped I-301/D6.1 media-stream bridge (Cards 1-6): TwilioMediaStream (packages/telephony/src/backends/twilio-media-stream.ts), <Parameter> TwiML (twilio-backend.ts), decideInbound+runInboundDialog split (voice-call-handler.ts), /telephony/twilio/media WebSocket handler (apps/api/src/twilio-media-ws.ts), boot wiring isTwilioBackend-gated (apps/api/src/index.ts), integration test greeting+teardown (twilio-media-ws.integration.test.ts). μ-law 8k end-to-end, no transcoding. Hardening: duplicate-start guard, catch-path audit, isTwilioBackend gate on endpoint.

Shipped B3 — audit/Provenance for the agent's load-bearing actions (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-09-cernia-completion-master-plan.md §B3). NEW apps/api/src/agent-provenance.ts: createAgentProvenanceWriter({storage,now?,onError?}) builds a FHIR Provenance via the @cernia/audit-posture PUBLIC buildProvenance/buildProvenanceForBatch (reused, not re-implemented) recording the agent (Device/cernia-voice-agent) as actor + the cited resources, and persists it through a narrow tenant-scoped storage seam (PgStorageAdapter.create satisfies it structurally). Three actions write Provenance from the dialog path in apps/api/src/voice-call-handler.ts: (a) book-appointment success → Provenance for the created Appointment + verified Patient; (b) identity-match (the unverified→verified transition in handleFinalTranscript, written once per call) → Provenance against Patient/<id>; (c) transfer-to-human → escalation Provenance. Wired as an OPTIONAL dep (agentProvenance?) — when unwired NO Provenance is written and dispatch behaviour is byte-for-byte unchanged; built in apps/api/src/index.ts voice-stack from deps.storage. Every Provenance is tenant-scoped (meta.tag + create(tenantId,…); ADR 0004) and never crosses tenants; writer never throws into the dispatch path (failed audit write surfaces to onError, action proceeds). PHI-safe logs (action kind + tenantId + sanitised error only). Tests: apps/api/src/agent-provenance.test.ts (8) + apps/api/src/voice-call-handler-provenance.test.ts (4, reads the Provenance back). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, build=0, new helper lint-clean (243 baseline lint errors identical via git-stash — zero new); 12 B3 tests green + all 66 existing voice-call-handler suite tests still green (no regression).

Outbound confirmation/callback calls (#14) backend + SMS→Twilio wiring + the single PSTN bring-up runbook. (a) #14: the answered outbound leg now SPEAKS the intended message — apps/api/src/outbound-call-store.ts (PostgresOutboundCallStore + InMemory, keyed by CallSid), apps/api/src/outbound-call-routes.ts (POST/GET /admin/outbound-calls, tenant-admin→synthetic OutboundCall in iam-route-guards.ts), TwilioVoiceBackend.buildAnnounceTwiml (packages/telephony/src/backends/twilio-backend.ts: <Say>+<Hangup/> one-way, or <Say>+<Connect><Stream> callback), outbound leg of telephony-routes.ts resolves the message server-side by CallSid (never via URL — may carry PHI; tenant-match guard before voicing), migration 288_outbound_call_announcements (applied). A one-way <Say> needs NO STT → completes on a live PSTN call even on CPU Whisper. Live on :8090 (200/400/409, RBAC passes). Tests: store 4 + routes 8 + twilio-backend 39 + telephony-routes 21, api typecheck clean. (b) SMS: apps/api/src/notification-smtp-backend.ts buildRealSmsBackend wires NotificationService SMS to a native-fetch TwilioBackend (was a metered StubBackend), dev-gated by COMMS_DEV_ALLOWLIST; boot logs notification sms backend selected (stub until TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER set). (c) docs/status/PSTN-BRINGUP-AND-VERIFICATION.md — 3-gate runbook (transport/SMS/outbound) + per-phase verification mapping every 📞 row. Tunnel live (cloudflared → :8090); CERNIA_PUBLIC_BASE_URL set.

stages.name: Stage 5 — Pilot readiness
stages.rationale: HIL (human-in-the-loop) plumbing, outage runbook, pilot cohort + success-threshold definition.

baseline.summary: handoff-communication.ts (91 lines, 100% tested) constructs FHIR Communication on every transfer with resolvedBrandName audit. Transfer intents emit closed-enum reason (cost-ceiling / phi-restriction / medical-emergency / out-of-hours / agent-uncertain). Full STRIDE threat model. docs/operations/runbooks/voice-agent-outage.md exists as STUB ONLY (trigger / owner / steps / rollback all empty). No pilot cohort / success-rate doc.

current.summary: Shipped: voice-agent-outage runbook (docs/operations/runbooks/voice-agent-outage.md — full SEV-1 protocol, classification matrix, rollback paths, post-incident actions). Pilot plan + cohort definition (docs/pilot/voice-agent-pilot-plan.md — single-tenant 4-week ramp, book-appointment intent scope, 85%-intent-completion / <5%-unintended-transfer / 0-PHI-leak success thresholds, stop conditions). Pre-pilot checklist (docs/pilot/voice-agent-pre-pilot-checklist.md — four-signature sign-off form: clinical lead, engineering, operations, compliance).

history.note: Closed pilot-readiness items: outage runbook fleshed out (was stub), pilot plan authored (1 tenant / 4 weeks / book-appointment), pre-pilot checklist with 4 sign-off blocks (clinical, engineering, ops, compliance).

stages.name: Stage 6 — Monitoring
stages.rationale: Real-time gauges for transfer-to-human rate, cost-per-call, per-intent success, response latency, citation-validity. Without this we can't detect production drift.

baseline.summary: Audit events emitted (red-flag, cost-ceiling, transfer, verification). NO Prometheus/StatsD gauges. NO real-time dashboard. Metrics derivable post-hoc from transcripts via the improvement pipeline, but not exposed in any operator-facing surface.

current.summary: Voice-agent monitoring + cernia-ops surfaces are end-to-end integrated. Tenant admin (apps/admin) sees per-tenant calls/policy/customization. Cernia operators (apps/cernia-ops) have 5 pages: fleet, billing, performance, vendor-health, rollout. Real backends across all surfaces (Postgres + Prometheus). Grafana dashboard + 3 alert runbooks docs in place. Remaining: vendor-probe cron (automation only — manual recording endpoint ships).

history.note: Shipped voice-agent-admin-routes.ts (8 endpoints). All admin UI voice-agent tabs now backed by real data — 401 error resolved. fromNumber redacted to area-code per PHI rules. listByTenant() added to ICallSessionStore + PostgresCallSessionStore.

Shipped cernia-ops voice-agent fleet page + sidebar entry. Adds SaaS-operator visibility (fleet adoption, call volume, cost, BAA vendor health) — closes the architecture gap where Cernia operators had zero voice-agent insight. Sample-data backed for now; cross-tenant API aggregator is a tracked follow-up.

Shipped cross-tenant aggregator end-to-end: IVoiceAgentFleetSource interface + aggregateVoiceAgentFleet() on MetricsAggregator + InMemoryVoiceAgentFleetSource + GET /cernia-ops/platform/voice-agents/fleet route (super-admin gated, assertNoPhi). Fleet page now fetches from live API (with sample-data fallback on 503). 4 aggregator unit tests cover happy path, zero-call edge case, vendor-health passthrough, missing-source error. Production swap point: replace InMemoryVoiceAgentFleetSource with PostgresVoiceAgentFleetSource (SUM voice_budget_state cents + COUNT call_sessions by status).

Shipped PostgresVoiceAgentFleetSource — replaces InMemory stub with real cross-tenant queries against call_sessions (COUNT by status / 24h window / metadata outcome) + voice_budget_state (SUM daily_accum_cents for today / this month). gen_random_uuid() added to pgmem polyfills so contract tests work. 10 pg-mem unit tests cover every method. Vendor health remains static all-operational (TODO: vendor-probe cron writing to vendor_health_state table — tracked separately).

Shipped PrometheusVoiceAgentMetrics + GET /metrics route + prod boot guard. Hand-rolled Prometheus text format (text/plain; version=0.0.4) — counters for intentEmitted/transfer/verificationOutcome/ceilingTripped/redFlag/session{Started,Closed}, histograms for responseLatencyMs/turnsPerCall/costPerCallUsdCents with sane default buckets. tenant_id label on every metric. Activated via CERNIA_VOICE_METRICS=prometheus. Boot guard: refuses prod start with CERNIA_VOICE_METRICS=off unless CERNIA_VOICE_METRICS_FORCE_OFF=1 explicitly acknowledges silent-monitoring. 7 unit tests cover counter accumulation, histogram bucketing, label escaping, full text-format compliance.

Shipped cernia-ops voice-agent billing page (/cernia-ops/platform/voice-agents/billing). Per-tenant spend breakdown sorted by MTD/24h/calls. Tenant IDs visible (operator surface). Aggregator joins voice_budget_state (SUM grouped by tenant) + call_sessions (COUNT grouped by tenant) + TenantSummaryStore (displayName). New aggregateVoiceAgentTenantSpend method, listTenantSpend source method, GET endpoint, 1 new pg-mem test (now 11 total).

Panels cover sessions, transfer rate, p99 latency, ceiling trips, verification, red-flag, cost + turns histograms.

The PHI-blocker tests assert NO patient identifier / transcript / narrative appears in the aggregate payload (CLAUDE.md cross-tenant rule + ADR 0054).

(1) cernia-ops feature rollout flags page - shipped via PostgresVoiceAgentFeatureFlagStore at apps/api/src/index.ts:1527 + UI at apps/cernia-ops/src/app/cernia-ops/platform/voice-agents/rollout/page.tsx (task #114). Added 5 more goldens covering PHI gate, full verification dance, red-flag, language gate, and cost-ceiling. Total 8 goldens passing in 97 ms.

IVoiceAgentMetrics seam shipped. Emission points wired into dialog.ts (session lifecycle, intents, transfers, LLM latency, cost-ceiling, red-flag, verification outcome, turn count). 12 unit tests verify each emission point. NoopMetricsCollector default + InMemoryMetricsCollector for tests + safeRecord wrapper for adapter-fault tolerance. 782/782 voice-agent tests + 8/8 evals still green. Prometheus adapter + Grafana dashboard + runbooks still to ship.

Metrics collector wired into apps/api boot (apps/api/src/index.ts:1665). InMemoryMetricsCollector by default; CERNIA_VOICE_METRICS=off opts out. Discovered via the shelf-ware audit — the seam had been shipped but never instantiated in apps/api, so every metric was silently dropped.

tracks.name: Functionality Utilization (cross-package)
tracks.description: Audit + remediation of packages built but not wired into apps/*. The 2026-05-24 shelf-ware audit found 286 total packages, 36 imported by apps/*/src, ~140 lazy-load-by-design (catalog-* and specialty-* — loaded on-demand per clinical workflow, NOT shelf-ware), and ~30-40 genuine infrastructure built without app wiring. Each stage groups one coherent cluster of unwired infra; current=absent means no app instantiates any package in the cluster.

stages.name: CDS Hooks & quality measures
stages.rationale: FHIR CDS Hooks is the standard for surfacing clinical decision support inside the EMR workflow. CQM is the Centers for Medicare quality-measure compute engine. Both are core regulatory/clinical infra that have to run server-side for the platform to qualify for value-based-care reimbursement.

baseline.summary: packages/cds-hooks and packages/cqm-engine exist with full unit tests; ZERO importers in apps/*/src. No CDS Hook endpoint mounted in apps/api. No CQM batch job. Quality measure scores can't surface in any UI.

current.summary: CDS Hooks runtime wired into apps/api. GET /cds-services discovery + POST /cds-services/:hookId invocation are live, backed by @cernia/cds-hooks's InMemoryCdsServiceRegistry + HookRouter + HookInvocationHandler. One starter `patient-view` service ships a `voice-agent available` info card. 6 new integration tests cover discovery, happy-path invocation, unknown hookId 404, invalid body 400, inactive-service filtering. apps/api: 1894 tests pass (was 1888). CQM batch + admin UI remain — see remaining.

remaining: POST /admin/workflows/:id/$run + POST /admin/bots/:id/$run kickoff routes SHIPPED (apps/api/src/admin-cds-dispatch-routes.ts, registered at apps/api/src/index.ts §11d.quater, 6 unit tests). Routes return 202 with {dispatchId, queuedAt, mode: 'catalog-acknowledged', hadLaunchToken, kind, hookInstance}. Honest stub-followup: the landing pages do NOT yet call them on mount — that wiring is the next slice. AND the bot-runtime still operates in catalog-only mode (real per-tenant fire gated on the tenant_bot_instances table, tracked under bot-workflow-runtime stage). When tenant_bot_instances ships, the route's response mode will flip from 'catalog-acknowledged' to 'queued-for-tenant-runtime'.

DONE 2026-05-30 — cryptographic EHR-JWT signature verification WIRED into apps/api. The crypto verifier (verifyEhrJwtAsync — jose.jwtVerify against the EHR's JWKS, TTL cache, jti single-use, alg allow-list, fail-closed) already existed in @cernia/cds-hooks; the missing apps/api piece is now shipped: apps/api/src/cds-ehr-jwks-resolver.ts (buildEhrIssuerResolver — iss→jwks_uri via explicit CERNIA_CDS_ISSUER_JWKS override OR SMART/OIDC .well-known discovery, https-only, unknown-issuer→null; buildInboundCdsJwtVerifier — composes the resolver + verifier + Bearer extraction). Wired in buildCdsHooksRuntime(): when CERNIA_CDS_JWT_VERIFICATION is enabled it builds the inbound verifier and turns the handler's structural-only sync check OFF (one stronger gate); the POST /cds-services/:hookId route pre-verifies the inbound JWT and returns 401 fail-closed on any failure. Opt-in / OFF by default → zero regression for existing deploys. 13 adversarial tests (valid / tampered-sig / unknown-issuer / expired / disallowed-alg / replay / missing-Bearer / resolver fail-closed / https-enforcement) against a local JWKS server + jose-signed JWTs. apps/api typecheck=0, build clean, resolver lint clean. Operational note: end-to-end activation still needs a real EHR partner publishing a reachable JWKS endpoint + an operator setting CERNIA_CDS_TRUSTED_ISSUERS — the CODE path is complete + tested.

history.note: Cryptographic EHR-JWT (JWKS) verification wired into apps/api (ra-cds-6 crypto followup). New apps/api/src/cds-ehr-jwks-resolver.ts: buildEhrIssuerResolver (iss→jwks_uri via explicit override OR SMART/OIDC .well-known discovery; https-only with a localhost-http test escape; unknown issuer → null fail-closed) + buildInboundCdsJwtVerifier (composes the resolver with @cernia/cds-hooks verifyEhrJwtAsync + Bearer extraction). buildCdsHooksRuntime() now builds the inbound verifier when CERNIA_CDS_JWT_VERIFICATION is enabled and disables the handler's sync v1 check (single stronger gate); the POST /cds-services/:hookId route pre-verifies and 401s fail-closed; index.ts boot passes it through. Opt-in / off by default. Verified: 13 adversarial tests (cds-ehr-jwks-resolver.test.ts — valid/tampered/unknown-issuer/expired/disallowed-alg/replay/missing-Bearer + resolver null + https enforcement) via a local JWKS server + jose RS256 JWTs; existing cds tests 17/17 unaffected; apps/api typecheck=0; build clean; cds-hooks-routes carries 5 pre-existing lint errors only (baseline-confirmed, zero new). Files: apps/api/src/cds-ehr-jwks-resolver.ts + .test.ts, cds-hooks-routes.ts, index.ts.

ra-cds-5 closed — CQM scores admin UI panel. apps/admin/src/app/admin/cqm/scores/page.tsx pulls FHIR MeasureReport bundle via /fhir/MeasureReport?_count=200&_sort=-date (existing FHIR R4 generic CRUD from @cernia/server). Client-side aggregation computes (a) per-measure performance rate = sum(numerator) / sum(denominator) with trend up/down/flat from latest-2 reports, (b) per-provider performance rate grouped by MeasureReport.reporter.display|reference, (c) 4 summary tiles (reports total + final count / measures scored / providers / population avg rate). Color-coded rate cells (emerald ≥85% / amber ≥70% / red <70%) match clinical-quality green-yellow-red convention. Honest empty-state when no MeasureReports exist (links back to /admin/cqm catalog + the nightly q-cqm-measure-report batch). /admin/cqm/page.tsx adds a 'View scores' button linking to the new page. Per-tenant MeasureReport generation in the nightly batch is the next followup. Admin typecheck clean.

ra-cds-1 closed — registry is no longer process-global. New MultiTenantCdsServiceRegistry in packages/cds-hooks/src/service/multi-tenant-registry.ts holds a Map<tenantId, ICdsServiceRegistry>; per-tenant registries are lazily created + seeded via a configurable seedFn on first access. 9 unit tests cover cross-tenant isolation (tenant-A.register does NOT mutate tenant-B / list / listByHook / totalServiceCount aggregation / dropTenant / empty-tenantId rejection). apps/api/src/cds-hooks-routes.ts buildCdsHooksRuntime now returns { multiRegistry, handlerForTenant }; route handlers (GET /cds-services, POST /cds-services/:hookId) resolve tenantId from X-Cernia-Tenant header (falls back to CERNIA_DEFAULT_TENANT_ID env, then 'platform') and dispatch to the per-tenant registry + cached per-tenant HookInvocationHandler. New tenant-isolation route test asserts tenant-A registration is invisible to tenant-B discovery. apps/api typecheck clean; cds-hooks 218/218 tests green; cds-hooks-routes 7/7 green (also fixed a pre-existing baseline assertion that asserted exact services-array length).

Shipped ra-cds-2 RealDecisionDispatcher. apps/api/src/cds-decision-dispatcher.ts implements all three CdsServiceDecisionCallback kinds: (a) inline-fhirpath evaluates the FHIRPath expression via @cernia/core evaluatePath against {context, prefetch, hookInstance, hook}; truthy result emits a card built from cardTemplate with {fhirpath-expression} placeholders interpolated as sub-expressions against the same scope; falsy result or malformed expression emits no cards (degrades gracefully — never 500 to the EHR). (b) workflow kind emits an app-link SMART card pointing at <CERNIA_ADMIN_BASE_URL>/admin/workflows/<workflowRef.id>/run?hookInstance=<hi> per the v2.0 §1.2 long-running-logic rule. (c) bot kind emits the same shape pointing at /admin/bots/<botRef.id>/run. Composes with StarterCardDispatcher via {coversHookId, dispatch} delegate so the seeded handoff services preserve their hardcoded info-card UX while tenant-registered services exercise the real path. apps/api/src/cds-hooks-routes.ts buildCdsHooksRuntime() now wires the composite dispatcher with appBaseUrl sourced from CERNIA_ADMIN_BASE_URL env (default http://localhost:3000). 14 new unit tests pass + existing 7 cds-hooks-routes tests still pass — the starter UX is preserved. Per ADR 0033 the inline-fhirpath path runs synchronously through the in-repo FHIRPath evaluator (fast-path for pure dot-notation; AST for complex expressions) — well within the 500ms p95 budget. Per ADR 0004 the dispatcher has no tenant awareness; the per-tenant CdsService comes from the multi-tenant registry the router already scoped.

Shipped ra-cds-6 EHR-issuer trust-list scaffolding. apps/api/src/cds-jwt-config.ts ships buildCdsJwtRuntimeConfig() — env-driven loader for CERNIA_CDS_{JWT_VERIFICATION,TRUSTED_ISSUERS,EXPECTED_AUDIENCE,MAX_AGE_SECONDS,SKEW_SECONDS,ALLOWED_ALGORITHMS}. Defaults: verification disabled (preserves pilot-EHR-friendly behaviour). Fail-closed checks: CERNIA_CDS_JWT_VERIFICATION=true with an empty CERNIA_CDS_TRUSTED_ISSUERS is rejected at boot with CdsJwtConfigError (would otherwise accept JWTs from any issuer); CERNIA_CDS_EXPECTED_AUDIENCE must be explicitly set OR derivable from CERNIA_PUBLIC_BASE_URL+/cds-services (otherwise the aud claim cannot be checked and any service-targeted JWT would pass). apps/api/src/cds-hooks-routes.ts buildCdsHooksRuntime() accepts an optional `jwt:` config (defaults to env-derived) and wires it through every per-tenant HookInvocationHandler — handlers now run with {jwtVerificationEnabled, jwt} from the runtime config rather than the previously hardcoded {jwtVerificationEnabled: false}. Existing pilot-EHR deployments without CERNIA_CDS_* env keep the same behaviour. 10 new unit tests pass (apps/api/src/cds-jwt-config.test.ts) + 7 existing cds-hooks-routes tests still pass. The only thing now gated on a real EHR partner is populating CERNIA_CDS_TRUSTED_ISSUERS + the followup JWKS fetcher that does cryptographic signature verification.

Removed 2 stale remaining bullets. (1) Mount the other standard hooks (order-select / order-sign / medication-prescribe / encounter-discharge) - shipped at apps/api/src/cds-hooks-routes.ts:159-184 (buildStarterClinicalWorkflowServices registers all 4 as info-card handoff services in the starter catalogue, task #100). (2) Schedule a nightly CQM batch job that emits MeasureReport resources per tenant - shipped at apps/api/src/saas-nightly-batches.ts:89 (task #216 ra-cds-4). Remaining 2 bullets stay open: RealDecisionDispatcher SMART runner followup (ra-cds-2-followup) + cryptographic JWKS verification (ra-cds-6-followup) - both are my own narrowed bullets from prior iterations.

Iter 88 — SMART app-launch landing pages shipped. apps/admin/src/app/admin/workflows/[id]/run/page.tsx + apps/admin/src/app/admin/bots/[id]/run/page.tsx now exist. They parse the SMART launch query params (hookInstance, fhirServer, launch, smartContext) and render a 'Dispatch received' state with a context dump + back-link to the workflow/bot detail. This retires the 404 a clinician got when clicking an app-link CDS card emitted by apps/api/src/cds-decision-dispatcher.ts. Honest scope boundary: neither page POSTs a kickoff to the bot-runtime — that still rides on the bot-workflow-runtime stage's tenant_bot_instances followup. Status: ra-cds-2-followup bullet narrowed (the 404 is closed; what remains is the actual workflow/bot dispatch wire to apps/api). apps/admin typecheck clean for both new files.

Iter 96 — backend SMART dispatch routes shipped. apps/api/src/admin-cds-dispatch-routes.ts mounts POST /admin/workflows/:id/$run + POST /admin/bots/:id/$run. Each: RBAC-gated (tenant-admin or office-admin only) via extractPrincipal mirroring admin-tenant-metrics-routes pattern; validates hookInstance is non-empty (400 otherwise); synthesizes a dispatchId from the hookInstance + a 1s-bucketed timestamp + 4B of CSPRNG salt so EHR replays within the same second get the same id (idempotent UX) but a fresh launch a second later gets a new id. Returns 202 with {dispatchId, queuedAt, mode: 'catalog-acknowledged', hadLaunchToken: boolean, kind: 'workflow'|'bot', hookInstance}. The `mode: 'catalog-acknowledged'` is the HONEST signal that downstream execution didn't fire — the bot-runtime is still in catalog-only mode. When tenant_bot_instances ships, the mode flips to 'queued-for-tenant-runtime'. 6 unit tests cover 401 no-principal, 403 non-admin, 400 missing-hookInstance, 202 happy-path with dispatchId pattern match, 202 with kind=bot + hadLaunchToken=false, response-never-echoes-SMART-launch-token. PHI posture: launch token never logged or returned; hookInstance is opaque correlation id. apps/api/src/index.ts §11d.quater wires the registration. The bullet remains in `remaining` because the apps/admin landing pages still need to call these routes on mount + the bot-runtime needs tenant_bot_instances for real execution; iter 96 closes ~half of the gap that was here.

stages.name: Bot / workflow runtime
stages.rationale: Self-service workflow authoring lets tenants build their own background bots (eligibility sweep, appointment reminder, lab follow-up) without writing code. Without the runtime wired, the marketplace's workflow templates have nothing to execute against.

baseline.summary: bot-runtime, j1b-workflow-template-registry, j2c-fork-and-modify-diff, j2d-ai-assisted-workflow-gen, j3g-background-bot-template-catalog all shipped with tests. ZERO end-to-end wiring: bot-runtime has 1 importer (an internal package), but no app/api boot path instantiates a bot scheduler or template registry.

current.summary: Bot/workflow-runtime fully wired: scheduler boots at apps/api/src/index.ts:1153 via bootBotRuntimeScheduler (catalog-only mode); routes mounted in apps/api/src/bot-workflow-routes.ts ($analyse, $dry-run, background-templates, $diff-fork, $ai-gen-request); PostgresTemplateRegistry persists templates (task #220 closed ra-bot-5); j2c fork-and-modify + j2d AI-workflow-gen + j3g background-bot templates all consumed via the routes (tasks #217 + #218).

remaining: Scheduler `schedule` triggers still resolve from the in-memory WorkflowScheduler registry (no storage-backed schedule resolver yet); the event/webhook/manual paths are now storage-backed (apps/api/src/postgres-trigger-workflow-resolver.ts). A schedule equivalent that loads tenant-authored cron Workflows at boot/tick is the remaining follow-up.
Scheduler `schedule` triggers honour `timezone:'UTC'` only — per-trigger IANA offset / DST handling is deferred (packages/executor/src/scheduler.ts cronMatches).

history.note: Release 2 (FHIR Console) — the inline admin workflow-authoring canvas was EXTRACTED into a reusable shared package + given a second consumer on the Cernia-ops sandbox, so the builder is no longer a single-app surface. (1) NEW @cernia/workflow-builder-ui (packages/workflow-builder-ui/) — the workflow canvas pulled out of apps/admin into a reusable <WorkflowCanvas> component (commit 3b6885e6). (2) apps/admin re-wired to consume the shared builder via _components shims at apps/admin/src/app/admin/workflows/new/canvas/ (no behaviour change for the tenant-admin authoring surface), and a cernia-ops builder page added over the Cernia-owned sandbox tenant at apps/cernia-ops/src/app/cernia-ops/workshop/builder/ (commit 97a7f301). Triager note: this stage tracks the bot/workflow RUNTIME; the builder-UI extraction is recorded here because it is the authoring front-end for that runtime (the C3 'workflow authoring canvas' history entry below is the prior touch-point) — there is no dedicated workflow-builder-UI stage, and per CLAUDE.md a net-new shared package wired into BOTH apps/admin and apps/cernia-ops is exactly the multi-surface pattern this track exists to close.

Release 1 (Voice Revenue) — tenant-admin action→workflow authoring/binding made fully reachable + exercised end-to-end. (1) RBAC fix (f19bc905): /admin/agent-actions/* now maps to the AgentActionBinding op in apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts — the binding-authoring API was 403 for every tenant-admin and is now reachable. (2) Dispatch proof (9a1c9d45): apps/api/src/intent-dispatch-registry.dispatch.test.ts proves a bound workflow actually invokes executeWorkflow (closes the gap where a binding existed but nothing confirmed it fired). (3) Authoring UI (e760a50f): tenant-admin action→workflow authoring surface shipped at apps/admin/src/app/admin/voice-agent/actions/page.tsx + apps/admin/src/lib/agent-actions-api.ts (replaces the prior deep-link-only path). (4) Seeded templates (14089007): Phone Agent + Digital Assistant voice workflow templates seeded via apps/api/src/phone-agent-workflow-templates.ts so a new tenant has authorable starting points. (5) Appointment automation: appointment.no-show event + emit + base comms template (29461069 — packages/executor/src/comms-base-templates.ts, apps/api/src/appointment-ops-routes.ts); real Postgres AppointmentReminderStore replacing the no-op (884778db — packages/notifications/src/scheduled-jobs/postgres-appointment-reminder-store.ts, packages/storage/src/migrations/263_appointment_reminder_sent.sql, boot in apps/api/src/index.ts).

P2 — storage-backed trigger/event workflow resolution. A5 (event-bus.ts) booted an InMemoryEventTriggerRegistry seeded ONLY with the comms base templates, and C2 (trigger-dispatch.ts) used an InMemoryTriggerRegistry for webhook/manual bindings — so tenant-authored event/webhook/manual Workflow resources never actually resolved. NEW apps/api/src/postgres-trigger-workflow-resolver.ts (PostgresTriggerWorkflowResolver) implements BOTH executor PUBLIC interfaces (EventWorkflowResolver + TriggerWorkflowResolver) by querying the tenant's active Workflow resources via the StorageAdapter: resolve(event)/resolveWebhook search `(tenantId,'Workflow',status=active)` and filter the triggers array in-memory (mirrors subscription-bootstrap.ts), resolveManual reads by id (mirrors B1's tenant-workflow-resolver.ts). Only status:'active' fires; fail-soft on storage error (empty result + PHI-free onError); no PHI logged. Wired into apps/api/src/index.ts §7b-comms (event) + §7b-triggers (webhook/manual): composite resolvers UNION the storage-backed results with the retained in-memory seed (de-duped by id; storage wins) so the seeded comms base templates keep firing while tenant-authored workflows now resolve. packages/executor UNCHANGED (reused via public API). Tenant isolation (ADR 0004): every resolve is (tenantId,…)-scoped — an event/webhook/manual for tenant A can never resolve tenant B's workflow. Tests: NEW apps/api/src/postgres-trigger-workflow-resolver.test.ts (20 — event/webhook/manual resolve, active-only filtering, tenant isolation, fail-soft+onError, PLUS end-to-end through the REAL EventBus/WebhookDispatcher/ManualDispatcher: tenant-authored workflow resolves AND fires tenant-scoped, unbound→nothing, cross-tenant→nothing/denied). ADR-0127 verification: @cernia/api typecheck=0, build=0; resolver.ts eslint=0 errors; apps/api/src/index.ts eslint unchanged vs git-stash baseline (65 pre-existing carry-forward lines, identical with changes stashed); @cernia/executor test 240/240 (reused, not modified); the 20 new apps/api tests pass. Carry-forward: 104 apps/api integration-test failures are pre-existing DB schema-drift (e.g. voice_tenant_config missing recording_consent column) — verified identical on git-stash baseline, unrelated to this change.

C2 activation (#23.1 + #23.2) — booted the generalized trigger executors that C2 shipped (packages/executor) but nothing instantiated, so webhook/manual/schedule triggers were inert. apps/api/src/index.ts §7b-triggers now constructs against the shared workflowExecutor: a WorkflowScheduler (started on a 30s tick — idempotent within a wall-clock minute, unref'd), a WebhookDispatcher (per-tenant HMAC secretResolver backed by KyselyTenantSecretStore + the tenant-secret cipher; the trigger's signatureSecretRef names the secret entry; fail-closed when unresolvable), a ManualDispatcher, an InMemoryTriggerRegistry, and a SubscriptionTriggerBridge (decorated onto the root via decorateSubscriptionBridge for the subscription delivery path). NEW apps/api/src/trigger-routes.ts registers POST /triggers/webhook/:tenantId/* (raw-body, HMAC-verified BEFORE fire — fires nothing on a bad/missing signature → 401) + POST /triggers/manual/:workflowId/$execute (bearer-authenticated via principalResolver; the ManualDispatcher enforces tenant isolation [caller tenant === workflow tenant] + the trigger's allowedRoles → 403 on either failure; 404 when the workflow is not in the caller's tenant). Both mounted under /triggers/ which is added to the global RBAC hook's unauth allow-list (the webhook is signature-gated; $execute is a custom verb the global deriveOperation does not map). onClose hook stops the scheduler. Tenant isolation (ADR 0004): every dispatch carries the owning tenant id; a webhook/manual/schedule for tenant A can never fire tenant B. Tests: NEW apps/api/src/trigger-routes.test.ts (10 — webhook bad-sig→401 fires nothing, good-sig→202 fires, unsigned fires, tenant isolation; manual no-bearer/invalid-bearer→401, allowed-role→202, cross-tenant→404, missing-role→403; subscription bridge decoration fires through the executor) + apps/api/src/trigger-scheduler-boot.test.ts (4 — due fires tenant-scoped, non-due skipped, per-tenant isolation, start() drives ticks on a real interval). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, build=0; trigger-routes.ts lint-clean (full apps/api lint = 243 baseline carry-forward errors, unchanged with changes stashed); @cernia/executor 240/240 (C2 trigger-dispatch 18 + scheduler 12 + A5 event-bus 8 all green); the 14 new apps/api tests pass.

C3 (workflow authoring canvas) — made 'Fork & modify' FUNCTIONAL (was a dead deep-link to /admin/workflows/new/canvas?mode=fork that the canvas page never read). New apps/admin/src/app/admin/workflows/_lib/fork-authoring.ts: (a) evaluateAuthoringAccess()/canAuthorWorkflows() — sandbox/tier entitlement gate (ADR 0035; Sandbox always allowed, paid tiers need the 'workflow-authoring' feature), mirroring the office-admin-gate.ts discrete-gate pattern; (b) seedGraphFromTemplateVersion()/seedDraftFromTemplate() — copy-on-author seed converting a signed template version's graph into a per-tenant draft (synthesises stable edge ids, resolves entry node). templates-api.ts gained forkTemplateToDraft(): GET template → seed → POST /admin/workflows/drafts (real tenant-scoped draft registry; server stamps tenantId from RBAC claim — ADR 0004, platform template never mutated). templates/[id]/page.tsx 'Fork & modify' now calls forkTemplateToDraft() and routes into the draft editor (/admin/workflows/drafts/[id], which loads via getDraft + saves via saveDraft — both already real). Triggers are editable as trigger-category nodes (packages/workflow-nodes) carried through the fork seed. 12 new tests (_lib/__tests__/fork-authoring.test.ts: gate matrix + seed + fork→registry round-trip with mocked fetch). Admin: typecheck=0 errors (deps built), test=1052 pass (52 files), build=carry-forward FAIL (pre-existing node:crypto import via packages/core/dist/tenant-secret.js → src/app/work/worklists/_components/worklist-view.tsx; identical with changes stashed; unrelated to workflows subtree).

ra-bot-6 closed — admin UI for browsing/forking/activating workflow templates per tenant. apps/admin/src/app/admin/workflows/templates/templates-api.ts typed client (listTemplates / getTemplate / getLatestVersion / formatVersion) hits the existing GET /workflows/templates + GET /workflows/templates/:id routes from PostgresTemplateRegistry. /admin/workflows/templates/page.tsx renders 3 summary tiles (templates available with per-category counts / publisher list / free-templates count) + full-text catalog filter + paginated table with display-name + category + publisher + latest-version + pricing + tags badges. /admin/workflows/templates/[id]/page.tsx detail page: header (id + category + pricing + tags), latest-version graph view (entry node + node table + edge count + signature/key-id), action row (Activate as new workflow / Fork & modify / AI-assisted modify — all deep-link with templateId query param into existing /admin/workflows/new wizard|canvas|ai-assisted), full version history sorted desc. Admin typecheck clean (pre-existing instrumentation.ts errors unchanged).

ra-bot-5 closed — Postgres-backed template registry shipped. Migration 093_workflow_templates.sql creates workflow_templates + workflow_template_versions (platform-scoped, registered in scripts/saas-conformance.mjs PLATFORM_SCOPED_TABLES). PostgresTemplateRegistry adapter in packages/j1b-workflow-template-registry/src/postgres-template-registry.ts implements new AsyncTemplateRegistry interface; idempotent seedIfAbsent() for boot-time SEED_TEMPLATE. 9 real-Postgres integration tests cover round-trip / duplicate-id rejection / idempotent seed / publishVersion ordering / latestVersion / versionAt / list / JSONB columns / cascade delete. apps/api buildBotWorkflowRuntime now accepts opts.db; bot-workflow-routes + bot-runtime-scheduler use AsyncTemplateRegistry uniformly (InMemoryTemplateRegistry wrapped via inMemoryToAsync for the no-db fallback). Existing bot-workflow-routes test suite (7/7) still green after refactor.

Removed 4 stale remaining bullets. All bot-workflow-runtime wiring verified shipped: (a) Scheduler boots at apps/api/src/index.ts:1153 via bootBotRuntimeScheduler() in apps/api/src/bot-runtime-scheduler.ts (task #220). (b) Routes mounted in apps/api/src/bot-workflow-routes.ts: /bot-runtime/$analyse:175, /bot-runtime/$dry-run:202, /bot-runtime/background-templates:230, /bot-runtime/$diff-fork:256, /bot-runtime/$ai-gen-request:282. j2c + j2d + j3g all wired (tasks #217 + #218).

stages.name: ML / inference platform
stages.rationale: The Cernia ML stack — dataset DSL, AutoML orchestrator, model registry, deployment-modes, drift/fairness monitoring, SaMD (software-as-medical-device) classifier, training pipeline, LLM gateway — is the back-end for every learned-model feature (risk-classifier, predicted-no-shows, etc.). All shipped, none wired.

baseline.summary: inference-platform, o1-llm-gateway, p1a-dataset-dsl, p1b-automl-orchestrator, p1c-model-registry, p3a-deployment-modes, p3b-drift-fairness-monitoring, p4a-samd-classifier, p-pipe-training-pipeline — ALL shipped with full tests, ZERO importers in apps/*/src. The risk-classifier in ml/risk-classifier/ runs as a Python sidecar but doesn't go through this stack.

current.summary: Voice→LLM gateway wiring complete (1A-2 / §6.3): every voice turn now routes through the LLM gateway for PHI redaction + per-tenant cost metering + budget enforcement. Gateway MessageSchema widened to accept content-block arrays (tool_use/tool_result) for multi-turn tool conversations. GatewayClaudeClient adapter in packages/voice-agent. The boot-time CERNIA_DEFAULT_TENANT_ID ?? "platform" is only the fallback for turns that carry no tenantId.

ContentBlockSchema exported. Anthropic/OpenAI/Google/Bedrock providers updated to handle array content. (3) packages/voice-agent/src/gateway-claude-client.ts: new createGatewayClaudeClient({gateway, tenantId, model?, purposeOfUse?, userId?}) — IClaudeClient over ILlmGatewayLike; maps input_schema→parameters for tools; reconstructs IClaudeContentBlock[] + stopReason + usage from LlmResponse; exported from packages/voice-agent/src/index.ts; 7/7 unit tests. ra-ml-autocapture (parallel batch 5): predictions auto-captured from the live model-dispatch path. apps/api/src/ml-platform-routes.ts — on a successful POST /admin/ml/models/:name/:version/$predict, a best-effort (try/catch, structural-only warn, never fails the prediction) capture writes a drift sample via PostgresMlPredictionCaptureStore (migration 142). New captureDb option threaded through buildMlPlatformRuntime (now returns captureDb) + MlPlatformRoutesOptions; index.ts passes captureDb: db. Mapping: score←output.score/probability/bare-numeric (skipped if not finite in 0..1 → never violates the CHECK), subgroup←body.subgroup||'default', observed←body.observed, features←body.captureFeatures||plain-object body.input. Tested vs real Postgres (:5434): NEW apps/api/src/ml-platform-autocapture.integration.test.ts — 3/3 (capture scoped to caller tenant, per-tenant isolation, captureDb-absent → predict still works + captures nothing); existing ml-platform-routes.test.ts + .integration.test.ts still 21/21. ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, build=0, 0 NEW lint (agent fixed its own 2 no-unnecessary-type-assertion; 1 pre-existing require-await carry-forward verified vs HEAD). This completes the drift-monitoring capture→window→computeReport loop end-to-end.

ra-ml-driftwire (parallel batch 4): ml-drift capture + windowed-drift read routes. apps/api/src/ml-drift-routes.ts gained opts{db?: Kysely<MlPredictionCaptureDatabase>} (registered { db } in index.ts): the existing computational $compute/$thresholds are unchanged + work without db; with db, NEW POST /admin/ml/drift/:model/:version/$capture (PostgresMlPredictionCaptureStore.capture → 201) + GET /admin/ml/drift/:model/:version/windowed?currentSize=&referenceSize= (or ?cutoff=ISO) → windowedDatasets → computeReport → DriftReport (422 insufficient-data when a window is empty), tenant-scoped (X-Cernia-Tenant), 400 on bad params. Tested vs real Postgres (:5434): NEW apps/api/src/ml-drift-capture-routes.integration.test.ts — 5/5 (capture 201, windowed severe-shift DriftReport from stored windows, tenant isolation 422, malformed 400, missing-params 400); existing in-process ml-drift-routes.test.ts still 6/6. ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, build=0, new file lint-clean. REMAINING (narrowed in bullet): auto-capture from the live model-dispatch path.

ra-ml-voicegw (parallel batch 3): LlmGateway-backed IClaudeClient adapter for the voice agent. NEW apps/api/src/voice-call-llm-gateway-client.ts — createLlmGatewayClaudeClient({gateway, tenantId, defaultModel?, purposeOfUse?, userId?, mapModel?}) implements the @cernia/voice-agent IClaudeClient.complete contract over @cernia/o1-llm-gateway LlmGateway.complete: system→leading {role:'system'} message; structured content blocks flattened to text (tool_result inlined); model string→ModelId via a default mapper; gateway content→{type:'text'} block + toolCalls→{type:'tool_use'} blocks; finishReason→stopReason; usage.promptTokens/completionTokens→inputTokens/outputTokens. Routes voice dialog turns through the gateway for PHI redaction + cost recording + audit. 8/8 unit tests against a stub gateway (forwards prompt/system/model in the gateway shape; adapts the response back; passes tenantId). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, build=0, new file lint-clean. REMAINING (narrowed in bullet): construct the adapter in voice-call-handler.ts tenant scope (needs per-call tenantId) + persist gateway CostStore/AuditLog to Postgres.

ra-ml-driftcap (parallel batch 3): prediction-stream capture store feeding the ML drift endpoint. NEW migration 142_ml_prediction_samples.sql — per-tenant (PK (tenant_id,id)), model_name/model_version/captured_at/score(double, CHECK 0..1)/observed(smallint, CHECK NULL|0|1)/subgroup/features jsonb (de-identified model inputs, NO PHI), index (tenant_id, model_name, model_version, captured_at DESC). NEW apps/api/src/ml-prediction-capture-store.ts — PostgresMlPredictionCaptureStore.capture(model,version,{score,subgroup,observed?,features?}) + referenceWindow/currentWindow/windowedDatasets returning @cernia/p3b-drift-monitoring PredictionRecord[] (the exact shape /admin/ml/drift/$compute consumes) via recent-N or cutoff strategies.
