ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, build=0, new files lint-clean. REMAINING (narrowed in bullet): call capture() from live model dispatch + a read route feeding $compute.

LLM gateway WIRED AT BOOT + live-verified — closes a real gap: the ra-ml-3 routes (llm-gateway-routes.ts) were built + unit-tested but llmGatewayRoutes was NEVER registered in apps/api/src/index.ts, so POST /admin/llm/$complete didn't exist at runtime (setting a provider key did nothing). index.ts now registers llmGatewayRoutes with gateway: buildLlmGatewayFromEnv() at section 10f-llm (right after llmObservabilityRoutes), 503-safe when no key. Any provider activates purely from .env — ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY / AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY / GOOGLE_API_KEY / AWS_BEDROCK_REGION — no code change per provider. Live-verified against the operator's real OpenAI key: new apps/api/src/llm-gateway.live.test.ts (gated on any provider key; describe.skipIf) ran gateway.complete({model:'gpt-4o-mini'}) → 'pong' (2/2 green). ROUTING NOTE surfaced + documented: the boot default is hipaa-strict, which routes ONLY to a HIPAA-eligible provider (Bedrock) and REFUSES plain OpenAI/Anthropic/Google (MODEL_META.hipaaEligible=true only for claude-bedrock) — so non-BAA test use requires CERNIA_LLM_ROUTING=primary-fallback in .env (the live test sets it). PHI is redacted (CERNIA_LLM_REDACTION, default strict) before any provider call. Test-mode: operator will add BAA + production hardening before routing patient data to a non-BAA provider. ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, build=0, @cernia/o1-llm-gateway build=0; live 2/2; index.ts additions (import + 10f-llm block) add zero lint errors (53 pre-existing baseline errors unrelated, verified by line mapping).

Per-tenant model registry refactor CLOSED — cross-tenant isolation fix. apps/api/src/ml-platform-routes.ts adds PerTenantModelRegistry: a lazy, FIFO-bounded cache that builds one TrackedModelRegistry per tenant id, each wrapping a tenant-scoped store (new PostgresModelRegistryStore({db,tenantId}) in production; per-tenant InMemoryModelRegistryStore otherwise) and seeded idempotently. buildMlPlatformRuntime now takes {db?, buildStore?, runner?} and returns {registry: PerTenantModelRegistry}; warms the default tenant at boot. All 6 routes resolve the caller's tenant via resolveTenantId(X-Cernia-Tenant) before touching the registry, so one tenant can no longer see or collide with another tenant's registered models (was a single process-wide in-memory registry shared across ALL tenants — a cross-tenant data-isolation defect per CLAUDE.md invariant #1). apps/api/src/index.ts boot passes db (per-tenant Postgres). Mirrors the per-tenant pattern of PerTenantTtsRouter/SttRouter/TwilioVoiceRouter. Tested vs real Postgres (:5434): apps/api/src/ml-platform-routes.integration.test.ts bootstraps the real 019_ml_model_registry.sql migration — tenant A's model is invisible to tenant B in discovery AND 404s on B's direct version/resolve lookup, and a direct DB assertion confirms the row is scoped to tenant A's tenant_id. 3 new integration tests + existing 18 in-memory tests all green (21/21). ADR-0127: apps/api typecheck=0, build=0, new files lint=0.

Investigated Postgres-backed model registry swap. PostgresModelRegistryStore is tenant-scoped (takes tenantId at construction); buildMlPlatformRuntime returns a single process-wide registry. Wiring the Postgres store therefore requires a per-tenant registry refactor (build-on-demand from request principal, or a TenantId-keyed map of registries). Made the swap path easier: buildMlPlatformRuntime now accepts opts.store override + seed registration is idempotent (swallows "already exists" so re-booting against a populated DB does not error). Bullet narrowed to reflect the real blocker.

ra-ml-5 closed — apps/api/src/ml-pipeline-routes.ts mounts the p1a + p1b training-loop endpoints. p1a: POST /api/ml/datasets/$validate / $compile / $quality (stateless wrappers around validateSpec / compileDataset / computeQuality). p1b: POST /api/ml/runs/$queue + per-run POST /api/ml/runs/:id/$start / $complete / $fail / $kill / $record-cost (stateless wrappers around the orchestrator state-machine functions). Stateless wrappers — persistent run store + budget accounting is the next follow-up. apps/api/package.json gains @cernia/p1a-dataset-dsl + @cernia/p1b-automl-orchestrator workspace deps; apps/api/src/index.ts registers the routes under section 10f-ml. apps/api typecheck clean.

ra-ml-6 closed — admin UI for browsing models + promoting versions. apps/api/src/ml-platform-routes.ts adds POST /admin/ml/models/:name/:version/$promote + $demote (wraps IModelRegistryStore.setPromoted; 404 on unknown). 4 new tests pass. apps/admin inference-api.ts gains promoteModelVersion + demoteModelVersion. /admin/inference/models/page.tsx ModelDetail gains a Promote/Demote button with inline SaMD class III/IV approval reminder. On success: row + selected state update optimistically; full list reflects the new promoted=true/false.

ra-ml-1 closed (adapter level) — HTTP inference-runner bridge shipped. apps/api/src/http-inference-runner.ts implements the inference-platform Runner contract via HTTP POST to a sidecar (Python risk-classifier in production; any HTTP-backed model server in general). POSTs {modelName, modelVersion, input} to <baseUrl>/predict with optional Bearer token + configurable timeout; canServe gates by model.accelerator='cpu' AND optional allowedModelNames allowlist; HttpInferenceRunnerError carries HTTP status + body; per-call timeoutSeconds override; PHI-safe (no input/output body logging — only HTTP status + latency upstream). buildHttpInferenceRunnerFromEnv() returns undefined when CERNIA_RISK_CLASSIFIER_URL is unset (CpuRunner stub fallback) or constructs from {URL, BEARER, TIMEOUT_MS} env vars. 12 unit tests pass: POST shape + URL composition / tokens propagation / non-2xx → HttpInferenceRunnerError / missing-output rejected / Bearer header / canServe gating / empty-baseUrl rejection / trailing-slash normalization / env-builder unset+set paths. apps/api typecheck clean. Followup (the production wiring): instantiate RequestRouter inside buildMlPlatformRuntime with a RunnerFactory that picks HttpInferenceRunner when env URL is set, and mount POST /admin/ml/models/:urn/$predict that calls router.submit.

ra-ml-4 closed — drift + fairness monitoring wired. apps/api/src/ml-drift-routes.ts mounts POST /admin/ml/drift/$compute (validates PredictionRecord[] shape; 400 on missing-fields or empty arrays; returns DriftReport from @cernia/p3b-drift-monitoring.computeReport) + GET /admin/ml/drift/$thresholds (canonical PSI minor/major/severe + fairness alert/auto-pause). 6 in-process tests pass: no-drift baseline + severe distribution shift + 3×400 validation + GET $thresholds shape. apps/admin /admin/inference/drift/page.tsx swapped from empty-state to a real interactive panel: 2 textarea inputs (reference + current PredictionRecord JSON, pre-filled with 20-record samples showing a 0.15-upward synthetic shift), Compute + Reset buttons, 4-tile result card (PSI / fairness gap / Brier score / sample sizes), severity badge + ALERT/AUTO-PAUSE chips from the DriftReport triggers, thresholds card sourced from the GET endpoint. apps/api typecheck clean; apps/admin typecheck clean. Real prediction-stream capture (live serving → rolling-window reference + current) is the next followup.

Shipped ra-ml-3 LLM gateway routes: apps/api/src/llm-gateway-routes.ts mounts POST /admin/llm/$complete (proxies through LlmGateway.complete — validation + budget pre-check + PHI redaction + provider routing + safety + cost record + audit), POST /admin/llm/budget (admin sets per-tenant monthlyLimitUSD + alertThresholdUSD), GET /admin/llm/$report (returns CostReport scoped to header tenantId — no cross-tenant query path). Tenant context comes from X-Cernia-Tenant header with CERNIA_DEFAULT_TENANT_ID fallback; body tenantId must match header (403 tenant-mismatch otherwise) per ADR 0004 row-level isolation. buildLlmGatewayFromEnv() reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + OPENAI_API_KEY + AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY + GOOGLE_API_KEY + AWS_BEDROCK_REGION; default routing is hipaa-strict + redaction=strict so misconfigured prod fails closed. Returns undefined when no provider keys set, so the routes register and 503 'llm-gateway-not-configured' (mirrors sso-routes/payment-dispatcher pattern). 14 unit tests pass (not-configured fallback × 3, happy path × 4, tenant mismatch, budget validation, report scoping + invalid range, env factory × 4). apps/api/package.json gained @cernia/o1-llm-gateway workspace dep. Followup narrowed in remaining[] above: voice-call-handler IClaudeClient swap + CostStore/AuditLog → Postgres persistence.

Closed ra-ml-1-followup. apps/api/src/ml-platform-routes.ts now mounts POST /admin/ml/models/:name/:version/$predict that resolves the registered model from the registry, runs runner.canServe(model), then dispatches via Runner.invoke(). buildMlPlatformRuntime() auto-detects an HttpInferenceRunner from buildHttpInferenceRunnerFromEnv() (CERNIA_RISK_CLASSIFIER_URL + friends) when no runner is passed explicitly. apps/api/src/index.ts:810 boot path threads the runner through. Returns 503 inference-runner-not-configured when no runner is wired (same fallback pattern as sso-routes + payment-dispatcher). HttpInferenceRunnerError 4xx maps to 400; 5xx maps to 502; unknown URN 404; canServe=false 400. Tenant via X-Cernia-Tenant header with env fallback. 6 new unit tests cover all paths (apps/api/src/ml-platform-routes.test.ts now has 18 tests total).

1A-2 review fixes: (1) phi-redaction.ts redactMessages() now redacts tool_result.content (HIPAA PHI leak fix — free-form tool results can carry SSN/MRN/etc.); tool_use.input remains exempt (structured JSON). (2) ClaudeRequest gains optional tenantId field; gateway-claude-client.ts complete() uses req.tenantId ?? opts.tenantId (per-call wins, opts is fallback); dialog.ts threads this.start.tenantId per turn. (3) apps/api boot log claudeId now reports "llm-gateway" when deps.llmGateway is set. (4) production-readiness.json wording corrected to reflect per-call tenant flow.

stages.name: Telehealth & remote patient monitoring
stages.rationale: RPM (remote patient monitoring) is the Q-wave's reimbursable feature: device ingestion + care-plan generation + adherence tracking. Telehealth visits via WebRTC are the synchronous-care complement. Both built, neither runs.

baseline.summary: q-s1-webrtc-signaling, q-s2-rpm-device-ingestion, q-s3-telehealth-visit-audit, q-s4-rpm-careplan-generator, q-s5-visit-summary-generator, q-s6-rpm-adherence-tracker all shipped. No WebRTC signaling endpoint in apps/api, no device webhook routes, no care-plan generation cron, no visit-summary UI.

current.summary: RPM compute endpoints live in apps/api: POST /rpm/readings/$evaluate, GET /rpm/care-plan-templates, POST /rpm/care-plans/$generate, POST /rpm/adherence/$compute. WebRTC signaling websocket (q-s1) registered at apps/api/src/index.ts:911. Stateful device webhook at apps/api/src/rpm-device-webhook-routes.ts feeds AlertEngine + persists alerts. Nightly batches sweep tenants for care-plan + adherence sweeps with real per-tenant fan-out. Telehealth visit endpoints (q-s3 quality + q-s5 summary) wired via apps/api/src/telehealth-visit-routes.ts. Clinic UI complete: apps/admin/src/app/admin/rpm/page.tsx (dashboard with per-patient activity + open-alert severity sort) + apps/admin/src/app/admin/rpm/[patientId]/page.tsx (care-plan editor + readings table + alerts ack) + apps/admin/src/app/admin/telehealth/visit-summary/page.tsx (q-s5 summary UI) + apps/admin/src/app/admin/telehealth/visit-quality/page.tsx (q-s3 quality dashboard).

history.note: ra-rpm-3 closed — apps/api/src/rpm-nightly-batches.ts upgraded from heartbeat-only to REAL population sweep. Exposes runCarePlanSweep + runAdherenceSweep helpers; bootRpmNightlyBatches accepts opts.db and runs them every tick. Per-tenant walk: SELECT DISTINCT rpm_care_plans.tenant_id → per-tenant PostgresCarePlanStore.list() → for each plan, PostgresRpmReadingsStore.query over the past 7 days → checkIns built from readings → computeAdherence + recommendIntervention from q-s6-rpm-adherence → buildInterventionQueue. Aggregate per-level counts logged. 5 real-Postgres integration tests prove it (apps/api/src/rpm-nightly-batches.test.ts): tenant enumeration; score-against-real-readings; tenant isolation (tenant-A patient never appears in tenant-B sweep); empty-readings patient → home-visit intervention (streak gate at 7 days); empty-goals plan skipped. apps/api/src/index.ts now passes db to the scheduler. Persisting InterventionRecommendation to a new rpm_interventions table is the next followup so the care-coordinator worklist can drain them.

Removed 2 stale remaining bullets. (1) WebRTC signaling websocket - shipped: webRtcSignalingRoutes registered at apps/api/src/index.ts:911 (q-s1, task #32). (2) Stateful POST /rpm/devices/:deviceId/readings - shipped: apps/api/src/rpm-device-webhook-routes.ts:199 mounts the route; lines 11/143-146 confirm it feeds AlertEngine and persists alerts (task #179 ra-rpm-1). Remaining 2 bullets stay open: q-s3/q-s5 (telehealth-visit-audit + visit-summary) are not yet routed in apps/api; clinic UI exists only for RPM dashboard (apps/admin/src/app/admin/rpm, task #212 ra-rpm-5) but not visit-summary or care-plan management.

Closed q-s3 + q-s5 wire-up bullet. apps/api/src/telehealth-visit-routes.ts mounts 6 pure-compute endpoints wrapping @cernia/q-s3-telehealth-audit (computeQuality, recordEvent, markNoShow, markCancelled, toFhirEncounter) and @cernia/q-s5-visit-summary (generateSummary): POST /admin/telehealth/visits/$compute-quality, $record-event, $mark-no-show, $mark-cancelled, $to-fhir-encounter, $generate-summary. apps/api/package.json gained both deps; apps/api/src/index.ts registers telehealthVisitRoutes alongside webRtcSignalingRoutes. 8 unit tests cover happy paths + invalid-input rejections. apps/api typecheck clean. Per ADR 0004 tenant context via X-Cernia-Tenant header with env fallback; per CLAUDE.md no PHI logged.

Shipped first slice of the clinic UI bullet. apps/admin/src/app/admin/telehealth/visit-summary/page.tsx renders the q-s5 summary by calling the apps/api -summary route from iteration 29. RPM dashboard + care-plan management UI remain - the clinic UI bullet stays open but is now partially shipped.

Iter 83 — STAGE COMPLETE. Final clinic-UI bullet retired as previously-shipped: (1) RPM dashboard apps/admin/src/app/admin/rpm/page.tsx renders per-patient activity sorted by worst open-alert severity, calling GET /api/rpm/patients on PostgresRpmAlertsStore + PostgresRpmReadingsStore (task #212 ra-rpm-5). (2) Care-plan management apps/admin/src/app/admin/rpm/[patientId]/page.tsx — care-plan editor with per-vital target ranges + save-inline, alongside readings table + alerts ack (closed by Wave Q5 PostgresCarePlanStore in task #214). (3) Visit-summary apps/admin/src/app/admin/telehealth/visit-summary/page.tsx wires q-s5 (iter-29 slice). (4) Visit-quality dashboard apps/admin/src/app/admin/telehealth/visit-quality/page.tsx + visit-quality-api.ts. Bullet was stale — work landed across multiple prior iters but the remaining[] entry wasn't narrowed. Stage flips partial → complete.

stages.name: Caregiver delegation & identity federation
stages.rationale: Allows a patient's family/caregiver to act on their behalf (proxy), JIT-grant temporary access to a covering provider, SAML/OIDC federation for hospital-network SSO, and break-glass emergency access. Compliance-load-bearing for any enterprise pilot.

baseline.summary: g-d1-caregiver-proxy, g-d2-delegation, g-d3-jit-access, g-d4-access-review-attestation, g-d5-saml-oidc-federation, g-d6-break-glass all shipped. Existing auth flow doesn't consult any of them; no SAML/OIDC providers configured in apps/api boot path.

current.summary: Caregiver-federation backend complete, and BOTH SSO protocols (OIDC + SAML 2.0) are now wired end-to-end at apps/api boot with full session mint. OIDC: TenantSsoFactory (index.ts step 10i.1) with KmsBackedJwtIssuer + FHIR-User provisioner/lookup + Redis/InMemory state store + FetchOidcHttpClient + tenant-secret ClientSecretResolver. SAML 2.0: TenantSamlProviderFactory (index.ts step 10i.2) builds the hardened in-repo SamlIdentityProvider per tenant — real XML-DSig with XSW guards + RSA/ECDSA-SHA2 algorithm allowlist + replay cache + InResponseTo binding, JIT provisioning, role resolution, and session mint via the SAME jwtIssuer.sign path OIDC uses (byte-identical sessions). Routes: GET /metadata, GET /login (SP-initiated), POST /acs (full session). The earlier node-saml validator detour was removed in favour of this single canonical path. SSO routes still 503 for tenants with no enabled IdP connection, so wiring only widens what works. No SAML handshake work remains deferred (multi-pod InResponseTo store shares the OidcStateStore horizontal-scale follow-up; IdP-initiated SSO is a per-connection opt-in).

history.note: SAML 2.0 SSO E2E CLOSED against the REAL stack with an in-repo STUB IdP (no external IdP, no procurement) — completes cit-pass-3 (Wave G) to ALL-GATES-GREEN. NEW tests/e2e/specs/saml-sso-flow.spec.ts (3, @wave-G) — Playwright 3 passed (30.7s) driving the FULL SP-initiated handshake through the running apps/api: tenant-admin persists an enabled SAML connection via POST /admin/idp-connections (carrying the stub's openssl self-signed x509 cert as the trust anchor; the g-d5 validateConnection BEGIN-CERTIFICATE check — bypassed by the in-process test's stub store — is satisfied with a real cert); GET /api/auth/saml/:t/login (UNAUTHENTICATED) → 302 + deflated AuthnRequest (id recovered for InResponseTo); the stub mints a real XML-DSig SAMLResponse via @cernia/auth-providers _testBuildSignedSamlResponse (dynamic-imported from the built dist by path, so tests/e2e gains no new workspace dep) bound by InResponseTo; POST /acs → the production SamlIdentityProvider verifies signature+XSW+replay+Conditions+InResponseTo → JIT-provisions a FHIR User → mints a REAL Cernia session JWT (decoded claims assert idp_type=saml, tenant_id bound, sub=User/…). Negative paths: tampered assertion → 401 saml-verification-failed; unsolicited (no InResponseTo) → 401. THREE genuine bugs surfaced by driving the full server (the in-process saml-routes.test.ts registers only the saml plugin, no global RBAC hook, so it missed all three): (1) CRITICAL — /api/auth/saml/* + /api/auth/sso/* were NOT in the unauth allowlist (permitAllForUnauthenticatedRoutes), so the global RBAC hook 401-gated SP-initiated SSO login: SSO was unreachable for unauthenticated users. Added both prefixes (each route still self-gates: 503 no-factory / 404 no-connection / real verification before any session mint). (2) /admin/idp-connections* had no deriveOperation mapping → */* 403; mapped to IdpConnection. (3) the /caregiver/* + /access/* mapping from the earlier 2026-06-02 entry. openssl is used at runtime for the stub cert (no committed secrets); the spec SKIPS (not fails) if openssl is unavailable, for portability. ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, iam-route-guards.ts lint=0; saml-routes.test.ts (8) + iam-route-guards.test.ts (17) re-run green (additive allowlist + deriveOperation branches, no regression).

E2E gate CLOSED for the caregiver-federation flows against the REAL stack (closes the cit-pass-3 named open item). NEW tests/e2e/specs/caregiver-federation-flow.spec.ts (5, @wave-G) — Playwright 5 passed (21.6s) driving the REAL apps/api /access/* + /caregiver/* routes over HTTP (PostgresJitAccessStore/PostgresBreakGlassStore/PostgresProxyStore, migrations 049/050) on real Postgres (:5433), real JWT from POST /dev/login: (1) REAL-RBAC gate (401 unauth / 200 tenant-admin); (2) JIT dual-control — medium-risk $request→pending-approval (not auto-approved)→appears in /access/jit/pending→separate-approver $approve→approved→re-$approve 409; (3) JIT $deny→denied; (4) break-glass dual-control — $invoke→pending-review (immediate access + mandatory after-the-fact review)→appears in /pending-review→reviewer-of-record $review review-approved→leaves queue→re-$review 409; (5) proxy $grant active→by-patient list→$revoke revoked. FIRST end-to-end coverage of the PERSISTED approve/review state machines (the unit tests use InMemoryProxyStore with no jit/break-glass store → those paths returned 503, never exercised e2e). ROOT-CAUSE FIX: apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation now maps /caregiver/proxy/* + /access/{jit,break-glass,delegation,attestation}/* (no route-level requirePermission) onto synthetic resource types (CaregiverProxyGrant/JitAccessRequest/BreakGlassInvocation/AccessDelegation/AccessReviewAttestation; write/search/read) — were */* catch-all → 403 for every role. ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, iam-route-guards.ts lint=0. The stage was already backend-complete (incl. the real signed-SAMLResponse handshake test in saml-routes.test.ts); this adds the real-stack E2E proof of the federation flows. A live-external-IdP browser SAML SSO E2E is the only conceivable remaining slice (needs an IdP to redirect to) — surfaced to the user as a green-vs-carve-out decision.

SAML 2.0 handshake CLOSED end-to-end — pivoted from the node-saml verifier detour to the hardened in-repo SamlIdentityProvider (@cernia/auth-providers), which the codebase already shipped as the canonical SAML SP. Discovery: SamlIdentityProvider.handleCallback already does the WHOLE handshake (real XML-DSig with XSW guards + RSA/ECDSA-SHA2 algorithm allowlist + replay cache + InResponseTo binding → JIT-provision → resolveRoles → jwtIssuer.sign → AuthenticateResult) — identical to the OIDC path. New apps/api/src/saml-provider-factory.ts (TenantSamlProviderFactory) maps the g-d5 SamlConnection → SamlProviderConfig (inverting attributeMapping) and constructs/caches one provider per (tenantId, connectionId) built with the boot jwtIssuer + a SAML FHIR-User store. saml-routes.ts rewritten: GET /metadata (provider.publishMetadata), GET /login (SP-initiated AuthnRequest, records the request id), POST /acs (provider.handleCallback → real Cernia session {token,sessionId,expiresAt,user}, 401/403 mapping). DELETED the redundant node-saml saml-validator.ts + saml-validator.test.ts and dropped @node-saml/node-saml + xml-crypto from apps/api. Bugfix: SamlIdentityProvider records JIT users with authProvider 'oauth2', so FhirUserProvisioner gained an externalAuthProvider option (default 'oidc') and the SAML user store is built with 'oauth2' — otherwise findByExternal (oidc-keyed) would re-provision a duplicate every login. Tests: saml-routes.test.ts rewritten (7) — drives the full SP-initiated flow (login→inflate AuthnRequest id→signed Response via the package's byte-exact _testBuildSignedSamlResponse against a runtime RSA keypair, no committed secrets→ACS), asserts JIT-provision + session mint + idpType='saml' + least-privilege defaultRoles fallback, plus 503/404/tamper-401/unsolicited-401/missing-400; sso-user-store.test.ts +1 (oauth2 re-login finds the user, oidc store does not). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, lint=0, build=0; @cernia/auth-providers rebuilt to expose the test helper. This CLOSES the long-deferred SAML last mile — SAML and OIDC now mint byte-identical sessions through one issuer.

[superseded by the pivot above] SAML 2.0 SP routes wired into apps/api (ACS verification slice). apps/api/src/saml-routes.ts: GET /api/auth/saml/:tenantId/metadata (SP metadata) + POST /api/auth/saml/:tenantId/acs (Assertion Consumer Service). The ACS handler reads the form-POSTed SAMLResponse (a urlencoded content-type parser is registered in the plugin scope), resolves the tenant's enabled SAML connection from the same PostgresIdpConnectionStore the OIDC factory uses, and runs it through buildTenantSamlValidator (real node-saml XML-DSig). 503 when the store isn't wired / 404 when no enabled SAML connection — safe-by-default, mirroring ssoRoutes. Registered at index.ts step 10i.2 (SP entity id from CERNIA_PUBLIC_BASE_URL, override CERNIA_SAML_SP_ENTITY_ID). 6 route tests (apps/api/src/saml-routes.test.ts) via app.inject: 503 (no store), 404 (no connection), SP-metadata XML, ACS accepts a runtime-signed Response and returns verified claims, ACS 401 on a tampered Response, ACS 400 on missing SAMLResponse — the happy path signs a real fixture with xml-crypto against a runtime openssl keypair (no committed secrets). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, new-file lint=0, build=0, 6/6 tests. The ACS endpoint returns {outcome:'verified', sessionPending:true}; the verified-claims → JIT-provision → session-mint bridge is the remaining last-mile (see deferred) — left for a dedicated slice so the SAML session is minted via the EXACT OIDC JwtIssuer path (no divergent hand-rolled session).

SAML 2.0 real XML-DSig verification SHIPPED — closes the cryptographic core of the long-deferred handshake (library = @node-saml/node-saml, operator-chosen). apps/api/src/saml-validator.ts: buildTenantSamlValidator(connection, opts) wraps node-saml SAML.validatePostResponseAsync for authoritative validation — XML signature (via xml-crypto), NotBefore/NotOnOrAfter, AudienceRestriction, StatusCode — plus a DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH explicit Issuer pin (node-saml's signature trust is certificate-based, so it accepts a validly-signed response regardless of the Issuer string; we additionally require profile.issuer === connection.issuer). Fail-closed: validatePostResponse throws on any failure → {ok:false}; claims never surface from an unverified response (ADR 0026 §3). wantAssertionsSigned=true. Maps node-saml Profile → typed SamlValidatedClaims (nameId, email, flattened attributes); SP metadata via generateServiceProviderMetadata. Tests (apps/api/src/saml-validator.test.ts) are REAL not mocked: generate a throwaway RSA keypair at RUNTIME (openssl; no committed secrets — repo gitignores *.pem) and sign a SAML Response with xml-crypto, then assert the validator ACCEPTS the valid signature + extracts claims and REJECTS tampered-payload / expired-Conditions / wrong-Audience / wrong-Issuer / unsigned (7/7). Deliberately NO stub verifier — that is exactly the bug class just fixed in the Stripe webhook path. Deps @node-saml/node-saml@^5.1.0 + xml-crypto@^6.1.2 added to apps/api. ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, new-file lint=0, build=0, 7/7 tests. Remaining (now narrowed in `deferred`): the Fastify ACS route + boot wiring + JIT claim mapping.

Shipped PostgresProxyStore (apps/api/src/caregiver-proxy-store.ts) + migration 049_caregiver_proxy_relationships. Implements ProxyStore from @cernia/g-d1-caregiver-proxy with tenant-scoped CRUD: grant, revoke (legal-kind admin-only enforcement preserved), findById, listForCaregiver, listForPatient, sweepExpired (CTE-based bulk flip of active rows whose expires_at is past). 12 pg-mem unit tests cover happy path, kind-specific default expiry, legal-kind admin requirement, double-revoke protection, tenant isolation across all reads, sweepExpired CTE correctness. JIT request store + break-glass invocation log remain on InMemory (separate stores, same refactor pattern).

Caught + closed wiring gap: caregiverFederationRoutes was constructing a fresh InMemoryProxyStore (the PostgresProxyStore shipped earlier was sitting unused). Refactored buildCaregiverFederationRuntime to accept opts.proxyStore + apps/api boot now passes new PostgresProxyStore({ db, tenantId: CERNIA_DEFAULT_TENANT_ID ?? "platform" }). Routes are now backed by persistent storage.

Shipped JIT approve/deny + break-glass review endpoints with persistent stores. Migration 050 (jit_access_requests + break_glass_invocations). PostgresJitAccessStore + PostgresBreakGlassStore in apps/api with full tenant scoping. Routes updated: $request + $invoke now persist on ok results; new POST /access/jit/:id/$approve, /$deny, /access/break-glass/:id/$review; new GET /access/jit/pending + /access/break-glass/pending-review for the future admin UI. 7 pg-mem unit tests cover round-trip persistence across the engine state machines (request→approve, request→deny, invoke→review, listing filters, tenant isolation).

Shipped g-d2 user-to-user delegation end-to-end. Migration 051_access_delegations + PostgresAccessDelegationStore + routes mounted in caregiver-federation-routes: POST /access/delegation/$create + /:id/$accept + /:id/$reject + /:id/$revoke, GET /by-delegator/:id + /by-delegatee/:id. Engine state-machine guard rails preserved (only valid transitions persist; 409 invalid-state otherwise). 503 not-configured fallback when store unset. Wired in apps/api boot.

Shipped g-d4 access-review attestation end-to-end. Migration 052 (review_campaigns + attestation_items, two tables). PostgresAttestationStore handles both. 7 new routes: POST /access/attestation/campaigns/$create + /:id/items/$add + /:id/$close, POST /access/attestation/items/:id/$decide, GET /campaigns/:id/summary + /campaigns/:id/items, GET /items/by-reviewer/:id. All engine state-machine guard rails preserved (only-open campaigns accept new items; only-pending items accept decisions; closeCampaign emits itemsToRevoke for the unreviewed sweep).

Shipped admin UI for delegations + attestations. apps/admin/src/lib/caregiver-federation-api.ts (typed client) + 2 new pages: /admin/access/delegations (list by delegator OR delegatee, create form, accept/reject/revoke actions, state-machine-aware buttons) + /admin/access/attestations (campaign create form, per-reviewer pending queue, attest/revoke decisions with optional note). Sidebar gets a new "Access" nav group with both entries. The 17 caregiver-federation endpoints shipped earlier are now reachable from the tenant admin UI.

Shipped g-d5 IdP connection config layer: migration 053_idp_connections (per-tenant SAML 2.0 + OIDC configs, protocol-discriminated columns, JSONB attribute/group mappings), PostgresIdpConnectionStore (multi-tenant; tenantId per call). Tenant-admin CRUD routes mounted: GET/POST /admin/idp-connections, GET/PATCH/DELETE /admin/idp-connections/:id. Validation runs g-d5 engine.validateConnection() on insert; 422 with structured ConfigIssue list on failure. Admin role required for write; all reads tenant-scoped. 11 pg-mem tests cover OIDC + SAML round-trip, validation rejections, tenant isolation across every read/write, idempotent delete. The SAML/OIDC HANDSHAKE itself (AuthnRequest signing, SAMLResponse verification, ACS callback, session bridge) is the remaining piece — needs samlify/passport-saml in @cernia/auth-providers + login route changes.

Shipped TenantSsoFactory (apps/api/src/sso-tenant-factory.ts) — closes the g-d5 → auth-providers translation layer. Per-tenant OidcIdentityProvider construction on demand: reads active OIDC connection from PostgresIdpConnectionStore, resolves clientSecretRef via injected ClientSecretResolver (TenantSecretResolver in prod), maps g-d5 AttributeMapping → auth-providers claimMapping, strips .well-known suffix from discoveryUrl to derive issuer. Built-in cache keyed by (tenantId, connectionId); invalidate(tenantId) drops on admin toggle. 10 pg-mem tests cover happy path, no-enabled error, multi-enabled disambiguation, SAML-rejection, cache hit, cache invalidation, client-secret resolution, issuer derivation, attribute-mapping translation. Remaining for full handshake closure: Fastify routes wiring start + callback into Cernia URL space (needs JwtIssuer + UserProvisioner + persistent OidcStateStore wired in apps/api boot); SAML protocol implementation (needs samlify/passport-saml).

Shipped final piece of g-d5 OIDC chain: ssoRoutes (apps/api/src/sso-routes.ts) mounting GET /api/auth/sso/:tenantId/start + /callback. Routes default to 503 sso-not-configured until apps/api boot wires JwtIssuer + UserProvisioner + UserLookup + OidcStateStore + OidcHttpClient + ClientSecretResolver (clean stub in index.ts with explicit TODO inventory). 6 route tests cover the 503 path + a working happy path via a stub factory (302 redirect on /start, 200 authenticated payload with trusted user fields on /callback, 400 on idp-rejected / invalid-callback). Total caregiver-federation tests now 46 passing across 5 suites.

Wired the OIDC SSO route deps at apps/api boot — closed the last actionable caregiver-federation deferral (the SSO-boot-wiring bullet). New apps/api/src/sso-user-store.ts ships FhirUserProvisioner + FhirUserLookup over the same tenant-scoped StorageAdapter the FHIR server uses: create() JIT-provisions a FHIR User (authProvider=oidc, status=active), findByExternal() dedups by externalIdentifier (paged auth-provider search + in-memory filter — User has no external-identifier search param yet; dedicated param is a future optimization), resolveRoles() reads active RoleBindings→Role names mirroring iam-route-guards' resolver, loadMfaCredentials() returns null (IdP owns MFA). Safe-by-default: JIT users get NO RoleBindings (least privilege); a shared bounded userId→tenantId index recovers tenant for the tenant-less resolveRoles call (always preceded by find/create in the OIDC flow). index.ts step 10i.1 now builds TenantSsoFactory with KmsBackedJwtIssuer + RedisOidcStateStore (REDIS_HOST-gated, else InMemory) + FetchOidcHttpClient + ClientSecretResolver over tenantSecrets.resolver (connection clientSecretRef doubles as the secret provider key) + the new provisioner/lookup; SSO registration moved after the tenant-secret runtime so the resolver is available. Routes still 503 for tenants with no enabled IdP connection — wiring only widens what works. 13 new adapter unit tests; existing 6 sso-route tests still green. @cernia/api typecheck + build clean. Remaining: SAML 2.0 handshake only.

stages.name: SaaS billing & marketplace
stages.rationale: Enterprise invoicing, ASC 606 revenue recognition, SaaS metrics (MRR/ARR/churn), contract auto-renewal, discount-approval workflow, marketplace checkout, tax compliance. The commercial back-end for selling Cernia at scale.

baseline.summary: marketplace, x1a-tax-compliance, x1b-asc-606-revenue-recognition, x1c-saas-metrics-computation, x2a-enterprise-invoicing, x2b-contract-auto-renewal, x3c-discount-approval-workflow, j1c-sandbox-tenant-lifecycle, j1d-marketplace-checkout all shipped. No marketplace UI in apps/*. No invoice generation job. No tax engine wired into payment paths.

current.summary: Comprehensive Stripe COMPLETE (S1-S8): native subscriptions w/ proration+trial conversion, embedded Payment Element, Billing Meters metered usage, dunning + Smart Retries, single reconciling webhook endpoint, refunds + admin surfacing, gated full-lifecycle test-mode harness, go-live runbook. Dev runs Stripe TEST mode; only the sk_live_ key flip + signed BAA remain (operator/legal, no code).

remaining: Deferred (post-GA, NOT a launch blocker): ML-model marketplace (P3-C) clinical-review dual-control queue before publish — the -A vertical publishes directly; a physician/compliance review gate is a future enhancement (apps/api/src/ml-marketplace-routes.ts).

Release 3 / T5 — publisher-verification VerifierBackends are a clearly-labelled DEV STUB (apps/api/src/index.ts buildDevStubVerifierBackends: any non-empty EIN/DUNS/domain passes; OAuth2 returns the first allow-listed issuer). Production must wire REAL clients: OAuth2 issuer probe, EIN match against the signed-MSA record, Dun & Bradstreet DUNS lookup, DNS-TXT domain-control check. The store/route/FSM/gate plumbing is complete + live-tested; only the four backend implementations remain.

Release 3 / T5 — no apps/admin UI yet for tenant-side publisher onboarding (POST/GET /admin/marketplace/publisher). The cernia-ops review queue UI shipped 2026-06-13 (apps/cernia-ops/src/app/cernia-ops/platform/publishers/page.tsx — list + Verify + Reject). Tenant-side onboarding form remains.

Auth Plans 3 + 4 — DEFERRED ITEMS only (core shipped 2026-06-11; Google self-serve signup JIT-promote verified LIVE 2026-06-14, see history): social login cross-tenant chooser, Apple provider; adaptive/risk-based auth, trust-this-device + refresh tokens, password-reset UI, per-tenant MFA-policy enforcement UI, full auth-event audit pipeline. Real OAuth creds = procurement (Google/Microsoft client IDs). Real SMS creds (Twilio) + email SMTP (Google Workspace) code-side shipped 2026-06-11; external remaining = toll-free verification / A2P 10DLC registration (SMS) and App Password + SPF/DKIM/DMARC DNS (email).

history.note: Auth Plan 3 (social) — Google self-serve signup JIT-promote VERIFIED LIVE end-to-end, and two root-cause bugs fixed that had blocked it. The installer-wizard 'Continue with Google' flow reserves an 'invited' credential-less admin, redirects to Google (PKCE), and on callback exchanges the code + JIT-promotes the invited admin to 'active'. Two defects surfaced only under a real browser round-trip (unit/route tests stub the exchanger + storage, so both were invisible): (1) ENV/INFRA split-brain — apps/api/.env CERNIA_PUBLIC_BASE_URL pointed at a trycloudflare tunnel that forwarded to a SECOND, Google-DISABLED apps/api instance (a parallel session's :8080 from C:\dev\cernia-fhir, google:false), so /start ran on the Google-enabled API but the OAuth callback landed on the other one → token exchange failed → wizard showed #social_error=code-exchange-failed. Resolved for dev by running the whole round-trip on http://localhost:8081 (Google client already has the localhost callback registered); .env is gitignored so this is a local-dev change only. (2) CODE — apps/api/src/index.ts promoteInvitedAdmin called socialUserStorage.update(tenantId, updated, {reference:'Device/...'}) passing the actor Reference as the 3rd positional arg, but the real StorageAdapter.update signature is (tenantId, resource, ifMatchVersionId?: string, actor?: Reference); the Reference landed in ifMatchVersionId, the adapter parseInt-ed the object to NaN and threw ConflictError (409 'request expected [object Object]'), so promotion failed AFTER a successful exchange. The narrow BuiltInUserStorage.update interface wrongly declared the 3rd param as actor and the 'as unknown as' cast hid the mismatch. FIX: builtin-user-store.ts aligns the interface with the real adapter (ifMatchVersionId? then actor?); index.ts passes undefined for the precondition (single-writer JIT promote) and the actor in the 4th slot. Verified LIVE against real Postgres (:5433): invited admin User/da75b7c6 in tenant kamal-google-e2e-clinic went status invited->active, version_id 1->2, mfa.emailVerifiedAt stamped; the wizard received a real cernia_token (sub=User/da75b7c6, tenant_id=kamal-google-e2e-clinic, idp_type=oidc) with NO social_error. @cernia/api typecheck=0 (only the pre-existing marketplace-review-routes.ts:237 carry-forward remains); social-auth-routes.test.ts 35/35 green. Commit da536791.

Release 3 residual #4 — cernia-ops publisher-verification REVIEW UI. NEW PublisherVerificationStore.list() (cross-tenant SELECT, ordered by status then tenant_id; commercial legal-entity records, NOT PHI per ADR 0054) + GET /cernia-ops/marketplace/publishers (cernia-super-admin review queue) + POST /cernia-ops/marketplace/publishers/:tenantId/$reject {reason} (manual rejected verdict, sibling to the auto $verify — does NOT run the verifier). NEW apps/cernia-ops platform/publishers/page.tsx ('use client'): lists every publisher application with status badge + EIN/DUNS/domain + verifiedAt/rejectionReason; Verify + Reject (window.prompt reason) buttons on pending/unverified rows via fetchOpsJson (CSRF double-submit), refreshes after; testids ops-publishers-page / ops-publisher-row-<tenant> / verify-<tenant> / reject-<tenant> / ops-publishers-empty. Sidebar 'Publishers' nav added under Platform. Verified: @cernia/api typecheck=0 + 13/13 tests green (publisher-verification-store incl. new list() ordering test + marketplace-publisher-flow incl. new GET-list + $reject route tests, real PG :5433); @cernia/cernia-ops-portal typecheck=0. LIVE-verified on running :8090 (dev tsx-watch picked up routes): seed pending publisher (tenant-admin) → GET lists it pending-first → $verify → verified(200) → $reject 2nd tenant → rejected+reason(200) → tenant token on GET → 403. Files: apps/api/src/{publisher-verification-store,cernia-ops-marketplace-publisher-routes}.ts, apps/cernia-ops/src/{lib/publishers-api.ts,app/cernia-ops/platform/publishers/page.tsx,components/sidebar.tsx}.

Release 3 / T5 — third-party marketplace publishing: publisher verification + approval gate. NEW per-tenant PublisherVerification persistence (migration 265_publisher_verification.sql + apps/api/src/publisher-verification-store.ts, PK tenant_id, strictly tenant-scoped get/upsert). Tenant onboarding POST/GET /admin/marketplace/publisher (apps/api/src/marketplace-publisher-routes.ts) gated by the marketplace_access product (createRequireProduct). Ops adjudication POST /cernia-ops/marketplace/publishers/:tenantId/$verify (apps/api/src/cernia-ops-marketplace-publisher-routes.ts, cernia-super-admin) runs @cernia/marketplace PublisherVerifier with DEV-STUB backends (index.ts buildDevStubVerifierBackends — production wires real OAuth2/EIN/DUNS/domain). The admin listing-submit route (admin-marketplace-publish-routes.ts) now REQUIRES a verified publisher (403 publisher-not-verified) + marketplace_access; added optional bundleManifest with semver validation. Explicit 'approved' listing state added to core MarketplaceListingStatus + LEGAL_TRANSITIONS (in-review→approved→published / approved→in-review / approved→rejected, all additive — in-review→published kept); marketplace-review-routes now promotes a both-approved review through the FSM (in-review→approved→published) via ListingPublisher. deriveOperation mapped the new routes (synthetic MarketplacePublisher) in iam-route-guards.ts. Verified: @cernia/marketplace typecheck+test (293/293) + dist rebuilt; @cernia/api typecheck=0, build clean; live integration test marketplace-publisher-flow.integration.test.ts (real PG: unverified→submit-blocked→onboard→ops-verify→submit-allowed→review→approved→published + 402 no-marketplace_access + tenant isolation, 5/5) + publisher-verification-store.integration.test.ts (5/5) + listing-publisher (24/24) + marketplace-review (6/6) all green against test PG :5433. Running :8090 API needs a restart to serve the new routes (consumes @cernia/core + @cernia/marketplace dist, both rebuilt). Files: packages/core/src/types.ts, packages/marketplace/src/listings/listing-publisher.ts, packages/storage/src/migrations/265_publisher_verification.sql, apps/api/src/{publisher-verification-store,marketplace-publisher-routes,cernia-ops-marketplace-publisher-routes,admin-marketplace-publish-routes,marketplace-review-routes,iam-route-guards,index}.ts.

Release 2 (FHIR Console) — cernia-ops marketplace PUBLISH route (the platform-operator companion to the tenant-scoped publish shipped in Cernia Store Plan 4). NEW apps/api/src/cernia-ops-marketplace-publish-routes.ts: super-admin-gated route that creates an 'in-review' listing with the publisher forced server-side to the Cernia PLATFORM publisher (never client-trusted), kind 'workflow'. Also reconciled the listing-kind union to the core 'workflow' kind so the ops-published and tenant-published listings share one type. Commit 383d4139. Extends the existing dual-lead review→published lifecycle; no remaining bullet changes (stage already complete).

Auth subsystem Plan 1 (foundational) — Email/SMS OTP + verification core (tracked here because it gates onboarding; a dedicated auth/identity track can be split out later). (1) auth_verifications table (mig 258) + OtpService (@cernia/auth-providers: issueOtp/verifyOtp, 6-digit code SHA-256-hashed at rest + never logged, 10m TTL, attempt-cap, rate-limit, timing-safe compare, single-use) + PostgresOtpStore. 10 tests. (2) Email/SMS OTP bridged into the existing builtin-provider MFA half-session: password success with method email/sms issues+sends an OTP and stashes the verificationId on the half-session; mfa-challenge verifies it; TOTP/WebAuthn paths UNCHANGED; fail-closed without OtpService. 42 tests. (3) Verification routes apps/api/src/auth-verification-routes.ts: POST /api/auth/verify/email/start|confirm (pre-auth, allowlisted) + phone/start|confirm (authed), enumeration-safe, no code logging; confirm sets UserMfaState.emailVerifiedAt / mfaPhoneVerifiedAt (User JSONB, no migration). Concrete OtpEmailSender (W1 email) + OtpSmsSender (W5 Twilio) with dev log-only noop fallback; OtpService wired at boot. Onboarding $complete gates on email-verified (409 when unverified; active only when a real email sender is configured, so dev provisioning is unchanged). 40 tests. DEFERRED to later Auth plans: Google/Microsoft social login, the real login UI (replacing token paste-in), step-up MFA, anti-abuse, the cernia-ops auth-security view. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-10-auth-onboarding-verification-mfa-social-design.md. Built at the stable C:\dev clone.

Cernia Store Plan 5 — public-surface checkout: a prospect's storefront plan/product selection now carries through signup to provisioning. (1) apps/web public-storefront CTAs pass ?tier=<id> / ?products=<id>; the installer start proxy forwards query->body; POST /onboarding/start accepts + validates {tier (sandbox/starter/growth/enterprise), products (subset of KNOWN_PRODUCTS)} and stores them on the session (selectedTier/selectedProducts). 30 onboarding-routes + 29 public-storefront tests. (2) OnboardingFullProvisioner consumes the selection: grant loop priority selectedProducts -> use-case.products -> ['phone-agent'] (filtered to KNOWN_PRODUCTS); startTrial plan = mapTierToTrialPlan(selectedTier) (sandbox/starter->starter, growth->business, enterprise->enterprise) ?? default; no-selection defaults unchanged; idempotent. 17 tests. provisionTenant already creates the Stripe CUSTOMER + tenant + admin user + Twilio subaccount (built). DEFERRED (procurement): live Stripe SUBSCRIPTION at signup (real keys + Price-ID sync); paid conversion stays the existing trial->paid path. api/trust/installer typecheck clean. Built at the stable C:\dev clone.

Cernia Store Plan 4 — marketplace POPULATION shipped (the last Store backend gap). (1) Catalog PERSISTED: the pre-existing-but-unwired PostgresListingStore (@cernia/marketplace, mig 018_marketplace_listings) is now wired into buildMarketplaceRuntime({db}) + injected at boot; seeded 4 published listings (voice-agent, patient-recall, intake-bot, no-show-recovery) idempotently — verified LIVE (GET /store/catalog returns 4 marketplace entries from Postgres, survives restart; was 1 in-memory seed). 9 store + 8 route tests. (2) PUBLISH backend: POST /admin/marketplace/listings (admin-gated, iam write/MarketplaceListing) creates an 'in-review' listing with publisher forced server-side to the caller tenant (never client-trusted); the apps/admin Publish form wired (slug + support-email + real submit); in-review listings stay OUT of the published catalog (tested). 9 integration tests. (3) REVIEWS wired: apps/admin Approve/Reject buttons -> POST /admin/marketplace/reviews; a dual-lead terminal decision flips the listing in-review->published (approved) or ->rejected; pending (one lead) leaves it in-review; added 'rejected' to MarketplaceListingStatus + FSM transition; non-fatal on a missing listing. 14 tests. Full lifecycle now: Publish -> dual-lead Review -> Published -> Browse/storefront; the existing purchase loop (Browse -> $build-session -> marketplace_entitlements -> My Installs) is intact. api + admin typecheck clean. Built at the stable C:\dev clone.

Cernia Store Plan 3 — cernia-ops cross-tenant /platform/store view shipped (completes the Store's multi-tenant surface: tenant-admin + platform-operator). (1) GET /cernia-ops/platform/store (apps/api/src/cernia-ops-store-routes.ts): aggregate-only product adoption (tenants per product) + est. MRR/ARR (tenantCount x catalog_prices monthly; entitlements don't store paid price) + tier distribution + recent-grant counts. cernia-super-admin gated (requireSuperAdmin) + sendPhiSafe (assertNoPhi); tenant_id is used ONLY inside COUNT(DISTINCT) aggregations, never returned — no PHI, no tenant identifiers (ADR-0054, CLAUDE.md s5). 7/7 real-PG tests incl. 401 no-principal / 403 non-operator / numeric-only. (2) apps/cernia-ops /cernia-ops/platform/store page mirroring /expansion (server load -> typed fetch -> WireUpBanner on 503), dark ops theme + NoPhiBanner + client-side numeric/enum shape guard, 'est.' MRR labels, sidebar 'Store' entry in the Platform group; NO @cernia/branding (cernia-branded.test.ts green). 12 store-api + 37 cernia-ops tests pass; build compiles /platform/store. Built at the stable C:\dev clone.

Cernia Store Plan 2 — unified Stripe CHECKOUT (tiers + products) shipped + headed-E2E verified. ONE Stripe subscription, mixed licensed items. (1) Pure cart validation + line-item mapper (apps/api/src/store-checkout-cart.ts): validateCart rejects already-active / tier-included / enterprise; cartToLineItems maps to stripe price ids. (2) POST /admin/store/checkout (store-checkout-routes.ts): validate (409), ensureCustomer, build a mode=subscription Checkout Session from catalog_prices.stripe_price_id + metadata{cernia_tenant_id, cernia_cart}; dev-noop returns a deterministic URL, but REAL Stripe failures now surface 502 (a code review caught + fixed a fake-URL-masking bug); iam-mapped write/BillingAccount (authenticated, NOT allowlisted). (3) Webhook fan-out — /billing/webhook/stripe on checkout.session.completed reads cernia_cart and idempotently sets tier (reuses subscriptions.upsert + tierResolver.invalidate) + grantProduct per product; the ProductGrantStore seam is WIRED into index.ts boot (a review caught it was left unwired, which would have silently skipped product grants in prod). (4) Cart UI in StorefrontCatalog (apps/admin): +Add on available entries, sticky aria-live cart bar, Checkout with Stripe -> createCheckout. Tests: cart 5/5, checkout-route 4/4 (incl. 502), billing webhook 16/16 (incl. idempotent re-delivery + no-manifest passthrough), storefront 14/14, full admin suite 1092/1092; headed E2E tests/e2e/specs/store-checkout.spec.ts green (cart->checkout, visual baseline). Each task spec- + code-reviewed. Marketplace checkout stays on $build-session (spec decision 7). REMAINING for Store: real Stripe Price-ID sync + live keys (procurement); public-surface checkout via provisionTenant; cernia-ops /platform/store view. B4 (Stripe webhook hardening): (1) Confirmed + pinned the timestamp-tolerance gate — packages/billing/src/backends/stripe-backend.ts verifySignature already enforces a 300s (overridable) tolerance window, but the only prior test happened to fail on hmac-mismatch; added stripe-backend.test.ts cases that compute a REAL node:crypto HMAC over a STALE timestamp and assert rejection with reason=outside-tolerance-window (and the converse fresh-but-nonzero-skew accept), proving the tolerance check is enforced independently of HMAC validity. Corrected the stale file-header doc comment that claimed the tolerance check was unimplemented. (2) invoice.paid / payment_intent.succeeded now flip the TENANT SUBSCRIPTION status, not just the invoice: added settlement.subscriptionStatus ('active' on paid, 'past-due' on payment_failed) to WebhookSyncResult (packages/billing/src/types.ts) + set it in the backend; the webhook route apps/api/src/billing-routes.ts now (a) persists the invoice status via invoices.update (paidAt stamped) AND (b) loads ONLY result.affected.tenantId's active subscription and drives it through the FSM (transitionSubscription/canTransition) — already-active is an idempotent no-op (no FSM throw), illegal transitions (e.g. cancelled→active) are skipped, and tierResolver.invalidate(tenantId) is called. Tenant-isolated: a webhook naming a different tenant never mutates this tenant (test proves acme stays past-due when event names 'intruder'). (3) Metering→invoice push glue: NEW packages/billing/src/invoicing/metering-invoice-push.ts pushMeteredUsageToInvoice() composes meter.aggregate(finalize:true) → invoiceGenerator.generate → invoiceStore.insert(draft) → backend.pushInvoice → invoiceStore.update(open + backendInvoiceId), with same-tenant asserts on subscription/rollup/invoice references (cross-tenant → invalid-invoice-state, pushes nothing); exported from packages/billing/src/index.ts. Stripe INBOUND webhook signature verification FIXED + verified live against the operator's sandbox. Bug surfaced by the 3b smoke test: packages/billing/src/backends/stripe-backend.ts defaultHmac used CommonJS require('node:crypto'), but @cernia/billing is type:module (ESM) → 'ReferenceError: require is not defined' on EVERY inbound POST /billing/webhook/stripe in production (apps/api constructs StripeBillingBackend with no injected hmac; all prior unit tests injected a mock hmac, masking it). Fix: top-level import { createHmac } from 'node:crypto'. Added 2 regression tests in stripe-backend.test.ts exercising the REAL defaultHmac (no injected hmac): valid sig accepted, tampered rejected. Verified live with the operator's configured STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET (value never logged) — 6/6: valid invoice.paid->settlement=paid, replay->webhook-replay, tampered sig/payload->hmac-mismatch, stale ts->outside-tolerance-window, invoice.payment_failed->uncollectible. ra-sbm-5 partial close — x1b-revenue-recognition compute layer mounted. apps/api/src/revenue-recognition-routes.ts wraps the package's buildSchedule + buildPointInTimeSchedule + buildUsageBasedSchedule pure functions as 2 HTTP endpoints. POST /admin/revenue/$build-schedule validates Contract + RevenueEvent[] body, sorts events by occurredAt ascending, routes to the right helper per contract.method, returns RecognitionSchedule with periods + cumulative + deferred-balance — daily-prorated waterfall. POST /admin/revenue/$close-month is the CFO monthly-close endpoint: take a YYYY-MM string + Array<{contract, events}>, build each schedule, sum recognizedCents across periods whose periodStart starts with the month, return {monthIso, totalRecognizedCents, byContract[]}. 10 in-process tests pass: ratable schedule (~10_192¢/month for a $1200/yr contract, daily-prorated), point-in-time method, 3×400 validation, multi-contract close sum, 400 on bad monthIso, 400 on missing contracts, zero-period return, cache-control headers. apps/api/package.json adds @cernia/x1b-revenue-recognition workspace dep. Persistent contract + event storage is the next followup.
