Comprehensive-Stripe S5 (dunning / Smart Retries) shipped: invoice.payment_failed -> dunning email + past-due (enteredPastDueAt stamped); customer.subscription.trial_will_end -> trial-ending email; nightly sweepDunning suspends past-due subs past the DunningPolicy grace window (+ suspended email); local retry-now scheduling REMOVED — Stripe Smart Retries (charge_automatically, a Stripe-dashboard setting) owns payment retries. billingNotifications email seam wired in index.ts. 15 dunning + 42 billing-routes tests. Remaining: S6 webhooks(+consolidate /stripe-pm), S7 refunds, S8 e2e.

Comprehensive-Stripe S6 (webhook completeness + reconcile) shipped: CONSOLIDATED the separate /billing/webhook/stripe-pm endpoint into the single /billing/webhook/stripe (one URL + one signing secret at go-live) — the main handler now applies paymentMethodSync to the tenant_payment_methods cache. Added charge.refunded (-> invoice metadata refundedAmount/At) + charge.dispute.created/closed (read-only logged). POST /admin/billing/$reconcile backfills the local mirror from Stripe (getSubscription + listPaymentMethods). stripe-backend 81 + billing-routes 52 + payment-methods 12 tests. The S3 /stripe-pm consolidation follow-up is CLOSED. Remaining: S7 refunds(initiation route + admin), S8 e2e harness + go-live runbook.

Comprehensive-Stripe S7 (refunds) shipped: backend createRefund (POST /v1/refunds via charge/payment_intent) + getInvoice; POST /admin/billing/invoices/:id/$refund (Carease, guards paid+not-fully-refunded, idempotency-keyed, webhook reconciles metadata.refundedAmount). apps/admin invoice list+detail surface a Refunded badge (from metadata) + a gated Refund action (confirm dialog, optional partial amount). 61 billing-routes + 90 stripe-backend + 8 admin invoice tests. (Disputes are logged server-side; no UI badge.) Remaining: S8 e2e test-mode harness + go-live runbook.

Comprehensive-Stripe S8 (final) shipped — COMPLETE S1-S8. Gated full-lifecycle test-mode harness packages/billing/src/backends/stripe-lifecycle.live.test.ts (ensureCustomer->setupIntent->meter->subscription[gated STRIPE_PRICE_ID]->getSubscription->updateSubscription[proration]->cancel->refund[gated STRIPE_REFUND_CHARGE_ID]; skips clean without a key, always-on gating contract). Go-live runbook docs/runbooks/go-live-stripe.md: what shipped S1-S8 + the operator/legal go-live checklist (sk_live_, live webhook secret, signed BAA, restricted key, single webhook URL+events, Smart Retries, live price/meter sync) + env-var reference + manual Payment-Element add-card verification + post-flip smoke/rollback. Stripe is system-of-record for recurring billing, dev runs TEST mode (CERNIA_BILLING_BACKEND=stripe + sk_test_); only the sk_test_->sk_live_ key flip + signed BAA remain at go-live (no code change). NO PHI to Stripe (tenant-id/tier metadata only).

Release 2 (FHIR Console) — NEW @cernia/fhir-console shared resource-browser package built + wired into BOTH tenant + platform-operator surfaces, closing the multi-surface gap this track exists to track. (1) packages/fhir-console/ — scaffold + FHIR client (metadata/capability feed + search/read, 15 client tests) + a <FhirConsole> component (resource-type sidebar + results list + JSON inspector) (commits 765c8b41, 31ae0e8b, 790bda0a). (2) Embedded in apps/admin at /admin/fhir over the tenant's OWN data, read-only (apps/admin/src/app/admin/fhir/ — layout.tsx + page.tsx, commit 0a2d400b). (3) Embedded in apps/cernia-ops over a Cernia-owned SANDBOX tenant via a new super-admin-gated sandbox-token route that ignores any client-supplied tenantId (apps/api/src/cernia-ops-sandbox.ts + cernia-ops-sandbox-routes.ts, security-tested in cernia-ops-sandbox-routes.test.ts; UI at apps/cernia-ops/src/app/cernia-ops/workshop/console/, commit 855b39c8) — keeps the cross-tenant boundary intact (operators browse the sandbox, never a real tenant's PHI). Triager note: there is no dedicated FHIR-data-tooling stage in this track; the FHIR console is recorded at TRACK level (not folded into cds-hooks-quality / ml-platform, which it doesn't extend) because it is a net-new cross-package surface, and per CLAUDE.md the right move is to log it under the closest existing track with an explicit note rather than invent a new top-level track. If FHIR data-tooling grows further, promote this to its own stage.

Track created from the 2026-05-24 shelf-ware audit. 6 stages, all baseline+current=absent. ~30-40 genuine shelf-ware packages grouped into clusters; ~140 catalog-* and specialty-* packages are lazy-load-by-design and intentionally NOT included here.

CDS Hooks runtime mounted in apps/api. GET /cds-services discovery + POST /cds-services/:hookId invocation backed by @cernia/cds-hooks. Starter patient-view service registered with a 'voice-agent available' info card. 6 new integration tests cover discovery, happy path, unknown hookId, invalid body, inactive-service filtering. apps/api: 1894/1894 tests pass.

Model registry mounted in apps/api as /admin/ml/models — list names, list versions, resolve, register. In-memory store seeded with risk-classifier-v4.0.0-comprehensive shim. 8 new integration tests. apps/api: 1902/1902 tests pass.

Marketplace discovery + slug-resolve + create endpoints live at /marketplace/listings, backed by @cernia/marketplace's InMemoryListingStore (seeded with Cernia voice-agent). POST /admin/saas-metrics/snapshot computes MRR/ARR/ARPA via @cernia/x1c-saas-metrics. 8 new integration tests. apps/api: 1910/1910 tests pass.

RPM compute endpoints live: /rpm/readings/$evaluate (threshold classification), /rpm/care-plan-templates (list 6 shipped), /rpm/care-plans/$generate, /rpm/adherence/$compute (score + intervention). Stateless pure-function over caller-supplied input. 7 new integration tests. Stateful device webhook + WebRTC signaling + UI remain.

Proxy lifecycle (grant / revoke / list-by-patient) + JIT request + break-glass invoke endpoints live in apps/api. In-memory proxy store. JIT + break-glass routes are pure-function with blocker codes for empty-scopes, short-justification, etc. 8 new integration tests. SAML/OIDC federation, persistent store, access-review-attestation, admin UI remain.

OIDC SSO login flow wired end-to-end at apps/api boot — closed the SSO-boot-wiring deferral. New sso-user-store.ts (FHIR-User provisioner + lookup over the StorageAdapter; JIT provisioning, RoleBinding role resolution, safe-by-default empty roles, MFA delegated to IdP) + TenantSsoFactory composed at index.ts step 10i.1 with KmsBackedJwtIssuer, Redis/InMemory OIDC state store, FetchOidcHttpClient, and a tenant-secret-backed ClientSecretResolver. 13 adapter tests; @cernia/api typecheck + build clean. Only SAML 2.0 handshake remains deferred.

Workflow template discovery (list/find/latest) + bot static-analysis endpoint live in apps/api. InMemoryTemplateRegistry seeded with the cernia-voice-followup template. 7 new integration tests. Scheduler + sandbox dry-run + j2c/j2d/j3g + admin UI remain. All 6 functionality-utilization stages are now partial — none absent.

tracks.name: External Integrations & per-tenant config
tracks.description: Per-tenant credentials + vendor backends the voice agent talks to (Twilio for telephony, Deepgram for STT, ElevenLabs for TTS, OpenAI/Anthropic for LLM). Also covers self-serve tenant provisioning + per-tenant workflow customization. Without this layer every tenant shares the platform's trial credentials and can't customize the assistant.

stages.name: Tenant secrets — per-tenant credential storage
stages.rationale: Each tenant must paste their own Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/LLM credentials so calls bill to their accounts. Plaintext is encrypted at rest with envelope encryption (AES-256-GCM); the resolver falls back to platform-level env vars when no per-tenant override exists.

baseline.summary: No per-tenant credential storage. The voice agent's Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/OpenAI/Anthropic keys came from process.env only. Every tenant in a multi-tenant deploy would share the same vendor accounts — billing attribution impossible, key rotation per tenant impossible, white-glove onboarding meant editing the platform-level env.

current.summary: Shipped end-to-end. Migration 046_tenant_secrets (composite-PK (tenant_id, provider), opaque ciphertext column, JSONB metadata). TenantSecretResolver in @cernia/core with NoopSecretCipher (dev) + AesGcmSecretCipher (prod). apps/api boot now wires KyselyTenantSecretStore (secrets survive restart) + refuses to start in production when CERNIA_TENANT_SECRET_KEY/KID is unset or wrong length — TenantSecretBootError thrown loud. PUT/DELETE handlers emit structured tenant-secret.write/delete IAM audit rows. KMS rotation runbook (docs/operations/runbooks/tenant-secret-key-rotation.md) covers cadence, rehearsal in staging, multi-key decrypt window, re-encrypt job, and rollback.

history.note: Swapped apps/api boot from InMemoryTenantSecretStore → KyselyTenantSecretStore. Added prod-mode TenantSecretBootError refusing missing/short keys. Authored KMS rotation runbook with rehearse-in-staging + multi-key window + re-encrypt job.

stages.name: Twilio voice backend boot wiring
stages.rationale: @cernia/telephony ships a TwilioVoiceBackend + LiveKit/SIP backend. apps/api needs to instantiate one and wire it into the voice-call handler so an inbound call actually arrives at the dialog engine.

baseline.summary: packages/telephony has TwilioVoiceBackend + LiveKit/SIP backend with full unit coverage. apps/api/src/voice-call-handler.ts handles barge-in + recording-pause-on-PHI. NOT yet wired: the boot path doesn't construct a TwilioVoiceBackend instance and the /telephony/inbound webhook is stub-only.

current.summary: TwilioVoiceBackend fully wired. buildTwilioVoiceClient() native-fetch factory added to @cernia/telephony (no SDK dependency — uses Node 20+ fetch). Boot path in apps/api constructs TwilioVoiceBackend when CERNIA_TELEPHONY_BACKEND=twilio + TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID + TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN + CERNIA_PUBLIC_BASE_URL are set; falls back to stub with structured log. 26 new tests cover create/fetch/update + auth header + idempotency. Typecheck + full telephony test suite clean.

history.note: LIVE OUTBOUND PSTN CALLS surfaced + closed TWO real telephony bugs that only a genuine call could expose (the inbound trial call could not reach the /voice path; outbound + a keypress past the trial preamble does). Setup: same cloudflared tunnel + operator trial Twilio account; destination +13029834679 added as a Verified Caller ID (trial accounts can only dial verified numbers); outbound placed via POST /telephony/outbound (real JWT → RBAC deriveOperation /telephony/outbound→TelephonyCall → caller-id allowlist assertOwnedByTenant(+12402444312) → real Twilio Calls API → CallSid → call.outbound.placed audit → call_sessions row → 200 — all proven live). BUG 1 (apps/api/src/telephony-routes.ts, POST /telephony/twilio/voice): the handler resolved the tenant from the To number — correct for INBOUND (To = our number) but WRONG for OUTBOUND, where Twilio fetches the TwiML *after* we placed the call and the tenant-owned leg is From (To is the external party). So every outbound TwiML callback resolved tenantId=null → call.unknown-number → no dialog (observed live: CallSid CA0f96c6… fetched /voice after a keypress but bailed to unknown-number). FIX: branch on body.Direction; for outbound-* resolve tenant from From, mark the existing outbound session answered, emit call.outbound.answered, return Connect/Stream TwiML (skip the inbound _dispatchInbound path). LIVE-VERIFIED on a fresh call (CallSid CA41d57c…): call.outbound.answered now fires with tenantId=22222222-… resolved from From, and the call_sessions row flips ringing→in-progress with answered_at set. BUG 2 (packages/telephony/src/backends/twilio-backend.ts placeOutboundCall): StatusCallbackEvent was sent COMMA-separated ('initiated,ringing,answered,completed'); Twilio's Calls API requires the call-progress event list to be SPACE-separated and parses a comma-joined value as one invalid token → subscribes to NO events → the StatusCallback never fires → outbound sessions never reach 'completed' (confirmed live: 0 /status POSTs for any of 3 outbound calls, while inbound /status worked because that comes from the phone NUMBER's own StatusCallback config, not this param). FIX: 'initiated ringing answered completed' (space-separated) + doc-comment corrected. ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0 + telephony-routes.test.ts 18/18 (added an outbound-direction regression test asserting tenant-from-From + call.outbound.answered + session direction stays outbound); @cernia/telephony typecheck=0 + twilio-backend.test.ts 26/26 (2 assertions updated to the space-separated form). The /voice fix is LIVE (apps/api hot-reloaded by tsx watch); the StatusCallbackEvent fix is unit-verified and goes live on the next @cernia/telephony rebuild + api restart. Secrets used as launch-time env overrides only; trial account = no BAA → synthetic/non-PHI calls only. UPDATE (live re-verify, same day): the BUG 2 comma→space change was INSUFFICIENT — Twilio rejected the space-joined value too, emitting notification 21626 'invalid statusCallbackEvents' on CallSid CA1bac94… (a live call surfaced this; unit tests never could). TRUE ROOT CAUSE: Twilio's Calls REST API requires StatusCallbackEvent as a REPEATED form param (StatusCallbackEvent=initiated&StatusCallbackEvent=ringing&…), not any single joined string. FINAL FIX in packages/telephony/src/backends/twilio-backend.ts buildTwilioVoiceClient.calls.create: split the whitespace event string and params.append() each event. LIVE-CONFIRMED on CallSid CAf4ebe5…: /status POSTs 1→5 (initiated/ringing/answered/completed all delivered), Twilio notifications now [] (21626 gone), audit chain call.outbound.placed→answered→ended (tenant 22222222), call_sessions row reached status=completed duration_seconds=4 — the outbound voice lifecycle now closes end-to-end on a genuine PSTN call. twilio-backend.test.ts 26/26 (added a getAll('StatusCallbackEvent') repeated-param assertion); @cernia/telephony typecheck=0. Also added a wiring cross-reference comment at the outbound-backend construction in apps/api/src/index.ts.

LIVE INBOUND PSTN CALL verified end-to-end through a public tunnel — the genuine-call half of the Wave-E telephony path that unit/synthetic tests could not exercise. Setup: cloudflared quick tunnel (https://sailing-multimedia-maple-nearly.trycloudflare.com) → local apps/api :8080 booted with CERNIA_TELEPHONY_BACKEND=twilio + the operator's real account (AC2ca625…, trial) + CERNIA_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=tunnel; the number +12402444312 (PN3eba5f6…) registered in tenant_phone_numbers (tenant 22222222-… purpose voice-agent) with its Twilio VoiceUrl + StatusCallback pointed at the tunnel via the Twilio REST API. RESULT: a real inbound call (CallSid CAed80803a93606fef5a64aac9edee9561, from +13029834679, status=completed, 13s) hit POST /telephony/twilio/status through the tunnel; our handler VALIDATED the Twilio HMAC-SHA1 signature against the reconstructed https URL (Host header preserved by cloudflared), emitted a call.ended audit (toNumberHash 067e8a95…d6ac93 — byte-identical to the hash of +12402444312, proving it was our number; status=completed, durationSeconds=13) and returned 204. Twilio's own Calls.json log corroborates the same CallSid/from/duration. This proves the live chain: real PSTN → Twilio → public tunnel → Fastify route → signature validation → audit → 204. The /voice (media) webhook did NOT fire on this inbound call — only /status did (log: 2 voice POSTs = both the synthetic signed test [valid→200+call_sessions row; bad-sig→403], 1 status POST = the real call) — because on a Twilio TRIAL account inbound calls play the mandatory ~13s trial greeting and the custom inbound VoiceUrl isn't proxied as on a paid account; NOT a handler defect (the /voice path is independently proven by the real-Twilio-signed synthetic webhook → 200 + persisted CAtuntest… session). REMAINING for live /voice media on a genuine inbound call: an UPGRADED (non-trial) Twilio account, and a signed Twilio BAA before any real patient audio is routed (trial = no BAA → synthetic/non-PHI only). Secrets used as launch-time env overrides only; never written to a committed file.
buildTwilioVoiceClient native-fetch factory + TwilioVoiceBackend boot wiring in apps/api. 26 new tests.

ra-tel-2 + ra-tel-3 closed. telephonyRoutes plugin (apps/api/src/telephony-routes.ts) registered in apps/api/src/index.ts section 10g-telephony — POST /telephony/twilio/voice (inbound HMAC-verified webhook with tenant lookup via PostgresTenantPhoneNumberStore), POST /telephony/twilio/status (status callback), POST /telephony/outbound (internal REST surface wrapping placeAuditedOutboundCall). Caller-id allowlist enforced via phoneNumbers.assertOwnedByTenant; OutboundSpoofError → 403 with call.spoof-attempt-blocked audit row. HMAC verification uses computeTwilioSignature from @cernia/notifications + constant-time compare. 5 new unit tests pass; full telephony-routes suite is 17/17 green.

ra-tel-4 closed. Verified the Twilio integration against the REAL Twilio Test API using account-provided test credentials: a SIMULATED outbound call to the magic number +15005550006 via the package's buildTwilioVoiceClient + TwilioVoiceBackend returned a valid CallSid (no real call placed, $0). Added durable gated smoke test packages/telephony/src/backends/twilio-backend.sandbox.test.ts — runs against the real Test API when TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID + TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN are present (skips cleanly otherwise; CI maps TWILIO_TEST_* org secrets). Test creds live in gitignored apps/api/.env (never committed). Remaining ra-tel-1 (per-tenant TwilioVoiceBackend keyed via TenantSecretResolver) is unchanged — separate code task, not creds-blocked.

ra-tel-1 closed — telephony-backend stage now complete (remaining empty). New apps/api/src/per-tenant-twilio-router.ts (PerTenantTwilioVoiceRouter): wraps the boot-time telephony backend and, on each outbound call, resolves TenantSecretResolver.resolve(tenantId,'twilio') — Account SID from vendorAccountId, Auth Token from plaintext — to build (lazily, LRU-cached, rebuilt on credential rotation) a per-tenant TwilioVoiceBackend so a tenant originates + is billed on their OWN Twilio account; tenants without an override fall through to the platform default. Mirrors the ra-tts-4 PerTenantTtsRouter pattern. Wired in apps/api/src/index.ts initialiseVoiceCallStack: only the outbound surface is wrapped (outboundBackend) — the inbound `twilio` field stays the concrete TwilioVoiceBackend because the router does not implement the Twilio-specific _dispatchInbound. ALSO recreated the lost migration packages/storage/src/migrations/046_tenant_secrets.sql (source jumped 045→047; the table survived only in cernia-schema-snapshot.sql — a deploy migrating without it would 500 on every tenant-secret read for tenant-secrets routes + PerTenantTtsRouter + this router). Tested vs real Postgres: apps/api/src/per-tenant-twilio-router.integration.test.ts bootstraps the real 046 migration, seeds tenant_secrets via KyselyTenantSecretStore+TenantSecretResolver, and proves per-tenant isolation (tenant A vs B resolve to distinct accounts), platform-default fall-through (unconfigured tenant + token-without-SID), and cache + rotation semantics. apps/api typecheck=0.

stages.name: Deepgram STT adapter
stages.rationale: Speech-to-text is the input side of the voice loop. Deepgram is the BAA-covered default; per-tenant model + language overrides are read from tenant_secrets.metadata.

baseline.summary: @cernia/voice-agent has an ISpeechToText seam but no production adapter. All current eval/golden runs use a stubbed STT that echoes back a scripted transcript.

current.summary: DeepgramWsTransport production adapter shipped in apps/api/src/deepgram-ws-transport.ts. Uses ws npm package (already a dependency) with Authorization: Token header. Parses Results, SpeechStarted, UtteranceEnd, Error message types into DeepgramServerEvent. boot path wires DeepgramSttBackend when CERNIA_SPEECH_STT=deepgram + DEEPGRAM_API_KEY are set; falls back to stub STT with warn log otherwise. 13 new unit tests cover factory shape, JSON parsing (all event types), error resilience, and handler idempotency. api typecheck + full api test suite clean.

remaining: Run the STT-accuracy goldens nightly against the production API + execute the Deepgram BAA for PHI traffic (legal/procurement — the code path is now live-verified; see 2026-06-01 history). The live μ-law round-trip smoke test (apps/api/src/deepgram-stt.live.test.ts) covers the integration contract.

per-tenant STT shared-Deepgram warn fires per resolveBackend call (per utterance) for production tenants without a Deepgram project — add a per-tenant/per-session dedup or rate-limit (apps/api/src/per-tenant-stt-router.ts). Tracked by P1-5 review (2026-06-08).

history.note: Deepgram STT VALIDATED live against the operator's real key — AND fixed a real bug that meant STT produced ZERO output in production. (1) BUG FIXED (apps/api/src/deepgram-ws-transport.ts): the ws 'message' handler gated on `typeof data === 'string'`, but the `ws` library delivers Deepgram's JSON TEXT frames as a Node Buffer (isBinary=false), so EVERY transcript/VAD/error message was silently dropped — STT never emitted a single transcript. The unit test gave false confidence because it called onJsonMessage() directly with a string, bypassing the ws delivery path. Fix: new handleSocketMessage(data, isBinary) decodes any non-binary frame to UTF-8 (Buffer | Buffer[] | ArrayBuffer | string); binary frames ignored. (2) Live proof: new apps/api/src/deepgram-stt.live.test.ts — a TTS→STT round trip in the Twilio telephony format (ElevenLabs Sarah → ulaw_8000 → DeepgramSttBackend encoding=mulaw/8000 over the real WS) — Deepgram transcribed 'the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog' at 0.998 confidence. Diagnosed via direct WS probes that confirmed the full backend query (nova-2-medical + redact + no_retention, both finish modes) transcribes correctly, isolating the defect to the transport's frame-type guard. (3) Regression guard: 3 new unit tests exercise handleSocketMessage with a Buffer text frame / string / binary (the path the old test missed). Gated on ELEVENLABS_API_KEY + DEEPGRAM_API_KEY (skipIf). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, build=0, transport+live tests 18/18, changed-file lint clean (1 pre-existing close() require-await baseline). Inbound transcription now works end-to-end on the operator's free-tier Deepgram key.

ra-stt-3 closed — STT is now per-tenant-aware. NEW apps/api/src/per-tenant-stt-router.ts (PerTenantSttRouter): wraps the boot-time STT backend and, on each openStream(), resolves PerTenantSpeechCredentials.resolveDeepgram(tenantId) (the ra-stt-2 helper over TenantSecretResolver) — when source==='tenant' it builds a lazily-cached, rotation-aware per-tenant DeepgramSttBackend so a tenant transcribes on — and is billed on — their own Deepgram account; otherwise it delegates to the platform default. Twin of the ra-tts-4 PerTenantTtsRouter / ra-tel-1 PerTenantTwilioVoiceRouter. Wired in apps/api/src/index.ts initialiseVoiceCallStack, gated on CERNIA_SPEECH_STT=deepgram + tenantSecretResolver (so a deployment with no platform Deepgram key still honours a per-tenant override). Tested vs real Postgres (:5434): apps/api/src/per-tenant-stt-router.integration.test.ts bootstraps the recovered 046_tenant_secrets.sql migration, seeds via KyselyTenantSecretStore+TenantSecretResolver — 4/4 (per-tenant isolation A≠B, platform-default fall-through, cache + rotation). ADR-0127: apps/api typecheck=0, build=0, new-file lint=0. Only the procurement-blocked nightly-real-Deepgram fixture run remains on this stage.

ra-stt-2 closed (resolver wiring level) — PerTenantSpeechCredentials helper shipped in apps/api/src/per-tenant-speech-credentials.ts. Adapter around @cernia/core TenantSecretResolver with resolveDeepgram/resolveElevenLabs/resolveAzureSpeech/resolveOpenAi methods; returns {plaintext, source: 'tenant'|'platform-env', vendorAccountId, metadata} or undefined when neither override nor env exists. defaultPlatformEnvVarFor() provides automatic env-var fallback (DEEPGRAM_API_KEY etc). 10 unit tests cover the full matrix: undefined-when-empty / per-tenant override / env-var fallback / per-tenant wins over env / tenant isolation / Deepgram+Azure+OpenAI fallback paths / empty-string treated as miss / custom platformEnvLookup. apps/api typecheck clean. Boot-time SpeechServiceFactory swap (replace the static env-var read in apps/api/src/index.ts with a per-tenant factory cache that consumes this helper) is ra-stt-3's remaining work.

ra-stt-4 closed (eval scaffolding level) — STT-accuracy goldens ship in @cernia/voice-agent-evals. packages/voice-agent-evals/src/stt/wer.ts implements canonical Word Error Rate via Levenshtein DP at word level (case-insensitive, punctuation-stripped tokenization) with full S/D/I breakdown. scoreSttAccuracy returns {passed, notes, wer, result} with a default 0.2 threshold (80% accuracy). packages/voice-agent-evals/src/stt/golden.ts: SttGolden type + runSttGolden with a pluggable Transcriber interface — the same goldens run against (a) tests/external-fakes/deepgram-fake.ts in CI, (b) real Deepgram nightly, (c) Whisper / Azure Speech. echoTranscriber + constantTranscriber for unit tests. 16 tests pass: 12 WER unit (identical text + case/punct ignored + S/D/I single-error + empty edge cases + threshold boundary) + 4 golden runner (echo passes / constant fails / custom threshold / ENOENT on missing WAV). Public barrel exports added. Real-Deepgram WAV+transcript fixture commit + nightly run is the next followup (procurement-blocked on Deepgram production key + BAA per ra-stt-2).
DeepgramWsTransport transport adapter + apps/api boot wiring. 13 tests.

stages.name: ElevenLabs TTS adapter
stages.rationale: Text-to-speech is the output side of the loop. ElevenLabs is the default; per-tenant voice id is read from tenant_secrets.vendorAccountId. Tenant-defined persona (existing voice-agent persona editor) selects the voice.

baseline.summary: ITextToSpeech seam exists; no production adapter. Persona editor already exposes a voiceId picker with sample previews but every save is dropped on the floor.

current.summary: ElevenLabsFetchTransport production adapter shipped in apps/api/src/elevenlabs-fetch-transport.ts. Uses Node 22 native fetch with xi-api-key header + streaming ReadableStream body iteration. boot path wires ElevenLabsTtsBackend when CERNIA_SPEECH_TTS=elevenlabs + ELEVENLABS_API_KEY are set; falls back to stub TTS with warn log otherwise. 8 new unit tests cover factory shape, streaming multi-chunk assembly, HTTP 4xx error propagation, null body guard, and close idempotency. api typecheck clean.

history.note: P3 voice-selection COMPLETE (Cards 1–6 all shipped, verified, e2e green). Per-tenant voice selection: configurable TTS model+full voice settings (Card 1, packages/speech/src/backends/elevenlabs-tts-backend.ts); curated catalog route GET /api/voice-agent/voices (Card 2, apps/api/src/voice-catalog-routes.ts + packages/speech/src/elevenlabs-voice-catalog.ts); per-tenant VoiceAgentPersona voiceModel+voiceSettings+voiceLanguage persisted to voice_tenant_config.persona (Card 3, packages/voice-agent/src/persona.ts + voice-agent-admin-routes.ts); admin voice picker page (Card 4, apps/admin/src/app/admin/voice-agent/persona/page.tsx); opt-in IVC clone + consent gate (Card 5, apps/api/src/voice-clone-routes.ts + voice-clone-consent-store.ts + packages/speech/src/elevenlabs-voice-clone.ts + migration 251_voice_clone_consent.sql). Card 6 hardening: setClonedVoiceId changed to UPDATE-only (throws ConsentNotFoundError if no consent row — prevents phantom system insert); voice-clone-routes.ts wraps setClonedVoiceId with ConsentNotFoundError → 409; tightened 2 weak tests (403 for non-admin consent endpoint; 400 for invalid base64). E2E (tests/e2e/specs/voice-selection.spec.ts — 5/5 green vs real apps/api + real Postgres :5433): catalog 502/200, unauth 401, persona round-trip 200 (voiceModel/voiceSettings/voiceLanguage survive GET), consent-gate CRUX 403 (no consent → consent-required), consent record 200. ADR-0127: @cernia/speech typecheck=0/125 tests/build clean; @cernia/voice-agent typecheck=0/879 tests/build clean; @cernia/finops 131 tests; @cernia/api typecheck=0/P3 targeted 89+26 tests; @cernia/admin typecheck=0/1029 tests; @cernia/storage build clean.

ElevenLabs TTS FULLY VALIDATED live on the free tier — real μ-law-8000 phone audio confirmed end-to-end, AND a real telephony bug fixed. (1) Free-tier voice: researched + probed the operator's key — Library voices and OLD defaults (Rachel 21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM) return 402 paid_plan_required, but NEW default voices work free via API; verified HTTP 200 for Sarah (EXAVITQu4vr4xnSDxMaL, female), Laura, Lily, Jessica, Will (Aria is library-gated). Live test now defaults to Sarah. (2) BUG FIXED (packages/speech/src/backends/elevenlabs-tts-backend.ts): output_format was sent in the request BODY, which ElevenLabs's /v1/text-to-speech/:id/stream endpoint IGNORES — it silently returned its mp3_44100_128 default (observed: an 'ID3'-tagged MP3, head 49443304). For the telephony path that is catastrophic: Twilio Media Streams expect raw μ-law 8 kHz and would have received MP3 → broken call audio. Moved output_format to the query string; the live synth now returns real μ-law (head ff ff 7e … — 0xFF silence + speech samples, 16 KB, no ID3). Updated the 2 backend unit tests that asserted body.output_format → assert the query param + body absence (6/6 green). The live test's format guard now keys off the ID3 signature only (μ-law leading silence 0xFF would false-trip an MPEG frame-sync check). ADR-0127: @cernia/speech typecheck=0/lint=0/build=0/6 tests; @cernia/api typecheck=0, live test 2/2. This bug would have shipped silently — the gated live smoke test caught it. The agent now speaks real phone audio on the operator's free tier; production voice is selected per-tenant via voice_tenant_config.persona.

LIVE ElevenLabs TTS smoke test shipped + run against the operator's real API key (apps/api/src/elevenlabs-tts.live.test.ts). Gated like the Stripe live-sandbox test (describe.skipIf on ELEVENLABS_API_KEY); mirrors the telephony path exactly — constructs ElevenLabsTtsBackend (model eleven_flash_v2_5) over the production buildElevenLabsFetchTransportFactory() and synthesizes a short phrase at format 'mulaw-8k' (→ output_format ulaw_8000, what Twilio Media Streams require). Contract assertion: a correct key + request shape + endpoint yields EITHER real μ-law audio (usable voice + adequate plan) OR a clean HTTP 402 paid_plan_required; only 401/400/422/5xx fail (real drift). VERIFIED live (2/2 green): the operator's key AUTHENTICATES and has TTS access, and our request reaches the /v1/text-to-speech/:voiceId/stream endpoint correctly — proven by a clean 402, not a 401/400. FINDING: on a FREE ElevenLabs tier, the API rejects ALL stock/Library voices ('Free users cannot use library voices via the API') — confirmed for both the operator's chosen Library voice (g6xIsTj2HwM6VR4iXFCw) and the canonical default Rachel (21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM). So free-tier API TTS is unusable with stock voices; producing real μ-law audio needs a paid plan (any tier) or the tenant's own voice. The byte-level μ-law assertion (incl. an MP3-default guard against the /stream endpoint ignoring output_format) is dormant until then. CERNIA_ELEVENLABS_TEST_VOICE_ID env override lets CI point at a usable voice. The .env key + CERNIA_SPEECH_TTS=elevenlabs are set so the boot path builds the real backend. ADR-0127: apps/api typecheck=0; test file is eslint-ignored (test glob); pre-existing package-wide lint debt untouched.

Cross-pod persona persistence CLOSED (the last tts-backend followup). Admin PATCH /api/voice-agent/persona now persists to voice_tenant_config.persona (migration 066) via PostgresVoiceTenantResolver.getPersona/setPersona — the SAME jsonb column the voice-call-handler resolver reads in rowToConfig(), so a persona edit is durable + visible to every pod's resolve(), not process-local. This SUPERSEDES the original Organization.extension[voice-agent-persona] followup target: the resolver does not read Organization.extension, it reads voice_tenant_config.persona, so writing to Organization.extension would never reach the call path. setPersona does a targeted UPDATE (leaves brand_name / escalation_phone / dialog_policy untouched) and returns false → PATCH 409 {error:'no-voice-config'} when the tenant has no base voice config (brand_name + escalation_phone are NOT NULL, so a persona can't precede the base row). personaStore wired in apps/api/src/index.ts (the PostgresVoiceTenantResolver is passed as personaStore when db is present; in-memory Map fallback retained for no-db / tests). 5 new real-Postgres integration tests + 8 existing resolver tests pass (13/13) against cernia-f1-test-pg:5434; apps/api typecheck clean; 7 pre-existing carry-forward lint errors only (verified identical via git-stash baseline — my additions introduce zero new lint errors). Files: apps/api/src/postgres-voice-tenant-resolver.ts (getPersona/setPersona), apps/api/src/voice-agent-admin-routes.ts (exported PersonaStore interface + persona GET/PATCH rewired to opts.personaStore with Map fallback), apps/api/src/index.ts (personaStore wiring), apps/api/src/voice-agent-persona-persistence.integration.test.ts.

ra-tts-4 voiceId-routing followup closed — PerTenantTtsRouter now applies a per-tenant voice id override. On each synthesize() call, when the tenant secret resolved source='tenant', the router overrides TtsOpts.voiceId with the secret's metadata.defaultVoiceId (preferred) or vendorAccountId (fallback) before delegating to the per-tenant ElevenLabs backend; default fall-through preserves the caller's persona voiceId unchanged. The override is cached alongside the backend in CacheEntry.voiceIdOverride and refreshed on each resolve so an operator updating only the voice id (same API key) takes effect without a key rotation. 6 new unit tests (15 total): metadata.defaultVoiceId override, vendorAccountId fallback, metadata-preferred-over-vendorAccountId, no-override preserves caller voiceId, default-fallthrough preserves caller voiceId, same-key voice-id refresh. apps/api typecheck clean (3 pre-existing carry-forward errors only). Files: apps/api/src/per-tenant-tts-router.ts + .test.ts.

ra-tts-4 closed — PerTenantTtsRouter shipped in apps/api/src/per-tenant-tts-router.ts. Wraps the default TextToSpeech backend (built at boot from ELEVENLABS_API_KEY env var) with a tenant-aware adapter that consults PerTenantSpeechCredentials.resolveElevenLabs(tenantId) on each synthesize() call. When the resolver returns source='tenant' the router builds (or reuses a cached) ElevenLabsTtsBackend keyed on that tenant's API key and delegates to it; when source='platform-env' or no override exists, falls through to the boot default backend. Bounded LRU cache (default 64 tenants, eviction by lastUsedAt) prevents unbounded growth at scale. Each tenant-keyed call emits a speech.tts.started audit row with backend='elevenlabs-tenant' for cost-allocation. Boot wiring in apps/api/src/index.ts: initialiseVoiceCallStack() now accepts deps.tenantSecretResolver; when supplied AND the default tts.id is 'elevenlabs', the tts is wrapped before being passed to registerInboundCallHandler. 9 unit tests cover the full matrix: no override → default fallthrough, source='platform-env' → default fallthrough, source='tenant' → tenant backend used, cache reuse, secret rotation triggers rebuild, LRU eviction, audit double-emit suppression on default fallthrough, id+hasBaa report the default's (audit-field stability), invalidate() drops a cached entry. apps/api typecheck clean (only pre-existing carry-forward errors on admin-tenants-routes 'update' field, unchanged).

Voice persona PATCH endpoint shipped — apps/api/src/voice-agent-admin-routes.ts now exposes GET + PATCH /api/voice-agent/persona keyed by tenantId from rbacClaims, backed by an in-memory personaStore (single-pod safe). Replaces the console.log placeholder in apps/admin/src/app/admin/tenants/[id]/voice-agent/page.tsx handleSave — the persona editor's Save now round-trips the full VoiceAgentPersona shape (agentName, voiceId, voiceGender, voiceAccent, formality, warmth, pace, greeting/signoff templates) through real HTTP with the X-Cernia-Tenant header. Cross-pod followup: write through to Organization.extension[voice-agent-persona] via PgStorageAdapter so the voice-call-handler tenant resolver sees the update. apps/api typecheck clean.

ra-tts-2 closed (resolver wiring level) — PerTenantSpeechCredentials.resolveElevenLabs(tenantId) shipped in apps/api/src/per-tenant-speech-credentials.ts. Returns {plaintext, source, vendorAccountId, metadata} so vendorAccountId surfaces the tenant's default voiceId and metadata?.defaultVoiceId surfaces the persona editor's choice. ELEVENLABS_API_KEY env-var fallback automatic. Shared with ra-stt-2 — 10 unit tests cover the full matrix (per-tenant override / env-var fallback / tenant isolation / undefined-when-empty). apps/api typecheck clean. Boot-time SpeechServiceFactory swap (consume the helper in apps/api/src/index.ts so the static ELEVENLABS_API_KEY env-var read becomes per-call per-tenant resolution) is ra-tts-4's remaining work.
ElevenLabsFetchTransport native-fetch streaming adapter + apps/api boot wiring. 8 tests.

Removed 2 stale remaining bullets across stt-backend + tts-backend. Both claimed "Build packages/voice-stt-deepgram" / "Build packages/voice-tts-elevenlabs". In fact the backends shipped in a different package path: packages/speech/src/backends/deepgram-stt-backend.ts (task #96) + packages/speech/src/backends/elevenlabs-tts-backend.ts (task #97). The path-prefix mismatch made these read as un-shipped. Remaining bullets on both stages (resolver-boot wiring, nightly fixtures, voiceId PATCH) stay open - those are genuinely partial.

stages.name: Self-serve tenant provisioning
stages.rationale: A new tenant should be able to land on apps/installer, paste their org details, hook up SSO, paste their Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs keys, and have a working voice agent with no Cernia handholding.

baseline.summary: apps/installer exists as a wizard scaffold (org details, branding, persona) but the 12 config items the voice agent needs (Twilio SID + token, Deepgram key, ElevenLabs key + default voice, phone number registration, LLM provider choice, language allowlist, business hours, transfer phone numbers, cost ceiling, red-flag overrides) are partial / scattered / not wired into apps/api boot.

current.summary: Voice-config step (step 4 of 8) in apps/installer wizard collects all 12 voice-agent config items (Twilio AccountSID+AuthToken to tenant-secret store, Deepgram + ElevenLabs API keys + voice ID, LLM provider, language allowlist, business hours, escalation phone, persona greeting). Each step wired to its server-side endpoint: tenant-secret-routes (credentials), persona PATCH (tone), tenant_phone_numbers register (E.164 routing). Voice-config wizard at apps/installer/src/app/wizard/[sessionId]/voice-config + server route at apps/installer/src/app/api/voice-config/[sessionId]. Outbound smoke-test and CSV/JSON white-glove import are operator-tooling extras tracked as deferred — not blocking pilot rollout.

remaining: Deferred (post-GA enhancement, NOT a GA launch blocker — triaged 2026-06-03): Post-install outbound smoke test: place a test call to the registered number, verify the tenant-branded greeting plays. Deferred — manual verification works for the pilot; automation needs an outbound-test-harness.

Deferred (post-GA enhancement, NOT a GA launch blocker — triaged 2026-06-03): White-glove CSV/JSON import: bulk-load enterprise tenants migrating from another platform. Deferred — first migration will be hand-walked; the pattern emerges from that.

OnboardingFullProvisioner retry/partial-failure: FIXED in 1B-2 review — conflict caught, resolveExisting wired at boot (storage.search by email). resolveExisting returns null if tenant row or admin User not found (rethrows). Residual: if first attempt failed before startTrial AND resolveExisting returns null (e.g. storage down), conflict is rethrown — session stays retryable but requires manual intervention. (apps/api/src/onboarding-full-provisioner.ts, apps/api/src/index.ts)

SHIPPED (onboarding-redesign, local-held, commits 5c03bbde + 03fdf0a7) — PostgresOnboardingStore replaces InMemoryOnboardingStore: wizard session state persisted as jsonb (migration 281 onboarding_sessions_v2), lossless round-trip, idempotent UPSERT, multi-replica safe; survives API restart. The same shared instance also makes Phase 4 checkout/paid state (checkoutStatus/paidAt) durable. 5 real-PG tests. apps/api/src/postgres-onboarding-store.ts, apps/api/src/index.ts.

Deepgram no-heal: a tenant whose deepgram_project_id exists but whose tenant_secrets.deepgram is missing cannot self-heal (Deepgram has no key-retrieval endpoint) — needs a $reprovision-deepgram flow (clear deepgram_project_id to force a fresh project) before Card 5 fail-closed turns this into a hard failure for production tenants. See apps/api/src/deepgram-project-provisioner.ts ensureProject() JSDoc for the remediation procedure. Integration point: apps/api/src/tenant-external-resources-store.ts, packages/storage/src/migrations/248_tenant_external_resources_deepgram.sql.

cernia-ops isolation-posture-summary RBAC audit hardening: the global RBAC hook logs allow for a tenant-admin (tenant *:admin covers read:IsolationPostureSummary) before the handler-level isSuperAdmin check returns 403. Audit trail is misleading (allow event then 403 from handler). Harden by: (a) mapping /cernia-ops/* to a platform-only resource type not covered by tenant *:admin RoleBindings, OR (b) emitting a deny audit event from the handler before the 403 reply. See apps/api/src/isolation-posture-routes.ts handler + apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation(). Test 12 in isolation-posture-routes.test.ts proves the handler gate blocks correctly (P1-6 follow-up).

per-tenant-stt-router.ts warnSharedDeepgram: fires on every request for a production tenant using the shared Deepgram key — needs dedup (e.g. per-tenant warn-once flag or rate-limit) to avoid log spam in production. apps/api/src/per-tenant-stt-router.ts.

SHIPPED (onboarding-redesign, local-held, commit f26086f3) — POST /onboarding/:id/numbers/search now rate-limited: per-session (10/60s) + per-IP (30/60s) in-memory sliding window → 429 generic body (no enumeration); CERNIA_ONBOARDING_SEARCH_* overrides. Stops an unauth caller with a valid session id from driving Twilio AvailablePhoneNumbers API cost. 7 + 4 tests. apps/api/src/onboarding-search-rate-limit.ts, apps/api/src/onboarding-routes.ts.

P4 SHIPPED — number port-in DONE (Cards 1-6 complete 2026-06-08). Admin multi-number management surface SHIPPED 2026-06-15 (onboarding-redesign Phase 6): apps/api/src/admin-numbers-routes.ts (GET/POST $search/POST/DELETE /admin/numbers, RBAC billing.manage, tenant-scoped, CUSTOMER pricing local $10/toll-free $15, Twilio wholesale never surfaced) + apps/admin /admin/numbers page + sidebar nav. DEFERRED remaining: real Twilio pricing API call (monthlyCostCents is our customer-facing allowance price, intentionally NOT fetched from Twilio wholesale pricing); number reassign-between-tenants. Integration points: apps/api/src/admin-numbers-routes.ts, apps/api/src/telephony-store.ts.

C4 boot-wiring DONE (2026-06-09): apps/api/src/index.ts now constructs the failover dep bundle via the new apps/api/src/voice-fallback-wiring.ts (buildVoiceFallbackWiring → buildVoiceFallbackBinProvisioner() + per-tenant PostgresVoiceFallbackStore + buildNumberConfigClient()) and threads it into BOTH the provisionTenantExternalResources call (numberConfig + failover deps) AND the voiceAgentAdminRoutes registration (opts.failover). Wiring is gated to real-Twilio posture only (provision: isProd && TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID && TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN; admin: telephony.id==='twilio' && tenantSecretResolver present) so dev/stub posture leaves it unwired and the promote gate keeps blocking (safe default). Per-tenant Twilio creds reuse the existing tenant_secrets provider='twilio' resolver pattern (vendorAccountId=subaccount SID, plaintext=auth token); resolveTenantTelephony joins those creds with tenant_external_resources.twilio_phone_number_sid. A provisioned number now gets a real platform-independent VoiceFallbackUrl. REMAINING (deferred, NOT a wiring gap): existing tenants whose number was provisioned BEFORE this wiring landed have voice_fallback_url=null until they either re-run onboarding or PATCH /api/voice-agent/fallback — a one-time backfill/$reprovision-failover sweep would close them out. Integration points: apps/api/src/index.ts, apps/api/src/voice-fallback-wiring.ts.

Self-serve signup Plan 2 increment E residual — orphan provisional-tenant cleanup SWEEP SHIPPED (2026-06-14): apps/api/src/orphan-tenant-cleanup.ts (findOrphanProvisionalTenants + cleanupOrphanProvisionalTenants, dry-run-default, injected seams) deletes tenants older than a TTL (default 7d) AND invited-admin-only (no active user) AND not social-linked — the inert rows left when a reserve-on-Google-click signup is abandoned or the facility name is changed mid-signup. CLI: `pnpm --filter @cernia/api cleanup:orphan-tenants` (dry-run; `-- --apply` to delete). 8/8 fast unit tests (orphan-tenant-cleanup.unit.test.ts) + real-PG integration test (orphan-tenant-cleanup.test.ts). REMAINING: schedule the sweep (cron / ops job) — currently run-on-demand.

installer .eslintrc SHIPPED (2026-06-14): apps/installer/.eslintrc.json ({"extends":"next/core-web-vitals"}, mirrors apps/admin) — `pnpm --filter @cernia/installer lint` now runs and is clean. Closes the unsatisfiable-lint-gate gap surfaced while shipping the Plan-2 installer UI.

Verified-SMS hard gate is BUILT end-to-end and flag-gated by CERNIA_SIGNUP_REQUIRE_PHONE (2026-06-15). One switch drives BOTH server and UI so they can't drift: server email path = $complete 409 (apps/api/src/onboarding-routes.ts), server Google path = reserve-tenant 409 (same file — the Google path provisions there, before the OAuth redirect, so $complete never sees it), and the installer UI reads GET /onboarding/config → requirePhone and blocks both 'Save & continue' and 'Continue with Google' until the admin phone is SMS-verified (apps/installer/.../account/page.tsx + phone-verify.tsx). Admin phone is also a REQUIRED field in both paths (validateAccount E.164) and is stamped onto User.mfa.mfaPhone by the provisioner. REMAINING: the flag is OFF by default because Twilio SMS isn't universally deliverable until A2P 10DLC registration is live (a prod tenant whose number can't receive SMS would otherwise be stranded) — flip CERNIA_SIGNUP_REQUIRE_PHONE=1 per-environment once SMS is reliably deliverable (in dev: tester's number in COMMS_DEV_ALLOWLIST). See apps/api/.env.example.

SHIPPED (onboarding-redesign, local-held, commits 596d6d03 + 03fdf0a7) — A2P-status reconciliation closes the 10DLC go-live BLOCKER: apps/api/src/a2p-status-reconciler.ts (reconcileA2pRegistrations + scheduleA2pReconciler) runs once at boot + every A2P_RECONCILE_INTERVAL_MS (default 15m), reads pending tenant_messaging_registration rows, queries Twilio per-tenant with that tenant's subaccount creds (a2p-registrar.queryStatus: campaign->brand for 10DLC, /v1/Tollfree/Verifications for toll-free), maps APPROVED/VERIFIED->approved + FAILED/REJECTED/BLOCKED->rejected, UPDATEs changed rows, and calls smsComplianceGate.refresh() when any approve. Per-tenant error isolation; fail-safe (leaves rows pending on Twilio outage). 20 + 17 tests. REMAINING for prod: real Twilio subaccount creds (no real status without them), then flip CERNIA_ENFORCE_10DLC=1. Integration points: apps/api/src/a2p-status-reconciler.ts, apps/api/src/a2p-registrar.ts, apps/api/src/index.ts.

SHIPPED (onboarding-redesign, local-held) — checkout-first payment: gate-on-payment design keyed to the onboarding session (no tenant exists at checkout). POST /onboarding/:id/checkout builds a Stripe Checkout Session (metadata cernia_onboarding_session + cernia_cart); checkout.session.completed marks the session paid (idempotent, no tenant grant — grants stay at $complete); $complete + reserve-tenant return 402 until isPaid; 'checkout' is the first wizard step (required iff a paid product); sandbox stays trial. 149 API + 139 onboarding + 27 installer tests. Integration points: apps/api/src/onboarding-routes.ts, apps/api/src/billing-routes.ts, apps/api/src/index.ts, apps/installer wizard checkout step. REMAINING for prod: live STRIPE_* keys + webhook secret (dev uses a checkout.stripe.test noop URL).

SHIPPED (onboarding-redesign, local-held) — tenant-number SMS 10DLC gate is now enforceable: apps/api/src/sms-compliance-gate-boot.ts instantiates + feeds the TenDlcRegistry from approved tenant_messaging_registration rows and is consulted via an optional NotificationService smsPreSendHook (the chokepoint where appointment confirm/reminder SMS go FROM the tenant number; OTP/Twilio-Verify bypasses NotificationService and is exempt). Additive + FLAG-GATED (CERNIA_ENFORCE_10DLC, default OFF=advisory log), fail-OPEN on read errors. 15 + 4 tests. REMAINING for prod: flip CERNIA_ENFORCE_10DLC=1 per-env, but ONLY AFTER the A2P-status reconciliation above can mark rows 'approved' (else every tenant SMS blocks). Integration points: apps/api/src/sms-compliance-gate-boot.ts, packages/notifications/src/notification-service.ts, apps/api/src/index.ts.

SHIPPED (onboarding-redesign, local-held) — facility profile persisted + E911/A2P on every number: tenant_facility_profile (migration 280) + apps/api/src/tenant-facility-store.ts persist the validated address + business identity at provision time; POST /admin/numbers now registers E911 (always) + submits A2P (when the tenant holds an SMS product) best-effort from that profile, so a number bought AFTER onboarding is no longer compliance-orphaned. 7 + 3 + 4 tests. REMAINING for prod: real Twilio subaccount creds (E911/A2P are Noop without them). Integration points: apps/api/src/tenant-facility-store.ts, apps/api/src/onboarding-full-provisioner.ts, apps/api/src/admin-numbers-routes.ts, apps/api/src/index.ts.

SHIPPED (onboarding-redesign, local-held, commit f26086f3) — facility address is now HARD-required at submit, product-driven: a structurally-valid facilityAddress is required whenever the product mix needs a number (aggregateRequirements.voice||.sms — phone-agent/digital-assistant/patient-portal), optional for api-webhooks/sandbox. Enforced in orchestrator validateAccount (addressRequiredForSession), a $complete + reserve-tenant 422 gate, and the installer account page (which also had a bug fixed: the Google path dropped the address). onboarding 153 + API 86 + installer 30. Integration points: packages/onboarding/src/wizard-orchestrator.ts, apps/api/src/onboarding-routes.ts, apps/installer account step.

DEFERRED (onboarding-redesign) — in-app voice/SMS purchase parity: the billing grantProduct seam re-derives voice_tier on every in-app product grant (index.ts:1912-1936, DONE), but it does NOT auto-create a number-provisioning + A2P task. Needs a first-run-checklist / setup-task store (does not exist). Until then the admin self-serves a number via the new /admin/numbers surface. A guided admin first-run checklist UI (SSO/migration as professional-services tasks, port-in, team invites, EHR connect) is also deferred — voice-config + voice-policy persistence (migration 272) already shipped.

DEFERRED (onboarding-redesign) — installer design-system polish: the installer adopted the shadcn token contract + Inter + applicability-aware WizardShell, but full shadcn primitive vendoring (Button/Input/Card/Select/Label/Separator) + a BrandProvider/FacilityWordmark frame is incremental and added per-page where new steps need it, rather than a wholesale migration. apps/installer/src/components/ui/*.

history.note: Onboarding redesign — two more open items CLOSED + COMMITTED LOCALLY (held per push-on-signal), via two parallel agents. (1) FIRST-RUN GUIDED CHECKLIST (commit 0625235d) — apps/api/src/first-run-checklist.ts seeds product-driven setup tasks into tenant_setup_task at provision time (voice-config for voice tenants → /admin/voice-agent; team-invite → /admin/users + ehr-connect → /admin/integrations for any paid product; always two dismissible professional-services tasks sso-setup + data-migration), wired via an optional setupTaskStore on OnboardingFullProvisioner (best-effort, never fails provisioning, idempotent) and rendered on /admin/setup with per-type icons, a 'Go' link button, and a 'Professional services' badge. This gives the deferred SSO / data-migration / team-invites / EHR-connect items a concrete guided home (the locked design intent: post-signup PS-assisted tasks) rather than leaving them as silent gaps. 10 + 3 tests. (2) PORT-IN ONBOARDING UI (commit f94df9ff) — the number-porting BACKEND was already wired ($complete accepts accountPin → provisioner builds the port request; the orchestrator validates port-in + strips the PIN), so this added the installer UI: a 'Port an existing number' mode on the phone step collecting the existing E.164 number + losing carrier + account number + a TRANSIENT account PIN, saved (via saveStep) WITHOUT the PIN; the PIN lives only in a module-scoped wizard-client stash that survives client-nav to the done step, is sent as accountPin in the $complete body, and is consumed+cleared there — NEVER persisted to the session / localStorage / cookies / logs, and lost on reload (fail-closed for a secret we refuse to store). The just-shipped search + multi-number flows are fully preserved. 50 installer (+13) + 161 onboarding tests. INTEGRATED VERIFICATION (ADR-0127): @cernia/api typecheck clean except carry-forward marketplace-review-routes.ts:237; 60 API (first-run-checklist + onboarding-full-provisioner + setup-tasks + tenant-setup-task) + 50 @cernia/installer + 161 @cernia/onboarding + 1142 admin tests; typecheck + lint clean. With these, every Deferred row on the onboarding tracker except the intentional future PS integrations is resolved; the redesign remains code-complete with only operational go-live items left. Onboarding redesign — both DEFERRED phase items CLOSED + COMMITTED LOCALLY (held per push-on-signal). Onboarding redesign — three more parallel hardening tracks INTEGRATED + COMMITTED LOCALLY (held per push-on-signal), closing the one functional go-live BLOCKER plus two durability/hardening gaps. Three fresh-context agents ran on disjoint writable sets; the main session grafted + verified them together. (1) A2P STATUS RECONCILER (commits 596d6d03 + 03fdf0a7) — THE 10DLC GO-LIVE BLOCKER IS CLOSED: a2p-status-reconciler.ts polls Twilio per pending tenant_messaging_registration row (campaign->brand for 10DLC, /v1/Tollfree/Verifications for toll-free) using that tenant's subaccount creds, maps APPROVED/VERIFIED->approved + FAILED/REJECTED/BLOCKED->rejected, UPDATEs the row, and calls smsComplianceGate.refresh() so the gate opens for newly-approved tenants; once at boot + every A2P_RECONCILE_INTERVAL_MS (default 15m), per-tenant error isolation, fail-safe on Twilio outage. (2) POSTGRES ONBOARDING STORE (commits 5c03bbde + 03fdf0a7) — PostgresOnboardingStore persists the wizard session as jsonb (migration 281), so a partial signup survives restart + is multi-replica safe; the same shared instance also makes Phase 4 checkout/paid state durable. GO-LIVE GATE now reduces to operational items: real Twilio/USPS/Stripe credentials (E911/A2P/address-validation/checkout all run as Noop/stub in dev), enforcement-flag flips (CERNIA_ENFORCE_10DLC after real A2P approvals; CERNIA_SIGNUP_REQUIRE_PHONE), real ToS/BAA legal copy, and Phase 7 live scenario E2E. Self-serve signup — signup-phone SMS verification now has a FLIPPABLE provider (Twilio Verify | own-number OTP), and Twilio Verify is VERIFIED LIVE end-to-end (landed LOCALLY, held per push-on-signal). Problem: the only built SMS path was own-number Programmable Messaging, which needs an owned Twilio number + A2P 10DLC registration before US delivery works — so signup SMS couldn't actually be sent until A2P is approved. FIX: new apps/api/src/signup-phone-verifier.ts introduces a SignupPhoneVerifier seam with TWO providers — VerifySignupPhoneVerifier (Twilio Verify REST: /Verifications + /VerificationCheck; Twilio generates/sends/checks the code from its own managed sender, NO owned number or A2P needed — Twilio explicitly steers OTP use cases here) and OtpSignupPhoneVerifier (the existing own-number OtpService path, for when Cernia's A2P brand/campaign is approved). buildSignupPhoneVerifier() picks by env: TWILIO_VERIFY_SERVICE_SID set → Verify, cleared → own-number. THE FLIP IS ONE ENV VAR. Both providers record the SAME consumed 'signup-phone' row in auth_verifications, so the flag-gated $complete/reserve-tenant gate (otpService.hasRecentVerification) is provider-agnostic — no gate change. Routes (apps/api/src/auth-verification-routes.ts) now call the verifier for the pre-auth /api/auth/signup/phone/{start,confirm} endpoints (authed MFA routes still use OtpService directly). Dev-safety: the Verify send honours the SAME COMMS_DEV_ALLOWLIST as the OTP senders (new exported isRecipientCommsAllowed in otp-senders.ts; gateByAllowlist refactored to use it, no behavior change). index.ts extracts otpStore, builds the verifier (allowSend=isRecipientCommsAllowed), passes it to the routes, and logs the active provider. LIVE PROOF: with a real Verify service (Cernia Health Signup, VA…) configured, POST /signup/phone/start → a REAL SMS arrived on the admin's personal mobile; POST /signup/phone/confirm with the texted code → {ok:true}; auth_verifications shows the signup-phone row consumed → hasRecentVerification=true. Verify (ADR-0127): @cernia/api typecheck clean except carry-forward marketplace-review-routes.ts:237; lint clean; signup-phone-verifier unit 9/9 + auth-verification-routes 14/14 + otp-senders-allowlist 4/4. .env.example documents TWILIO_VERIFY_SERVICE_SID as the provider switch. NOTE: CERNIA_SIGNUP_REQUIRE_PHONE is still OFF (the hard gate) — can be turned on now that SMS is deliverable via Verify. Integration points: apps/api/src/signup-phone-verifier.ts, apps/api/src/auth-verification-routes.ts, apps/api/src/otp-senders.ts, apps/api/src/index.ts.

Self-serve signup — admin RoleBinding now seeded at provision time (landed LOCALLY, held per push-on-signal). GAP found during live end-to-end verification: self-serve onboarding created the Tenant + admin User but NEVER seeded a Role catalog or admin RoleBinding, so the new admin's token carried roles:[] → every tenant API call would 403 in apps/admin ('no role bindings'). A healthy tenant carries Role(tenant-admin/tenant-member/office-admin) + an admin RoleBinding; signup-created tenants had only Tenant + User. FIX: apps/api/src/fhir-storage-tenant-provisioner.ts now, right after creating the admin User, seeds buildDefaultRoles() (re-keyed to slash-free ids so the 2-part Role/{id} RBAC resolver can parse them) + a RoleBinding{principal:User/${adminUserId}, role:Role/tenant-admin, status:active}. principal is User/ (NOT Practitioner/ as dev-login uses) to match how BOTH production login paths resolve roles — builtin-user-store.ts:140 (password) + social-auth-routes.ts:653 (Google) search principal=User/${userId}. Runs once per tenant (a re-provision conflicts on the Tenant create first); per-create conflict-guard is belt-and-suspenders. Covers BOTH paths: Google (reserve-tenant → invited branch) AND email (\$complete → active branch). The TenantProvisionStorageWriter seam was widened from Tenant|User to also accept Role|RoleBinding (prod adapter already forwards to the generic storage.create). VERIFIED LIVE: a fresh Google signup now mints a token with roles:['tenant-admin'] (was roles:[] pre-fix) and the new tenant has Role(3)+RoleBinding(1) bound to the admin's exact User id. Tests: provisioner unit 15/15 (asserts the catalog + User/<id>→Role/tenant-admin binding), onboarding-full-provisioner 17/17 + onboarding-routes 63/63 (real PG). @cernia/api typecheck clean except carry-forward marketplace-review-routes.ts:237; lint clean. Integration point: apps/api/src/fhir-storage-tenant-provisioner.ts.

Self-serve signup — VERIFIED-SMS hard gate wired end-to-end, flag-gated (landed LOCALLY, held per push-on-signal). Builds on the same-day dual-path restore. Most of the gate already existed ($complete 409 + pre-auth signup-phone OTP routes + a flag-gated isPhoneVerified checker wired in index.ts only when CERNIA_SIGNUP_REQUIRE_PHONE=1); this increment closed the two gaps that made it a true gate. (1) Google path: the tenant provisions at reserve-tenant BEFORE the OAuth redirect, so $complete's gate never protected it — added the identical flag-gated phone 409 to the reserve-tenant handler (apps/api/src/onboarding-routes.ts; uses the existing opts.isPhoneVerified, so no new wiring). (2) Single source of truth for the UI: NEW GET /onboarding/config returns { requirePhone: opts.isPhoneVerified !== undefined } — derived from the SAME wired option that drives the server 409, so UI and server can never disagree (pre-auth, covered by the /onboarding/ allowlist). Installer: NEW /api/onboarding/config proxy (fail-safe requirePhone:false when API unreachable/demo) + verify-client.fetchSignupConfig(); the account step fetches it and, when true, blocks BOTH 'Save & continue' (emailSubmitBlocked includes !phoneVerified) AND 'Continue with Google' (handleGoogle rejects an unverified phone in-place rather than failing mid-redirect), and PhoneVerify drops its 'optional' copy. DESIGN: the flag stays OFF by default so signup is never blocked where SMS isn't deliverable yet (A2P 10DLC pending) — flipping it on activates the UI block AND both server 409s together. .env.example documents the single flag + its three effects. Verify (ADR-0127): @cernia/api typecheck clean except the pre-existing carry-forward marketplace-review-routes.ts:237; onboarding-routes 63/63 (RG01-03 reserve-tenant gate, CFG01-02 config, + existing PG01-03 $complete gate); @cernia/installer typecheck=0, lint=0, 17/17 (account/page +2 requirePhone tests for both paths). Integration points: apps/api/src/onboarding-routes.ts, apps/api/.env.example, apps/installer/src/app/api/onboarding/config/route.ts, apps/installer/src/lib/verify-client.ts, apps/installer/src/app/wizard/[sessionId]/account/{page,phone-verify}.tsx.

Self-serve signup — DUAL-PATH account step restored + phone-required-both-paths + viewable legal templates (landed LOCALLY, held per push-on-signal). Corrects a prior over-correction (commit e0f32ed4) that had made the account step Google-ONLY, breaking non-Google signup. (1) Dual-path account step (apps/installer/src/app/wizard/[sessionId]/account/page.tsx): a method toggle (google|email, role=radio) — Google mode disables the admin-email field ('Comes from your Google account') and shows facility+legal+phone+terms only; Email mode shows email+EmailVerify, password, an MFA-method selector + TotpEnroll (QR), and phone. Falls back to Email mode automatically when GET /api/auth/social/providers reports google:false. (2) Phone REQUIRED in BOTH paths: validateAccount now rejects a missing/non-E.164 primaryAdminPhone (packages/onboarding/src/wizard-orchestrator.ts) and the provisioner stamps it onto User.mfa.mfaPhone in both the active (password) and invited (social/reserve) branches (apps/api/src/fhir-storage-tenant-provisioner.ts). Google-path MFA = Google's own 2FA + phone on file (no separate Cernia TOTP enforced on the Google path). Legal copy is deliberately placeholder — to be finalized by the user's legal team, NOT authoritative. (3) apps/api/src/provision-tenant-external-resources.test.ts — saveAuthToken callback updated to accept the third parameter. (4) apps/api/src/per-tenant-twilio-router.test.ts (NEW) — 4 unit tests: vendorAccountId present → per-tenant backend built + correct accountSid used; vendorAccountId=null → platform-default fall-through; no secret → platform-default; platform-env secret (source='platform-env') → platform-default. (5) apps/api/src/twilio-subaccount-provisioner.test.ts — 3 updated + 2 new tests: create path asserts saveAuthToken receives (tenantId, token, sid); idempotent+existingAuthToken heals the secret; idempotent without token skips the heal.
