P4-6b cross-card integration bug fix: port-completion webhook (POST /telephony/twilio/porting, 'completed' branch) now registers the ported number in tenant_phone_numbers via new registerPortedNumber callback + deactivates the stale temp number row via unregisterTempNumber callback. Without this fix every inbound call to the ported line returned null from lookupByNumber → Reject TwiML (dead number for the tenant). Fix wired in: apps/api/src/telephony-routes.ts (TelephonyRouteOptions.registerPortedNumber + unregisterTempNumber + completed-branch calls after step (d)), apps/api/src/index.ts (boot wiring from PostgresTenantPhoneNumberStore.registerIdempotent + .deactivate — same store as the provisioner's registerNumber callback), apps/api/src/telephony-routes-porting.test.ts (13 tests: completed→ported number registered + routable, temp row deregistered; replay no double-register; failed path no register). Stale temp row cleaned via .deactivate (soft-delete, is_active=FALSE). typecheck=0, build=0, 13/13 tests pass.

stages.name: Per-tenant workflow customization
stages.rationale: Different customers want different things: SNF wants 'medication-pickup-reminder' but not 'cancel-appointment'; pain clinic wants a custom intake script; pediatric clinic wants distinct red-flag thresholds. The voice agent must be configurable per tenant — not forked per tenant.

baseline.summary: Building blocks exist: @cernia/j1b-workflow-template-registry (template per tenant), TenantPolicy.allowedActions / practitionerScope / redFlagOverrides, Organization.extension[voice-agent-persona], j2c fork-and-modify-diff, j2d AI-assisted-workflow-gen. None composed into a single configurability surface; tenant admins can't see or change any of it without engineering involvement.

current.summary: Full tenant-customization surface now binds end-to-end. TenantCustomization schema + resolver + FHIR codec in @cernia/voice-agent; PostgresTenantCustomizationStore (migration 055) wired at apps/api boot + passed to voice-agent-admin-routes (PATCH) and voice-call-handler (session start). applyTenantCustomization() runs before dialog.start; ALL three resolved fields now bind: allowedIntents narrows the policy (mandatory intents re-injected), systemPromptAugment is injected as a CLINIC INSTRUCTIONS block in the system prompt (prompt.ts buildSystemPrompt, after tone / before injection-defense), and additionalRedFlagPhrases extend the red-flag screener catalogue (red-flag-screener.ts; extend-only urgent-2h/transfer-clinician-now synthetic flags — can never narrow the safety floor). Wired via two new DialogStartOpts fields (types.ts). Eval golden 'tenant-with-customization-applied' (packages/voice-agent-evals) asserts the resolved system prompt carries CLINIC INSTRUCTIONS + the augment text, allowedIntents narrowed the allowed-tools line, and the tenant red-flag phrase short-circuits to a clinician transfer. Customer-facing guide at docs/guides/voice-agent-customization.md.

remaining: Deferred (post-GA enhancement, NOT a GA launch blocker — triaged 2026-06-03): Intent Handler Overrides (advanced): per-intent backend URL override so a tenant can replace the platform handler for a specific intent with their own webhook. Deferred — out of scope for the pilot.

history.note: Residual #1: the D2 SCOPE BOUNDARY CLOSED. A REAL, working FHIR-REST EHR connector is now wired as the RecordGateway's ehrConnectorResolver, so ehr-passthrough / hybrid-bidirectional tenants with a configured Connection sync LIVE (the engine was adapter-agnostic + config-driven since 2026-06-13 but had NO connector, so every EHR-routed op threw NotImplementedError). NEW apps/api/src/http-fhir-ehr-connector.ts: HttpFhirEhrConnector implements the generic EhrConnector with genuine HTTP fetches. read GET {base}/{type}/{id} (undefined on 404); search GET {base}/{type} with query string (unwraps the searchset Bundle); create POST; update PUT {base}/{type}/{id}; delete DELETE; Accept/Content-Type application/fhir+json; non-2xx (except read-404) throws a PHI-free EhrConnectorHttpError (status + verb + resourceType only, never id/body/token). Pluggable EhrAuth: none, bearer, or smart-backend-services (signed-JWT client-assertion via jose to a client_credentials POST, cached bearer until near expiry; token acquisition injectable for tests). NEW apps/api/src/connection-ehr-connector-resolver.ts: ConnectionEhrConnectorResolver builds it from the tenant's active outbound/bidirectional fhir-r4-client Connection (protocol.baseUrl + protocol.auth ConnectionAuth mapped to EhrAuth, secretRefs resolved via the SAME tenant_secrets/KMS TenantSecretResolver seam, scoped to the tenant per ADR 0004); no such Connection throws so the gateway emits NotImplementedError (preserves the T7.1 sync-inactive guardrail; never a silent Cernia fallback). Wired buildEhrConnectorResolver into RecordGatewayRouter at apps/api/src/index.ts (ehrConnectorResolver now SET; Mode A cernia default never consults it, DEFAULT PATH UNAFFECTED). Tests: http-fhir-ehr-connector.test.ts (16 cases) + connection-ehr-connector-resolver.test.ts (8 cases) = 24/24 green. LIVE round-trip http-fhir-ehr-connector.live.test.ts against the running Cernia API own FHIR server (http://localhost:8090/fhir, /dev/login tenant-admin bearer): create Patient 201 with id + versionId 1, read same, search by family 1 match, update gender versionId 2, delete, read-after-delete undefined(404). @cernia/api typecheck=0; new files lint-clean + no require( (boot landmine); ZERO new lint errors vs git-stash baseline (all carry-forward in untouched voice/webrtc files). API boots clean on :8091. PROD Epic/Cerner = a fhir-r4-client Connection pointed at their SMART base URL + client creds (CONFIG, NOT new code); Epic-specific quirks stay with the existing scheduling-only EpicFhirR4ClientAdapter.

Deferred D1 (Release-1 B3-followup) CLOSED — billing now flips the voice tier. Release 1 shipped the voice-tier MECHANISM (voice_tier column, allowedActionsForTier, resolveAllowedActions override-ceiling) but NOTHING flipped voice_tier on an entitlement change, so a tenant who bought Pro stayed stuck on the Starter ceiling. Wired the missing write path: (1) NEW apps/api/src/postgres-voice-tenant-resolver.ts setVoiceTier(tenantId, tier) — upserts voice_tenant_config.voice_tier, materialising a minimal placeholder row (Unconfigured / +10000000000, satisfies the NOT-NULL + E.164 CHECK) when the tenant has no config yet, and ON CONFLICT touches ONLY voice_tier (never clobbers brand_name/escalation_phone). (2) NEW apps/api/src/voice-tier-sync.ts — single source-of-truth helper syncVoiceTierFromEntitlements(db, tenantId): re-derives the tier from the tenant's CURRENT entitlements (Pro product `digital-assistant` active → 'pro', else 'starter') and writes it, so it's correct regardless of which product changed. (3) Hooked at BOTH entitlement-change seams: the Stripe checkout product-grant fan-out (apps/api/src/index.ts productCatalog.grantProduct adapter, after grantProduct) and the onboarding provisioner (apps/api/src/onboarding-full-provisioner.ts, after its grant loop). Both call the same helper; sync failure is non-fatal (entitlement grant is authoritative, tier is a derived projection). (4) Exported resolveAllowedActions from @cernia/voice-agent index so the policy-build resolver is reusable. End-to-end integration test (apps/api/src/voice-tier-sync.test.ts, 6 tests, real Postgres :5433) proves the FULL chain entitlement → voice_tier → resolveAllowedActions: grant Pro → voice_tier='pro' AND resolved allowedActions gains pay-balance/verify-insurance/list-invoices; revoke → 'starter' AND those Pro intents drop; placeholder-row creation; existing-config preservation. Also fixed a baseline bug: apps/api/src/postgres-voice-tenant-resolver.test.ts didn't load migration 262 so its resolve() reads (which SELECT voice_tier) errored — 9 pre-existing failures now 11/11 green. ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, @cernia/voice-agent typecheck=0 + build=0, voice-tier-sync.ts lint-clean (the lone line-66 lint error in postgres-voice-tenant-resolver.ts is pre-existing/carry-forward, verified via git-stash baseline), no require() in any changed file, API boots clean on :8090 (full module graph loads; only failed on port-bind because a live instance held :8090).

Release 2 (FHIR Console) — per-tenant EHR RECORD-MODE configuration surfaced end-to-end (tenant self-serve over the existing RecordGateway). (1) NEW HTTP route GET/PUT /admin/record-mode over the existing RecordGateway, with an RBAC deriveOperation mapping so a tenant-admin can read + set their own record mode (apps/api/src/tenant-record-mode-routes.ts, commit 860a7422). (2) Migration 264 adds conflict_strategy + per-resource sync_resources columns to the tenant_record_mode table, and a tenant-admin Integrations UI at /admin/settings/integrations lets the tenant configure them (packages/storage/src/migrations/264_record_mode_sync_config.sql + apps/admin/src/app/admin/settings/integrations/ — page.tsx + integrations-api.ts, commit 9f70c8cc). Triager note: recorded under tenant-customization (per-tenant config of an external integration) — the closest existing stage; this extends the per-tenant configurability surface to EHR sync settings. Does not touch the stage's lone remaining 'Intent Handler Overrides' deferral (unrelated voice-agent item).

Deferred D2 (Release-2 record-mode) CLOSED at the ENGINE level — generalized, config-driven RecordGateway sync engine + conflict strategy. Release 2 shipped the record-mode CONFIG (migration 264: mode + conflict_strategy + per-resource sync_resources) and the admin UI, but the EXECUTION was partial: apps/api/src/record-gateway.ts (RecordGatewayRouter) only routed Appointment-create + Slot-search to the EHR and threw NotImplementedError for every other resource/op. Built the generalized engine: (1) RecordGatewayRouter now resolves the FULL config (new RecordConfigResolver.resolveConfig — same TenantRecordModeStore instance) and routes EACH FHIR op (read/readVersion/historyList/create/update/delete/search) per resource type via routeFor(): mode 'cernia' or a cernia-only / unconfigured type → local verbatim (UNCHANGED default — the vast majority of tenants); read-only → reads/searches pull from the EHR connector, writes stay local; bidirectional → reads serve local with EHR fallback, writes go local AND push to the EHR; ehr-passthrough → ops go to the EHR (Cernia keeps only audit). Appointment/Slot are now just the scheduling special-case preserved behind the existing ehrSchedulingResolver seam (not hardcoded mode branches). (2) Conflict strategy on divergent bidirectional writes is REAL + tested for all three: ehr-wins (EHR authoritative, local reconciled to match, EHR not overwritten), cernia-wins (local pushed over EHR), manual (new RecordConflictError thrown, NEITHER side overwritten). (3) NEW generic EhrConnector interface (read/search/create/update/delete by resourceType) — the widened, vendor-agnostic generalization of the scheduling-only appointmentWrite/slotSearch seam; resolved per-tenant via new EhrConnectorResolver (ADR 0004 single-point-of-selection). Tests use an in-memory fake EHR. (4) Wired configResolver: recordModeStore at apps/api/src/index.ts so per-resource routing is live in prod. Tests: apps/api/src/record-gateway-sync.test.ts (14 NEW: cernia-only/unconfigured stays local + EHR untouched; read-only pulls reads from fake EHR + write stays local; bidirectional writes both sides; each conflict_strategy on a divergent fake-EHR copy; ehr-passthrough goes to the EHR) + record-gateway.test.ts updated for the generalized contract (34) + ehr-scheduling-resolver.test.ts (7) = 55/55 green. @cernia/api typecheck=0; record-gateway.ts lint-clean + no require(); the lone tenant-record-mode-store.ts line-397 lint error is pre-existing/carry-forward (verified git-stash baseline). API boots clean on :8091 ('Cernia FHIR server is ready', no NotImplementedError at boot); cernia-mode (default) Patient search=200 + create=201 through the gateway — DEFAULT PATH UNAFFECTED. SCOPE BOUNDARY: the REAL Epic/Cerner SMART-on-FHIR adapter implementing EhrConnector against a live FHIR endpoint is the remaining production wiring and is intentionally NOT set (ehrConnectorResolver unset) — until it lands, any resource configured to route to the EHR raises NotImplementedError rather than silently using Cernia. The engine itself is adapter-agnostic + config-driven.

Release 1 (Voice Revenue) — tenant-customization allowedActions is now tier-ceilinged (ADR 0035 entitlement gate). New voice_tier column (packages/storage/src/migrations/262_voice_tier.sql) drives a hard paywall ceiling in packages/voice-agent/src/voice-tier.ts + tenant-customization.ts: a tenant's resolved allowedActions can never exceed its tier ceiling, so a Starter tenant cannot self-grant Pro-tier actions via the customization surface. Commits 2849251a, d758ef52, 2e91b5cc, 20c9b73e. This extends the existing per-tenant customization binding (allowedIntents/allowedActions) with a tier-enforced upper bound.

Shipped InMemoryTenantCustomizationStore + wired single instance into voice-agent-admin-routes + voice-call-handler. applyTenantCustomization called at session start; allowedIntents override now reaches the dialog policy. Customer-facing customization guide authored.

Closed the last two tenant-customization deferrals (blockers 8→6). Threaded systemPromptAugment + additionalRedFlagPhrases through new DialogStartOpts fields (packages/voice-agent/src/types.ts) → buildSystemPrompt CLINIC INSTRUCTIONS block (prompt.ts) + red-flag screener additionalPhrases (red-flag-screener.ts, extend-only synthetic urgent-2h flags); captured immutably at dialog start (dialog.ts) and wired in apps/api/src/voice-call-handler.ts. Satisfied the prior TDD-red spec in tenant-customization.test.ts (CLINIC INSTRUCTIONS: header). Added eval golden tenant-with-customization-applied.json + runner systemPrompts capture + 3 dedicated harness assertions (packages/voice-agent-evals). Verified: voice-agent typecheck/build clean, 179 tests in the 4 touched test files green; evals 28 green; @cernia/api typecheck clean. Remaining tenant-customization 'deferred' is only the advanced Intent Handler Overrides (out of pilot scope).

RESOLVED the 'persist via Organization.extension' deferral — tenant customization is now durably Postgres-persisted. PostgresTenantCustomizationStore (apps/api/src/tenant-customization-store.ts, backed by migration 055_tenant_customizations) is wired at apps/api/src/index.ts:3236 (db present → Postgres; InMemory only as the no-db fallback) and passed to BOTH voice-agent-admin-routes (PATCH) and voice-call-handler (session start). Customization now survives apps/api restart + is multi-pod safe, so the Organization.extension FHIR-codec alternative is superseded (same resolution shape as the cross-pod persona fix). Verified by boot-wiring inspection; the bullet's 'in-memory, restart wipes it' claim was stale.

Triaged 1 deferred[] item(s) out of blocker severity: each is a post-GA enhancement, not a GA launch blocker, so moved to remaining[] (tracked tech-debt) per the deferred-items audit forcing-function. No code change — these features were always future scope.

Track created from the per-tenant architecture decision (lock: per-tenant credentials from day one, self-serve installer, External Integrations track now / Tenant Provisioning track expanded inside it).

End-to-end foundation: migration 046, TenantSecretResolver + Noop/AesGcm ciphers + InMemoryStore in @cernia/core, KyselyTenantSecretStore in @cernia/storage, Fastify routes (GET list / PUT upsert / DELETE clear) with cross-tenant 403 guard + audit, boot wire at slot 10i, admin Credentials & Integrations sub-page with cards for Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/OpenAI/Anthropic, sub-page nav link from the persona editor. 20 core unit tests + storage pg-mem contract test green. Remaining: Postgres swap, AES-GCM-required boot gate, KMS key rotation runbook.

TenantCustomization schema + applyTenantCustomization resolver + FHIR codec in @cernia/voice-agent. buildSystemPrompt systemPromptAugment injection (CLINIC INSTRUCTIONS block, after tone, before injection-defense). 24 unit tests. Admin UI /admin/voice-agent/customization (allowed intents, prompt augment, workflow templates, red-flag phrases, business hours). Admin API client getCustomization + updateCustomization. Remaining: apps/api endpoint mount + dialog-engine wiring + eval golden.

Final dialog-engine wiring + eval golden — the two remaining tenant-customization deferrals. systemPromptAugment + additionalRedFlagPhrases now thread through new DialogStartOpts fields into buildSystemPrompt (CLINIC INSTRUCTIONS) + the red-flag screener (extend-only synthetic urgent-2h flags); wired in voice-call-handler. Eval golden tenant-with-customization-applied added with system-prompt capture in the runner. Satisfied the prior TDD-red spec. typecheck/build/lint(of touched files) clean; 179 voice-agent + 28 evals tests green.

tracks.name: Sample-data elimination ("no mock data")
tracks.description: User-driven mandate (2026-05-25): every admin UI page must fetch from a real apps/api endpoint; the 30 sample-*.ts files under apps/admin/src/app/_data/ must be deleted. Tracking per-domain progress phase-by-phase. Full audit: docs/status/sample-data-elimination-plan.md.

stages.name: Phase 1 — Real backend infrastructure
stages.rationale: Postgres + MinIO + Redis + Mailpit + NATS in Docker, apps/api boot against real DB, dev-seed produces one real tenant + users + FHIR resources, all 4 Next.js apps running natively. Required for any swap to be testable end-to-end.

baseline.summary: Apps had never been started against a real backend on this machine. Postgres data dir on OneDrive caused fsync hangs; a native PG service was bound to host:5432 colliding with the Docker stack; admin tenant routes weren't wired; api Dockerfile didn't copy scripts/ (postinstall fail); storage migrate.js couldn't find src/migrations/ (tsc doesn't copy .sql); connection-routes.ts double-registered POST /Connection over @cernia/server's generic FHIR CRUD; 122 pnpm-store packages were partially extracted.

current.summary: Full stack up: Postgres 16 on :5433 (production durability flags), MinIO :9000/:9001, Redis, Mailpit, NATS. 40 migrations applied. tenant-acme seeded (20 FHIR resources). apps/api on :8080 with real RSA jwks. Admin :3000, trust :3001, patient :3002, installer :3003 all return HTTP 200. Fixes: docker-compose.override.yml remaps PG to 5433, scripts/ copied into api Dockerfile, migrate.js resolver tries dist/migrations then src/migrations fallback, connection-routes.ts removed duplicate POST /Connection (keeps $run), fix-pnpm-store.mjs repaired 122 broken packages.

history.note: 1A-1 (§11.2 #9): GET /health (liveness) + GET /ready (readiness: DB reachable + migrations applied) added to apps/api. Plugin: apps/api/src/health-routes.ts. Registered in apps/api/src/index.ts after authMeRoutes. Both paths were already in permitAllForUnauthenticatedRoutes so no allowlist change needed. 3 unit tests (vitest, real Postgres :5433). E2E probe spec written at tests/e2e/specs/health-probes.spec.ts (requires live stack on :8080 to run).

stages.name: Phase 2 — Per-domain audit
stages.rationale: Before swapping anything, know which of the 30 sample-*.ts files map to which admin pages and which existing apps/api routes can be reused vs need to be built. Without this we'd thrash randomly across domains.

baseline.summary: No catalog existed; ad-hoc grep was the only way to know which page consumed which sample file.

current.summary: docs/status/sample-data-elimination-plan.md produced. 30 files cataloged: 0 wired, 11 partial (apps/api route exists, UI not refactored), 12 need-route (no route exists), 7 deferrable (work-app-only, no admin consumer). Effort estimate: ~50 work-days across the admin-facing surface. Phased execution plan (Phase 3.1 quick wins → Phase 3.6 specialized domains).

stages.name: Phase 3.1 — Quick wins (voice-budget, voice-improvements, questionnaires)
stages.rationale: All three have an apps/api route already shipped; only the UI fetcher needs to be swapped. ~3 admin pages total. Fastest measurable progress.

baseline.summary: All three pages render from sample-*.ts despite their backend routes existing.

current.summary: Done. 3 sample files deleted, 4 admin pages swapped (voice-budget + settings + voice-agent improvements + forms/[taskId]). New typed clients in /billing/voice-budget/api.ts + /voice-agent/improvements/api.ts. All HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean.

stages.name: Phase 3.2 — Core SaaS layer (tenants, iam, billing)
stages.rationale: Foundational. sample-tenants.ts feeds 7 admin pages and sample-iam.ts feeds 12 — together they unblock the majority of the admin surface. Billing is revenue-critical and has a route ready.

baseline.summary: Tenant list, detail, branding, transitions, voice-agent persona, voice-agent credentials all key off SAMPLE_TENANTS today. /admin/users/* keys off SAMPLE_IAM. Billing pages key off sample-billing.ts.

current.summary: cernia-ops portal de-mock COMPLETE — every operator surface renders real, super-admin-gated, PHI-safe data; anti-sample-import guard fully strict; sample-data.ts is test-only.

remaining: Webhook registration (POST /admin/webhooks) is now PRODUCT-gated behind the developer_portal entitlement via the wired createRequireProduct preHandler (Release 3 / T6). Remaining refinement: map the developer_portal product onto the saas-platform tier matrix (@cernia/saas-platform) so the entitlement is auto-granted by Growth+ tier rather than only via an explicit product grant; today entitlement is per-tenant product-grant driven.

history.note: Followup close — admin tenant overview metrics. New apps/api/src/admin-tenant-metrics-routes.ts exposes GET /admin/tenants/:id/metrics, registered in apps/api/src/index.ts §11d.ter immediately after cerniaOpsRoutes. Reuses the same cerniaOpsTenantStore (PostgresTenantSummaryStore on migration 062) so admin overview + cernia-ops dashboard never disagree. Tenant-admin role gate + path-vs-token tenant-id match (cross-tenant attempts get 404, not 403, to avoid existence-leak). Returns {tenantId, userCount, adminUserCount, monthlyApiCalls=apiRequests24h*30, apiRequests24h, apiErrors24h, refreshedAt}. apps/admin/src/app/admin/tenants/tenants-api.ts gained TenantMetricsWire + fetchTenantMetrics. apps/admin/src/app/admin/tenants/[id]/page.tsx OverviewTab deletes the TODO(metrics) placeholder and the 'reads 0' disclaimer; fetches metrics on mount; renders '…' during load and '—' on error rather than fabricating zeros; surfaces refreshedAt + a 'newly-provisioned' hint when the row is empty. Honest stub-followup: tenant_summary is refreshed nightly (≤24h lag); resourceCount is replaced with adminUserCount (no FHIR-resource COUNT column on tenant_summary by design). apps/api typecheck clean; apps/admin typecheck shows only pre-existing carry-forward errors (wave7.test.ts → deleted sample-*.ts + instrumentation.ts missing @cernia/observability + @sentry/nextjs).

cernia-ops increment 4a: overview/all-tenants/tenant-detail/platform-health/voice-agents views de-mocked — now fetch real /cernia-ops/* routes via shared ops-fetch (fetchOpsJson, credentials:include) + OpsAsync, render loading/error/empty/data, no SAMPLE_* fallback; dropped fabricated health sub-fields (redisHitRate string, fake P95 latency, fake service-dependencies); added dev:seed-ops-demo + anti-sample-import guard (apps/cernia-ops/src/lib/no-sample-data-in-app.test.ts, shrinking allowlist). E2E spec (tests/e2e/specs/cernia-ops.spec.ts) updated: removed assertions on SAMPLE_* content (Acme Health sentinel, tenant-acme route, hardcoded metric strings inside OpsAsync), replaced with: heading-always-visible assertions + settled-state assertions (ops-async-error or ops-async-loading or data table) + explicit SAMPLE_SENTINEL absence check. Browser operator-cookie auth not yet wired in E2E (needs context.addCookies after minting cernia-super-admin token + dev:seed-ops-demo call — documented in spec header). Remaining: incidents/quotas/escalations/$impersonate/settings-internal pages (increments 4b-4e, tracked in apps/cernia-ops/src/lib/no-sample-data-in-app.test.ts allowlist).

cernia-ops increment 4b: escalations view de-mocked. New GET /cernia-ops/customers/escalations aggregate over support_tickets + sla_tier_assignments (apps/api/src/cernia-ops-escalations-routes.ts) — super-admin gated + sendPhiSafe; selects ONLY non-PHI columns (id/tenant/status/priority->severity/category/product_area/age/slaStatus), NEVER subject/description/requester_email/body (anti-leak test ESC06). Page apps/cernia-ops/.../customers/escalations/page.tsx now fetches via OpsAsync (no SAMPLE_ESCALATIONS); dev-seed-ops-demo seeds support_tickets + sla_tier_assignments. Removed from the no-sample-data-in-app guard allowlist (3 pages left: incidents/quotas/$impersonate). Remaining de-mock: 4c quotas, 4d incidents, 4e settings-internal + $impersonate.

cernia-ops increment 4c: quotas view de-mocked. New GET /cernia-ops/platform/quotas (apps/api/src/cernia-ops-quotas-routes.ts) — super-admin + sendPhiSafe; per-tenant usage (usage_records sum over trailing 24h) vs tier ceiling (subscription_tiers.body->includedQuotas perMonth, joined via subscriptions.tier_name); status ok/warn/over/untracked. Page apps/cernia-ops/.../platform/quotas/page.tsx now fetches via OpsAsync (no SAMPLE_TENANTS / quotaForTier). dev-seed-ops-demo seeds subscription_tiers + subscriptions + usage_records. Guard allowlist down to 2 (incidents, $impersonate). Remaining: 4d incidents (derive+pin), 4e settings-internal + $impersonate.

cernia-ops increment 4d: incidents view de-mocked (the heaviest). New cernia_ops_incidents table (migration 260) + apps/api/src/cernia-ops-incidents-routes.ts: GET merges derive-on-read signals (alerts row-presence + vendor_health_state status!=operational) with operator-PINNED records (a pinned row whose derived_key matches a derived signal suppresses the twin) + POST pin/create + PATCH annotate/resolve; super-admin + sendPhiSafe; response field operatorNote (note is a PHI forbidden-key). Page apps/cernia-ops/.../platform/incidents/page.tsx fetches the merged view via OpsAsync with Pin (derived) / Resolve (pinned) actions (ops-fetch extended for POST/PATCH). dev-seed-ops-demo seeds a degraded vendor + an alert + a pinned incident. Guard allowlist down to 1 ($impersonate). Remaining: 4e settings-internal + $impersonate, then sample-data.ts is deleted + the guard goes fully strict.

cernia-ops de-mock COMPLETE (increments 4a-4e). 4e: settings-internal wired to cernia_super_admins (GET /cernia-ops/settings/internal/super-admins, operatorEmail rename + phi-blocker ALLOWED_VALUE_KEYS hardened to a direct-string-only scope w/ 6 key-scoping tests); tenants/[id]/$impersonate page fetches the real tenant-detail route (reuses 4a fetchTenantDetail). The anti-sample-import guard (no-sample-data-in-app.test.ts) is now FULLY STRICT (empty allowlist) — NO app/** file imports sample-data; sample-data.ts retained for test fixtures only (sample-data.test.ts contract checks + voice-agents sentinel tests). All 10 cernia-ops surfaces now render real super-admin-gated PHI-safe data: overview/all-tenants/tenant-detail/platform-health/voice (4a), escalations (4b), quotas (4c), incidents derive+pin (4d), settings-internal + impersonate (4e).

Deferred D3a (backend SSO mechanism) — the cernia-ops portal now has a PRODUCTION cookie session login, closing the 4a gap ('Browser operator-cookie auth not yet wired' — the API read tokens ONLY from Authorization: Bearer while cernia-ops pages call with credentials:'include'). (1) Cookie-token fallback: registered @fastify/cookie (apps/api/src/index.ts step 7.0b) and extended principalResolver (apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts) so that when NO Authorization: Bearer header is present it falls back to the HttpOnly cernia_ops_session cookie and verifies it via the SAME jwtIssuer — Bearer ALWAYS wins when present, so admin/patient/API auth is byte-unchanged (all 27 iam-route-guards tests stay green). (2) CSRF double-submit: alongside the HttpOnly session cookie a NON-HttpOnly cernia_ops_csrf cookie is set; the global RBAC hook now requires X-Cernia-Ops-CSRF === the cookie for COOKIE-authenticated POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE (403 csrf-failed on mismatch); Bearer-auth requests carry no cookie and are exempt; GET/HEAD exempt. (3) Login/logout: apps/api/src/cernia-ops-session-routes.ts — POST /cernia-ops/auth/login reuses the EXISTING BuiltInIdentityProvider credential check (same provider as /api/auth/login) and REQUIRES the cernia-super-admin role (403 if authenticated-but-not-operator, 401 on bad creds), sets the HttpOnly session + CSRF cookies (SameSite=Strict, Path=/, Secure in prod, Max-Age = JWT lifetime) and returns {ok:true} (token NEVER in body); a CERNIA_DEV_LOGIN-gated dev path mints a super-admin session cookie for local/E2E; POST /cernia-ops/auth/logout clears both cookies. Both routes allow-listed in permitAllForUnauthenticatedRoutes (CSRF + auth exempt). Tests: 11 in cernia-ops-session-routes.test.ts + 5 cookie/CSRF cases added to iam-route-guards.test.ts (38 total green). Verified live on :8090: POST /cernia-ops/auth/login {dev:true} -> 200 + Set-Cookie: cernia_ops_session (HttpOnly); cookie-only GET /cernia-ops/platform/health -> 200; cookie POST without CSRF -> 403 csrf-failed, with matching header -> passes. @cernia/api typecheck=0, changed-source lint=0. PROD-PATH GAP: an operator whose account has MFA enabled cannot complete this single-step login (authenticate returns mfa-required -> 401); MFA operators need the step-up flow or non-interactive-MFA provisioning on this surface. Part 2 (D3b — cernia-ops login UI + Playwright) still pending. Files: apps/api/src/cernia-ops-session-routes.ts, apps/api/src/cernia-ops-session-routes.test.ts, apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts, apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.test.ts, apps/api/src/index.ts, apps/api/package.json.

Release 3 / T2 — self-serve API-key auth WIRED into the request path (the complete-but-unwired ApiKey system is now live). (1) Token-format reconciliation: apps/api/src/admin-api-key-routes.ts buildSecret() now mints the issuer's cernia_pk_<12-char base32 prefix>_<32-char base32 secret> format (was the divergent cernia_pk_<64 hex>, no separator, whole-secret hash) and stores secretPrefix=cernia_pk_<prefix> + secretHash=hashPassword(inlineSecret only), so a $generate-secret key now ROUND-TRIPS through DefaultApiKeyVerifier (proven by admin-api-key-routes.test.ts test 2). (2) Cross-tenant lookup: NEW apps/api/src/api-key-lookup.ts PgApiKeyLookup.byPrefix() queries the live `resources` table on the STORED generated column apikey_secret_prefix with NO tenant filter (the token is the sole credential; tenant is derived AFTER the match) — safe because secretPrefix has 60 bits of entropy + a platform-wide uniqueness constraint and ApiKey is a credential resource, NOT PHI (CLAUDE.md invariant 5). recordUsage() writes lastUsedAt by id, best-effort/non-fatal. (3) principalResolver branch: apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts PrincipalResolverDeps gains an optional apiKeyVerifier; a Bearer/cookie token starting with cernia_pk_ is verified as an API key (cross-tenant prefix lookup + constant-time scrypt verifyPassword + status/expiry gates), the tenant is read from the key's urn:cernia:tenant meta.tag, and the principal is built via the SAME resolvePrincipalFromClaims so RBAC/RoleBindings apply identically (authVia='api-key'). A cernia_pk_ token NEVER falls through to the JWT verifier: null verify → 401, no verifier wired → 401. The JWT Bearer path is byte-unchanged (regression test A4). (4) $revoke + $rotate: apps/api/src/admin-api-key-routes.ts adds tenant-admin-gated POST /admin/api-keys/:id/$revoke (status=revoked + revokedBy/revokedAt, idempotent, cross-tenant id → 404) and POST /admin/api-keys/:id/$rotate (mints a new verifiable secret for the same principal/name, revokes the old with reason='rotated', returns the new secret once with no-store headers). Boot wiring: apps/api/src/index.ts constructs DefaultApiKeyVerifier(PgApiKeyLookup(db)) injected into registerRbacHook so EVERY authed route honors API keys, and passes the tenant-scoped storage to adminApiKeyRoutes for revoke/rotate. Tests: NEW api-key-lookup.test.ts (6, real Postgres :5433 via createMigratedDatabaseFixture — cross-tenant byPrefix, unknown/soft-deleted→null, recordUsage), NEW admin-api-key-routes.test.ts (8 — format-reconciliation round-trip, revoke idempotent + revoked-key-no-longer-verifies, rotate, non-admin 403 / cross-tenant 404), + 6 API-key-branch cases in iam-route-guards.test.ts (seeded-RoleBinding authenticates with right roles, revoked→401, expired→401, JWT regression, no-verifier→401, unknown-prefix→401). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0; the 47 tests across the 3 files (33 iam + 8 admin + 6 lookup) all green; changed source files lint-clean (package-wide 314 pre-existing baseline lint errors in unrelated files are carry-forward, unchanged). FINDING: the dev `cernia` DB on :5433 holds 9 ApiKey rows all in the OLD cernia_pk_<8 hex> format (e.g. cernia_pk_9b42e3a9, no _ separator) — these dev artifacts stop authenticating under the reconciled verifier (their plaintext was never recoverable); no key migration needed on a pre-launch system. Files: apps/api/src/admin-api-key-routes.ts, apps/api/src/api-key-lookup.ts, apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts, apps/api/src/index.ts + the 3 colocated *.test.ts.

Release 3 / T2 follow-up — three live-path bugs the T2 unit suite missed (it mocked RBAC + seeded the tenant tag) FIXED so self-serve API keys actually work end-to-end against the running stack. Bug A (RBAC fail-closed 403): deriveOperation (apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts) had NO mapping for /admin/api-keys/* custom-verb routes, so the global RBAC hook fell through to the {*,*} catch-all and 403'd a tenant-admin BEFORE the route's own isAdmin check ran. Added 4 mappings → write:ApiKey: $generate-secret, $issue, :id/$revoke, :id/$rotate. Bug B (UI-created keys never authenticate, 401): the admin UI creates ApiKeys by POSTing the resource, but storage scopes tenancy by COLUMN and does NOT stamp meta.tag urn:cernia:tenant, so the verifier failed closed ('API key is not tenant-scoped'). NEW server-side POST /admin/api-keys/$issue (apps/api/src/admin-api-key-routes.ts) issues via DefaultApiKeyIssuer, which stamps the tag from the AUTHED principal's tenantId (SERVER-AUTHORITATIVE — never client body); defaults the key principal to the caller so it inherits the caller's RoleBindings; tolerates empty/{} bodies; returns {secret, apiKey} once with no-store headers. Bug C (FHIR auth layer rejects api keys): T2 wired apiKeyVerifier into registerRbacHook ONLY, but @cernia/server's FHIR routes (GET /ApiKey, /Patient, …) have a SEPARATE auth preHandler (createAuthMiddleware → identityProvider.authenticate) that only knew JWTs — so an api key got 'Token malformed' (401) on any FHIR route even after passing the RBAC hook. FIXED by composing the FHIR-router identityProvider in apps/api/src/index.ts (step 6b): a cernia_pk_ bearer is verified as an api key (tenant from meta.tag, fail-closed if absent), any other token delegates UNCHANGED to the JWT provider; one shared DefaultApiKeyVerifier backs BOTH the FHIR layer and the RBAC hook. NEW apps/api/src/api-key-live-flow.test.ts: real createServer + real registerRbacHook + real PgStorageAdapter on :5433, asserts dev-login → $issue (200, tag stamped) → authenticate a REAL FHIR route (GET /ApiKey, 200) → $revoke (200) → same secret rejected (401), plus non-admin $issue → 403. Each of the three bugs was reproduced (test fails) then fixed (test passes). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0; api-key-live-flow + iam-route-guards + admin-api-key-routes = 43 tests green; zero NEW lint errors (64 pre-existing carry-forward in unrelated files). PROVEN LIVE on a fresh isolated boot (:8097, same :5433 DB the running stack uses): POST /dev/login → POST /admin/api-keys/$issue (200, meta.tag=urn:cernia:tenant) → GET /ApiKey with the issued secret → auth-with-issued-key=200; after $revoke → 401. Files: apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts (deriveOperation only), apps/api/src/admin-api-key-routes.ts, apps/api/src/index.ts, apps/api/src/api-key-live-flow.test.ts.

Release 3 / T3 — tenant self-serve WEBHOOKS shipped by EXTENDING the existing SubscriptionEngine rest-hook engine (no second delivery path). A tenant-admin registers an HMAC-SIGNED FhirSubscription rest-hook and receives POSTs when matching resources are written. (1) Signing (security): packages/subscription/src/{types.ts,dispatcher.ts} gained an OPTIONAL rest-hook channel field `signingSecret` (additive — existing Workflow/Connection callers unaffected); when set, dispatchRestHook adds `X-Cernia-Signature: sha256=<hex HMAC-SHA256(secret, rawBody)>` over the EXACT bytes POSTed (body serialized ONCE so the tenant can recompute). (2) Secret storage — approach (a), REUSE the encrypted tenant-secret store: apps/api/src/webhook-secret.ts stores each webhook's secret in tenant_secrets (migration 046) under synthetic provider key `webhook:<subscriptionId>` via TenantSecretResolver (AES-256-GCM in prod, Noop in test); encrypted at rest, tenant-isolated by the (tenant_id,provider) PK, generated server-side, returned ONCE at registration with Cache-Control:no-store, NEVER written to the FhirSubscription body (so it cannot leak via a FHIR read). get() accepts ONLY a tenant-sourced row (never the platform-env fallback — that would be a forgeable shared secret). Rejected (b) HMAC(masterKey,subId): one compromise forges every tenant + no per-hook rotation. (3) Bootstrap (apps/api/src/subscription-bootstrap.ts): startup scan now also materialises active rest-hook FhirSubscriptions (criteria derived from filterBy/topic — bare resourceType matches every write of that type, the SAME criteria format Workflow triggers use), AND the onWrite hook (re)materialises a FhirSubscription on create/update + tears its handle down on off/entered-in-error, keyed by tenant::subId so there is never a duplicate. LOOP-AVOIDANCE: a FhirSubscription write is treated as CONFIG (re-materialise) and is NEVER fanned out as a deliverable match. (4) Routes (apps/api/src/webhook-routes.ts, tenant-admin gated + deriveOperation→FhirSubscription in iam-route-guards.ts): POST /admin/webhooks (server-authoritative tenant; pre-assigns id, stores secret BEFORE create fires onWrite so the materialised handle is SIGNED; returns {subscription,secret} once, 201, no-store), GET /admin/webhooks (lists own; never a secret), POST /admin/webhooks/:id/$test (synthetic signed Parameters ping through the SAME dispatcher), POST /admin/webhooks/:id/$disable (status=off + unmaterialise + secret removed). Tenant isolation: register/list/test/disable all scoped to the authed principal's tenant; deliveries fire only for the subscription's tenant (engine is per-tenant via onResourceWrite(tenantId,…)). MANDATORY LIVE TEST (apps/api/src/webhook-live-flow.test.ts — the T2-lesson acceptance bar): real node http receiver + real createServer + real PgStorageAdapter on :5433 + real engine bootstrap → register a Patient webhook → write a Patient via a REAL FHIR route → receiver gets EXACTLY ONE POST whose body is the Patient and whose X-Cernia-Signature equals an INDEPENDENTLY-computed sha256 HMAC; the secret is NOT readable via GET /FhirSubscription/:id; a Patient in a DIFFERENT tenant does NOT reach the receiver. ADR-0127: @cernia/subscription typecheck=0 + 179 tests green (incl. 2 new dispatcher signing tests); @cernia/api typecheck=0; the live signed-delivery + webhook-routes (9) + subscription-bootstrap (16, incl. 5 new FhirSubscription cases) = 26 tests green vs :5433 with DB_PASSWORD=cernia; changed source files lint-clean (314 api + 1 subscription pre-existing baseline lint errors in unrelated files = carry-forward, verified identical via git-stash). TIER-GATE GAP (honest): the customWebhooks Growth+ entitlement (@cernia/saas-platform) is NOT yet enforced on POST /admin/webhooks — the route is tenant-admin RBAC-gated (the security boundary) but any tier can register; wiring the saas-platform tier resolver is a follow-up. Files: packages/subscription/src/{types.ts,dispatcher.ts,dispatcher.test.ts}, apps/api/src/{webhook-secret.ts,webhook-routes.ts,webhook-routes.test.ts,webhook-live-flow.test.ts,subscription-bootstrap.ts,subscription-bootstrap.test.ts,iam-route-guards.ts,index.ts}.

Release 3 / T6 — subscription/entitlement gates: closes the T3 customWebhooks gap. (1) Two new platform products developer_portal + marketplace_access added to PLATFORM_PRODUCTS (apps/api/src/product-catalog-store.ts), KNOWN_PRODUCTS (apps/api/src/entitlement-middleware.ts), the index.ts:3089 activeProducts knownProducts array, and the boot seed log line — seedProducts() now seeds all four idempotently at boot (verified live: boot log 'product-catalog: seeded phone-agent + digital-assistant + developer_portal + marketplace_access'). (2) POST /admin/webhooks (register only — $test/$disable/GET stay open so a lapsed tenant can still inspect/tear down) is gated behind developer_portal via the WIRED createRequireProduct('developer_portal', checker) preHandler, passed into webhookRoutes as registerPreHandler (mirrors the phone-agent voice-agent gate); un-entitled → 402 product-not-entitled, entitled → 201. The entitlement checker + boot seed were hoisted in index.ts to run BEFORE the webhook registration so one PostgresProductCatalogStore-backed checker backs the gate. (3) /dev/login now idempotently grants the dev tenant developer_portal+marketplace_access+phone-agent+digital-assistant (grantRef='dev-login') via a new grantProducts seam on devAuthRoutes — DEV-ONLY (guarded by the same isDevLoginEnabled() gate, skipped for platform-operator tokens) + best-effort (a grant failure is logged and never breaks login) — so the platform stays usable locally and the @release-3 specs stay green. (4) Developer portal UI (apps/developer/src/app/webhooks/page.tsx) renders a graceful 'Upgrade required' prompt (data-testid=wh-upgrade) on a 402 instead of a raw error. Tests: NEW apps/api/src/webhook-routes.entitlement.test.ts (3 — un-entitled→402 product-not-entitled + handler never runs, entitled→201 + secret once + no-store, cross-tenant isolation) via real webhookRoutes + real createRequireProduct + fastify.inject; +2 cases in entitlement-middleware.test.ts (KNOWN_PRODUCTS includes both new slugs; developer_portal 402→pass). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, @cernia/developer typecheck=0; the entitlement+webhook suite = 26 tests green vs :5433 (DB_PASSWORD=cernia, incl. the live-flow test); the 4 @release-3 specs (developer-api-keys, developer-webhooks, developer-sdk-sandbox x2) all PASS headed against API :8090 + portal :3006; zero NEW lint errors in changed files (also fixed 2 require-await stubs to non-async). LIVE PROOF on :8090 (fresh worktree boot): dev-login tenant → POST /admin/webhooks → 201 + whsec_ secret (grant worked); a tenant with developer_portal cancelled in product_entitlements → POST /admin/webhooks → 402 product-not-entitled. Files: apps/api/src/{product-catalog-store.ts,entitlement-middleware.ts,entitlement-middleware.test.ts,webhook-routes.ts,webhook-routes.entitlement.test.ts,dev-auth-routes.ts,index.ts}, apps/developer/src/app/webhooks/page.tsx.

SOLO — the posture security-catalog admin surface (the LAST admin surface in this stage without an E2E gate) CLOSED AGAINST THE REAL STACK. tests/e2e/specs/posture-flow.spec.ts (3, @wave-posture) — Playwright 3 passed (37.2s) against real apps/api :8080 + real JWT from POST /dev/login: (1) the /admin/posture dashboard renders the 5 seeded breach-detection rule-types (mass-export[high], mass-delete[critical], off-hours, impossible-travel, custom-fhirpath) with severity badges; (2) the focused-scanner registry renders the 4 seeded scanners (access-control, credential-hygiene, mfa-gap, tenant-policy); (3) REAL-RBAC proof at the api layer — GET /admin/posture/rule-types returns 401 with NO token and 200 (total=5) with a tenant-admin Bearer, /scanners 200 (total=4). ROOT-CAUSE FIX (same recurring lowercase-/admin RBAC catch-all): apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation now maps the two PLATFORM-SCOPED catalog GETs onto synthetic read-only types — /admin/posture/rule-types → SecurityPostureRule (search), /admin/posture/scanners → SecurityPostureScanner (search) — so a tenant-admin's *:admin authorises them (were */* → 403). apps/admin/src/app/admin/posture/posture-api.ts now sends Authorization: Bearer (localStorage cernia.token) and DROPPED credentials:'include' (these routes are platform-scoped → NO x-cernia-tenant header; bearer-only auth, no cookies, compatible with wildcard CORS); the page already consumed the real client (no sample data) — added data-testids (posture-rule-row[data-rule-id]/posture-rule-severity, posture-scanner-row[data-scanner-id], posture-error). NO new migration (catalog is package-served, no table). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, @cernia/admin typecheck=0, the 2 touched admin files + iam-route-guards.ts lint-clean. With posture closed, every admin surface in phase-3-6-specialized that has a shipped backend is now E2E-verified end-to-end against the real stack. PARALLEL BATCH #3 — three more admin surfaces in this stage CLOSED AGAINST THE REAL STACK (same proven pattern as cit-pass-1/4/5 + batches #1/#2): apps/admin (next dev :3210, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE→:8080) over HTTP against the REAL apps/api routes on real Postgres (:5433 fast-clone snapshot, now 141 tables), authenticating with a REAL JWT from POST /dev/login (CERNIA_DEV_LOGIN=1) → real global RBAC hook. Playwright run: 10 passed (2.3m) across 3 specs — (a) tests/e2e/specs/edge-fleet-flow.spec.ts (3, @wave-edge): dashboard renders this tenant's seeded edge node with an active status badge / node detail page resolves via GET /admin/edge/nodes/:id / tenant-B never sees tenant-A's node; (b) tests/e2e/specs/extensions-flow.spec.ts (4, @wave-extensions): capabilities catalog + ui-slots catalog render the platform discriminators / a plugin installed via the real POST /admin/extensions/installations lists for this tenant / tenant-B never sees tenant-A's installations; (c) tests/e2e/specs/migration-jobs-flow.spec.ts (3, @wave-migration): list renders this tenant's job with a pending status badge / detail page opens / tenant-B never sees tenant-A's jobs. ROOT-CAUSE FIX (the same recurring class as ERX/ML/allergy/discount/invoices/sensitive-data): all three route families are lowercase custom /admin/* paths with NO route-level requirePermission, so the global RBAC hook's deriveOperation fell through to the */* catch-all and DENIED them (403) for every role. apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation now maps them onto synthetic resourceTypes with canonical verbs (verified live — all 5 representative GETs return 200 with a tenant-admin token): /admin/edge/nodes[/:id] + /admin/edge/sync-runs → EdgeNode (POST→write, GET-list→search, /:id GET→read / PATCH→write / DELETE→delete); /admin/extensions/{capabilities,permission-grammar,ui-slots} → Extension (read), /admin/extensions/installations[/$uninstall|$upload] → ExtensionInstallation (POST→write, GET→search); /admin/migration/jobs[/:id] → MigrationJob (list/create + /:id read/write/delete). The three admin api-clients (edge-api.ts / extensions-api.ts / migration-api.ts) now send real Authorization: Bearer (localStorage cernia.token) + x-cernia-tenant (NO -id suffix for these route families) and dropped credentials:'include' (wildcard CORS + bearer, no cookies); detail pages use useParams() (not Next-15 use(params)); +data-testids on list/detail pages with row data-attrs placed ON the row element for direct selection. Snapshot already current (141 tables, migrations 094 edge/extensions/migration tables present) so NO manual migration needed this batch. Session total now 12 real-stack E2E gates across 3 parallel batches + 3 solo (~48 tests). ra-ext-upload (parallel batch 2): plugin-archive HTTP upload route. apps/api/src/extensions-routes.ts gains POST /admin/extensions/installations/$upload (registered only when {db} supplied) — accepts a RawPackageContents JSON body, calls ingestPluginArchive (apps/api/src/plugin-archive-ingest.ts) with a configurable signatureVerifier option (new optional ExtensionsRoutesOptions.signatureVerifier, defaults TrustAllSignatureVerifier for dev; prod injects the real @cernia/marketplace package-signer per ADR 0041/0043). 201 + stored PluginInstallation on success; 422 {error:'signature-rejected'} on PluginSignatureError; 400 {error:'invalid-manifest'} on PluginManifestError; 400 invalid-input on a malformed body. This UI-surface / app-architecture change (a new front-office persona app alongside apps/admin / apps/cernia-ops / apps/patient) now has a first-class home: see the 'desk-app' stage in this track for the apps/desk surface as a tracked persona app (deferred item D5 closed 2026-06-13).

stages.name: The Desk — standalone front-office persona app (apps/desk)
stages.rationale: The Desk is the front-office / office-admin daily-driver surface (schedule, calls, patients, intake, insurance, no-shows, waitlist, settings). In Release 1 it was extracted out of apps/admin into its own Next.js app on :3004, making it a first-class persona app alongside apps/admin (tenant admin/clinical), apps/cernia-ops (platform operators), and apps/patient (patient portal). This stage tracks apps/desk as that persona surface — previously the only record of the Desk lived under the work-app sample-data cleanup stage, which did not own it as an app.

baseline.summary: The Desk lived as an (office-admin) route group inside apps/admin; no standalone persona app.

current.summary: apps/desk is a standalone Next.js app on :3004 (@cernia/desk), extracted from apps/admin and removed from it. All Desk tabs (dashboard, schedule, calls, patients + intake + per-patient insurance, no-shows, waitlist, settings) ported with their real backend wiring (FHIR fetcher + voice-calls-api against apps/api). Office-admin RBAC gate (office-admin-gate.ts, office-admin/tenant-admin only) and shared session hooks (use-current-roles.ts → /api/auth/me) carried over. Covered by @release-1-tagged Playwright E2E specs (desk live specs in the 9-spec Release-1 suite, passing headed against the live stack) plus per-client unit tests (intake, insurance, schedule actions, settings).

remaining: apps/desk has no standalone auth/login surface of its own — it shares the localStorage Bearer-token pattern (apps/desk/src/lib/fhir-fetcher.ts reads TOKEN_KEY from window.localStorage) and resolves session/roles via apps/api GET /api/auth/me. A direct hit to :3004 with no token has no in-app sign-in flow; cross-app SSO/session handoff with apps/admin is unspecified.

No dedicated CI/deploy target for apps/desk — .github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml only deploys apps/admin; apps/desk is not in any canary/deploy pipeline (only incidental coverage via the Playwright browser-matrix E2E).

Production-readiness of the standalone surface not yet audited as a first-class app: accessibility (a11y) pass, error/empty/loading states across all tabs, and a not-authorized/expired-token UX (vs. the office-admin-gate banner) have not been reviewed for apps/desk on its own.

history.note: Deferred item D5 — gave apps/desk a first-class home. The Desk was extracted from apps/admin into a standalone Next.js app (@cernia/desk) on port :3004 and removed from apps/admin; the extraction landed on main via PR #216 (commits 78fdbcc9 extract + 7a83bba4 remove-from-admin; the @release-1 desk E2E specs in 0832bd14). The prior triager note under phase-3-7-workapp-cleanup ('no production-readiness track currently owns apps/desk') was updated to point here. Surface lives at apps/desk/ (routes under apps/desk/src/app/desk/*).
Track created from user mandate: "I do not want any mock data. Everything should be wired in properly through backend services." Acknowledged as multi-week scope.

Brought up full local stack against real Postgres. Postgres 16 :5433 + MinIO + Redis + Mailpit + NATS healthy; 40 migrations applied; tenant-acme seeded (20 FHIR resources); apps/api :8080 returning real RSA jwks; admin :3000, trust :3001, patient :3002, installer :3003 all HTTP 200. Fixed 5 blockers: native PG port collision (override file → 5433), api Dockerfile missing scripts/ copy, migrate.js src/migrations resolver, duplicate POST /Connection registration (@cernia/server already provides FHIR CRUD), 122 partially-extracted pnpm packages (fix-pnpm-store.mjs).

Per-domain audit produced at docs/status/sample-data-elimination-plan.md. 30 sample files cataloged with status (0 wired / 11 partial / 12 needs-route / 7 deferrable), admin-page consumers, matching apps/api routes, effort estimate (~50 work-days), and 7-phase execution order.

3 sample files deleted (voice-budget, voice-improvements, questionnaires). 4 admin pages swapped to real backend (voice-budget incl. settings sub-page, voice-agent improvements, forms/[taskId]). New typed clients in /billing/voice-budget/api.ts + /voice-agent/improvements/api.ts. Pages return HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean.

3 sample files deleted (tenants, iam, billing). 27 admin pages swapped + 5 backend CRUD routes added to admin-tenants-routes (GET list, GET :id, PATCH, DELETE soft-delete, GET transitions). IAM swap leveraged existing FHIR generic CRUD for User/Role/RoleBinding/ServiceAccount/ApiKey — no new endpoints needed. Billing swap leveraged existing /Subscription, /Invoice, /UsageEvent generic CRUD plus 3 custom operations. Also caught + fixed a pre-existing Next-14-vs-15 use(params) bug across 7 [id]/* pages that had been 500-ing on baseline. All 32 swapped pages HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean.

3 sample files deleted (audit, reports, communications). 9 admin pages swapped. AuditEvent + Report use FHIR generic CRUD; communications moved to a static lib/communications.ts (no server endpoint; legitimate inline UI copy per the brief). Added AuditEvent types to @cernia/core public barrel. All 9 pages HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean.

4 sample files deleted (cds, integrations, patient-portal, voice-agent). 17 admin pages swapped. CDS leverages /cds-services + generic /CdsService FHIR CRUD. Integrations uses /Connection generic CRUD with client-side protocol filter. Patient-portal uses /admin/patient-portal/* routes + generic /Patient CRUD. Voice-agent /lib/voice-agent-api.ts rewritten to drop mock-mode toggle. All 17 pages HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean. TODOs: CDS invocations + integration transaction logs + portal link-issue actions still need apps/api endpoints (empty-state cards in UI).

3 sample files deleted (inference, marketplace, hie). 17 admin pages swapped. ML registry pages use /admin/ml/models routes; marketplace uses /marketplace/listings; HIE uses new typed wrappers in hie-api.ts. Pages without backend endpoints (jobs, batch, drift, evaluations, quotas, installs, reviews, payouts, hie/participation, hie/queries, hie/rls) render explicit empty-state cards — no fabricated data. 941/941 tests pass; typecheck + lint clean.

9 specialized sample files deleted (ai-governance, compliance, cqm, edge-fleet, extensions, ig-profiles, migration, posture, sensitive-data). 40+ admin pages now render empty-state cards explicitly stating 'Backend endpoint not yet shipped' — no fabricated data. Helper type files retained where domain shapes are needed for the empty-state DTO. Honest gap: per the original brief, these need ~25 work-days of backend endpoint + migration work to truly populate. Status=partial reflects 'sample data eliminated' but 'real data not yet present'.

5 work-app sample files deleted (dashboards, desk, resources, tasks, worklists). The audit was slightly wrong: 'desk' had 6 (office-admin) admin consumers, not work-app — those were converted to empty-state. Work-app pages /work/{tasks/[id], forms/[taskId]} swapped to fetchResource<Task> using generic FHIR CRUD. Final count: ZERO sample-*.ts files in apps/admin/src/app/_data/. ZERO orphan imports across apps/admin/src/. Dev server restarted once after Tailwind/webpack cache held stale references.

Desk workspace (all 7 (office-admin) tabs) fully wired to real backends. Dashboard: 4 stat tiles — live /fhir/Appointment date-range + /fhir/Patient?_summary=count + voiceAgentApi.getOverview('day'). Schedule: status-filter pills + appointment table with computed duration from /fhir/Appointment. Calls: paginated call log from voiceAgentApi.listCalls() with day/week/month window filter. Patients: searchable + paginated /fhir/Patient roster. No-shows: /fhir/Appointment?status=noshow with last-7/30/90-day range toggle. Settings: reminder/escalation/no-show-fee/urgent-line form (in-memory until desk-settings API endpoint ships). Waitlist: wired to new GET /waitlist/entries (resolves follow-up I-E-N5-admin-list-endpoint — added listEntriesForTenant to @cernia/scheduling PostgresWaitlistStore + route in apps/api waitlist-routes.ts). All 3 packages typecheck clean.

tracks.name: CIT — Comprehensive Integration & Testing
tracks.description: Closes four gates for every existing wave: integration ladder (B/A/O/P), backend integration tests against real services, Playwright E2E + multi-tenant + RBAC + PHI-boundary + accessibility, SaaS architecture conformance. Plus a fifth gate: zero unresolved deferred items. See .claude/plans/idempotent-growing-dahl.md.

current.summary: scripts/audit-wave-integration.mjs shipped; emits 5-state ladder per wave into docs/status/wave-integration-ladder.json + dated MD snapshot.
scripts/audit-deferred-items.mjs shipped; first run surfaced 1430 items (249 blockers / 572 tech-debt / 609 review) across 8 kinds. Output at docs/status/deferred-items.json.

history.note: SCHEMA SNAPSHOT REGENERATED — closes the CI-reproducibility carry-forward from the two E2E batches. packages/testing-real-services/cernia-schema-snapshot.sql was stale at 128 tables / migration ~133; the session's E2E gates needed migrations 136-145 applied by hand to the :5433 test DB. Brought that DB fully-migrated (applied the 12 pending table-creating migrations 134/135/136/137/138/139/140/141/142/143/144/145 and recorded all 127 migration filenames in schema_migrations), then regenerated the snapshot via the canonical scripts/generate-schema-snapshot.mjs (offline mode: CERNIA_SNAPSHOT_RAW_FILE + CERNIA_SNAPSHOT_APPLIED_FILE, since docker isn't on the node-subshell PATH on this host). New snapshot: 141 tables (migrations define 143 — 2 dropped/renamed by later migrations, within the generator's slack guard), 127 schema_migrations seeded. Verified: loads cleanly (ON_ERROR_STOP=1, exit 0) into a fresh throwaway DB → 141 tables + 127 migrations + all key new tables present (discount_approval_requests, enterprise_invoices, legal_holds, saas_subscriptions, ml_prediction_samples); the @cernia/testing-real-services migration-drift-guard.test.ts passes (2/2). CI/fresh checkouts now reproduce all 9 of this session's real-stack E2E gates (discount/invoices/legal-holds included) via createMigratedDatabaseFixture WITHOUT manual migration.

PARALLEL-AGENT BATCH #2 — 3 more real-stack E2E gates closed concurrently (compliance frameworks, enterprise-invoices, sensitive-data). Same orchestration: 3 sub-agents on DISJOINT apps/admin subdirs + new specs; orchestrator owned the shared iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation block, applied the missing-table migrations, and ran the serial E2E. 12 tests, all green against the real stack: tests/e2e/specs/compliance-flow.spec.ts (6 — HIPAA framework list / framework detail + structured controls / control detail / control row-nav / framework row-nav / platform-scoped second tenant), tests/e2e/specs/enterprise-invoices-flow.spec.ts (3 — created draft invoice in list with status badge / aging-report panel + total / tenant-B isolation; PostgresEnterpriseInvoiceStore migrations 137+138), tests/e2e/specs/sensitive-data-flow.spec.ts (3 — dashboard renders all 3 KPIs with no 403/error / legal-holds empty-state for a clean tenant / tenant-B reads its own empty scope; PostgresLegalHoldRegistry migration 140). deriveOperation now also maps /admin/compliance/* → ComplianceFramework, /admin/enterprise-invoices* → EnterpriseInvoice (incl. /aging-report + $send/$record-payment/$void), /admin/sensitive-data/* → SensitiveDataRecord (GET-only aggregates + /list). Tenant headers: enterprise-invoices uses x-cernia-tenant; compliance + sensitive-data are platform/JWT-claim-scoped (no header — sensitive-data resolves tenant from the JWT, compliance is a static catalog), both forward x-cernia-tenant-id for parity. +12 deriveOperation unit cases (iam-route-guards.test.ts still 17 groups green). The compliance agent also rewired the [framework] + [framework]/[controlId] detail pages OFF the static sample-compliance mock data ONTO the real getFramework/getControl API (using useParams(), not the Next-15 use(params) that 500'd CQM). MIGRATIONS APPLIED TO THE TEST DB this session (absent from the 128-table snapshot): 136 (discount_approval_requests), 137 (enterprise_invoices), 138 (enterprise_invoice_tax), 140 (legal_holds). The packages/testing-real-services schema snapshot should be regenerated to include 136-140+ so CI reproduces these E2E gates without manual migration. Verification: typecheck clean (api/admin); all touched files lint-clean. Session total: 9 real-stack E2E gates (ERX full pass + ML + allergy + discount + cqm + ig + compliance + invoices + sensitive-data).

PARALLEL-AGENT BATCH — 3 more real-stack E2E gates closed concurrently (discount-approvals, CQM catalog, IG-profiles). 3 sub-agents ran in parallel, each owning a DISJOINT apps/admin subdir + a new tests/e2e/specs spec; the orchestrator owned the one shared file (apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation) + the serial E2E run + commit. 13 tests, all green against the real stack (apps/admin next dev :3210 → real apps/api → real Postgres :5433 → real JWT via POST /dev/login): tests/e2e/specs/discount-approvals-flow.spec.ts (4 — list pending / single-CSM approve→approved / deny→denied / tenant-B isolation; PostgresDiscountApprovalStore migration 136), tests/e2e/specs/cqm-flow.spec.ts (5 — CMS-QPP-2024 catalog list / measure detail / row-nav / domain filter / platform-scoped second-tenant; static catalog), tests/e2e/specs/ig-profiles-flow.spec.ts (4 — 25-IG catalog list / detail / not-found / platform-scoped parity). Recurring lowercase-/admin RBAC catch-all fixed for THREE more route families in deriveOperation: /admin/discount-approvals* → DiscountApproval, /admin/cqm/* → CQMMeasure, /admin/ig-profiles* → ImplementationGuide (canonical verbs; tenant-admin *:admin covers). Each wave's *-api.ts now sends Bearer + the correct tenant header (discount uses x-cernia-tenant; cqm/ig are platform-scoped and forward x-cernia-tenant-id for parity); +data-testids on each surface; +18 deriveOperation unit cases across the session (iam-route-guards.test.ts, 17 test groups green). Bugs fixed during integration: (a) the CQM detail page used the Next-15 `use(params)` async-params API but this build passes params as a plain object → SSR 500; rewired to useParams() (the sibling allergy/erx pattern); (b) the discount spec's row locator used filter({has}) for a data-attr that sits ON the row element → rewritten as a direct attribute selector; (c) migration 136 (discount_approval_requests) is absent from the 128-table test snapshot — applied manually for the run; the snapshot should be regenerated to include 136+ for CI reproducibility. Verification: typecheck clean (api/admin); all touched files lint-clean (pre-existing unrelated lint debt in cqm/page.tsx exhaustive-deps + inference/page.tsx BarChart3 untouched). Note: CQM + IG catalogs are platform-scoped read surfaces (no per-tenant PHI), so their 'isolation' tests assert identical-catalog parity rather than a 404.

Closure Pass 5 (Wave S / S-S8) — ALLERGY-REGISTRY E2E GATE CLOSED against the real stack (third real-stack E2E close this session). tests/e2e/specs/allergy-registry.spec.ts (3 tests, @wave-S) runs apps/admin (next dev :3210, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE→:8080) over HTTP to the REAL apps/api allergy routes (PostgresAllergyStore, migration 076) on real Postgres (:5433), with a REAL JWT from POST /dev/login. Run: 3 passed (3.0m) — per-patient list renders the patient's allergies; detail $verify lifts a patient-reported (unverified) record → verified/lab-confirmed via the UI; per-tenant isolation (tenant-B gets 404 on tenant-A's id, ADR 0004). Two genuine bugs fixed: (a) the SAME lowercase-route RBAC catch-all as ERX/ML — apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation now maps /allergies* (no route-level requirePermission) onto the FHIR AllergyIntolerance resourceType (POST→write, by-patient[/verified]→search, /:id→read, /$verify→write, DELETE→delete) so tenant-admin's *:admin covers it; (b) a response-shape mismatch — apps/api returns { items, total } but apps/admin/src/app/admin/allergies/allergy-api.ts read `.allergies` and crashed the list page on [...undefined]; the client now maps items→allergies (tolerant of both). allergy-api.ts also now sends real Authorization: Bearer + x-cernia-tenant-id (allergy routes use the -id suffix, like ERX — unlike the ml routes); +data-testids on the list/detail pages (allergy-patient-input/-list/-row/-verified-badge/-confirming-test/-verify/-not-found). Tests: +6 deriveOperation allergy unit cases (iam-route-guards.test.ts, now 17 green). typecheck clean (api/admin); touched files lint-clean (the unrelated inference/page.tsx BarChart3 lint error remains pre-existing/untouched). cit-pass-5 stays in-progress — K/N/R/U/V admin UIs + S5/S6/S7 + ~49 Wave-T specialty modules remain (largest surface); this closes the Wave-S allergy slice end-to-end.

Closure Pass 4 (Wave P) — ML-MODEL-REGISTRY E2E GATE CLOSED against the real stack (same pattern as Pass 1). tests/e2e/specs/ml-model-registry.spec.ts (3 tests, @wave-P) runs apps/admin (next dev :3210, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE→:8080) over HTTP to the REAL apps/api ml-platform routes (PerTenantModelRegistry, migration 074) on real Postgres (:5433), with a REAL JWT from POST /dev/login. Run: 3 passed (2.2m) — registry lists the seeded risk-classifier; promote⇄demote round-trips the version promotion state through the UI; per-tenant isolation (promoting in tenant-A leaves tenant-B's same-named model a candidate). The /admin/ml/models* routes had the SAME lowercase-/admin RBAC catch-all hazard ERX did (no route-level requirePermission → global hook derived */* → no role could satisfy): fixed in apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation by mapping them onto a synthetic MLModel resourceType with canonical verbs (GET list→search, GET versions/:version→read, POST register/$promote/$demote→write, $predict→execute) so tenant-admin's *:admin covers the surface. apps/admin/src/app/admin/inference/inference-api.ts now sends real Authorization: Bearer + x-cernia-tenant headers (note: ml routes read x-cernia-tenant, NO -id suffix) instead of Accept-only; +data-testids on the models page (ml-model-list/-row/-detail/-promotion-badge/-promote-toggle). Tests: +7 deriveOperation ML unit cases (iam-route-guards.test.ts) green. Verification: typecheck clean (api/admin); touched files lint-clean (the inference/page.tsx BarChart3 lint error is pre-existing/untouched). cit-pass-4 stays in-progress — its Wave-O/Q E2E gates + the procurement-gated LLM-gateway/risk-classifier PRODUCTION activation remain; this closes the Wave-P ML-registry slice end-to-end.

Closure Pass 1 (ERX) — E2E GATE CLOSED → all 5 gates green, cit-pass-1 DONE. Per the user directive to use a real production-like environment (real APIs/backend, not demo-mode fakes), the @wave-ERX Playwright spec now runs end-to-end against the REAL stack: apps/admin (next dev :3210, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE→:8080) over HTTP to the REAL apps/api (PostgresErxStore, migration 071) on real Postgres (cernia-e2e-pg :5433), authenticating with a REAL JWT from apps/api POST /dev/login. Result: 4 passed (2.6m) — create-draft (composer→POST /erx/prescriptions/$draft→Postgres), queue-list visibility, full FSM walk draft→pending-review→pending-signature→signed, and tenant-B cross-tenant 404 isolation (ADR 0004). Shipped to close it: NEW composer apps/admin/src/app/admin/prescribing/new/page.tsx + createDraftPrescription() in erx-api.ts; ~20 data-testids across the prescribing list/detail pages; erx-api now sends real Authorization: Bearer + x-cernia-tenant-id (dropped credentials:'include' for wildcard-CORS compatibility); spec relocated to tests/e2e/specs/erx-draft-workflow.spec.ts (the collected testDir) and rewritten real-stack (no CERNIA_ADMIN_DEMO). Standing up real RBAC for the prescribing surface surfaced + fixed THREE genuine platform bugs (NOT test-only): (i) apps/api/src/iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation had no rule for the lowercase /erx/prescriptions* routes → catch-all */* (no role could satisfy) → now mapped to a synthetic Prescription resourceType with canonical verbs; (ii) packages/storage/src/search-sql.ts KNOWN_GENERATED_COLUMNS omitted all 14 Phase-4 IAM generated columns (rolebinding_principal_ref/_role_ref/_status, role_name/_scope, user_*, serviceaccount_*, apikey_*) → RoleBinding lookup by principal fell back to JSONB containment that can't match a {reference:…} object → RBAC denied EVERY authenticated principal; registered them so the indexed exact-match path is used; (iii) apps/api/src/dev-auth-routes.ts now idempotently seeds the tenant default-role catalog + a resolvable RoleBinding (slash-free Role ids — the RBAC resolver only parses 2-part Role/{id} refs) for the minted principal, gated by CERNIA_DEV_LOGIN so it never runs in prod. Tests added (all green): +5 deriveOperation ERX cases, +2 IAM-column search cases, +2 dev-login seeding/idempotency cases (the latter asserting the binding resolves through the real @cernia/rbac resolver). Verification: typecheck clean (storage/api/admin); touched files lint-clean; new unit tests pass; the only carry-forward failures are pre-existing (index.ts lint debt + the tenant-brand-store real-PG fixture's password-auth infra mismatch).

Closure Pass 1 (ERX) — Admin + Conformance gates CLOSED. scripts/audit-wave-integration.mjs accuracy fix: (a) path extraction now matches API paths that follow a `${apiBase()}` interpolation inside a template literal (regex lead char `['"`}]`, no trailing-quote requirement) — the prescribing erx-api.ts client (`${apiBase()}/erx/prescriptions`) was previously missed → false `admin: shelf`; (b) the sample-data flag is now scoped to a wave's OWN admin surface (surfaceWaveSampleData: a sample-data file only counts if it shares a route-dir subtree with an api-call hitting the wave's prefixes) instead of an app-wide bool — so ERX (prescribing imports zero fixtures) is no longer tainted by billing/cds/audit pages' fixtures; (c) ⚠ derived from rung values so an n/a ops/patient rung can't inherit those apps' app-wide flags. Result: ERX backend=wired, admin=WIRED (clean), overall=wired, NO ⚠ — Admin + Conformance(sample-data) gates green. Side-benefit: every wave's admin sample-data rung is now accurate per-wave (Q also flipped to clean wired; E/G/H/I/J/K/P/W/X correctly STILL wired-with-sample-data — their own pages import fixtures). Full audit exits 0, no wave regressed (only correct refinements). REMAINING for Pass 1: the E2E gate — tests/e2e/wave-ERX spec targets /admin/erx/* but the shipped UI is /admin/prescribing; spec↔UI alignment + a Playwright run (CI-only) is the sole remainder.

Comprehensive-plan triage pass (no new code gaps; documentation + one status correction). (1) COMPLETED cit-p1 (Phase 1, real-services test harness) → its only open blocker, the runMigrations vitest-worker hang, was RESOLVED by the DONE cit-p12-harness-hang (createMigratedDatabaseFixture, iter 111); with that closed the harness is complete and every integration test this session (40+ across migrations 135-145) runs on it — CIT Phases now 12/12. (2) Added a grounded WHY-NOT-DONE note to each remaining incomplete CIT item in cit-prod-todo.json: closure-passes cit-pass-1..7 (a ~40-55 engineering-day multi-wave Admin-UI/E2E/conformance effort per cit-closure-passes-roadmap.md — backend gates are the green majority; the gaps are per-wave Admin UIs + E2E specs + procurement-gated vendor/Stripe wiring), master-gate mg-* (each gated on the closure passes / strict audits / a 7-day CI clock / a human compliance signature — none code-closeable now; mg-conformance-strict's 361 remaining are all no-in-process-stubs with 0 blocker + 0 architecture-invariant violations, i.e. the multi-week stubs-abolition per cit-stubs-abolition-roadmap.md), and the def-* deferred-items metrics (ongoing incremental quality counts, not single closeable tasks). saas-conformance + audit-wave-integration + audit-deferred-items all exit 0 on this session's work. Curated rollup intentionally left unrecomputed.
