stages.name: Phase 1 — Real-services test harness

current.summary: packages/testing-real-services shipped: per-test Postgres schema (createPostgresFixture) + Redis prefix + MinIO bucket + NATS namespace + Mailpit. The full-schema path is now FAST via createMigratedDatabaseFixture() — a CREATE DATABASE TEMPLATE clone (~60ms/test) of a once-built template, replacing the slow per-test migration run. ra-cit-harness-1 resolved (was never a hang — fsync-bound table rewrites; see iter-110/111 history).

remaining: RESOLVED 2026-05-30 — migration-chain break FIXED + full chain verified end-to-end. The chain previously HALTED on a fresh deploy at 080_stripe_invoices.sql because `invoices` was created by TWO incompatible migrations (024 @cernia/billing + 080 @cernia/m3-stripe-invoice). Fix (commit 2e5fa624): renamed 080's table+indexes+comments `invoices`→`stripe_invoices` and its sole consumer (m3-stripe-invoice store + test); @cernia/billing keeps 024's `invoices`. VERIFICATION: a clean fresh-apply replay of ALL 115 source migrations into a throwaway DB now applies 115/115 → 128 tables (previously died at 080 with only 52). No further hard collisions; the comprehensive duplicate-CREATE-TABLE scan confirmed `invoices` was the only one. m3 typecheck=0; m3 real-PG 20/22 (2 pre-existing baseline-confirmed failures, see next bullet).

FIXED 2026-05-30 — PostgresInvoiceStore.recordPaymentFailure() now populates invoice_failures.tenant_id (NOT NULL, denormalised from the invoice per ADR 0004) by resolving the owning invoice's tenant_id from stripe_invoices and including it in the insert; throws if the invoice doesn't exist (a failure can't be recorded for a non-existent invoice). No InvoiceStore signature / migration change — realizes migration 080's documented intent. The 2 previously-failing m3 tests now seed the invoice first; m3 real-PG suite is 22/22 green, full m3 suite 176/176, typecheck=0, build clean.

Cleanup (benign, found 2026-05-30): caregiver_proxy_relationships is re-declared by BOTH 049_caregiver_proxy_relationships.sql AND 072_caregiver_proxy.sql — identical column set (049 adds a revoked_by CHECK; 072 a state DEFAULT 'active'). 072's CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS silently no-ops; no runtime break (both consumers read the same columns), but 072 should drop its redundant CREATE TABLE so the migration set has exactly one creator per table. Bundle into the migration-collision remediation.

DONE 2026-05-30 — REGENERATED cernia-schema-snapshot.sql (was stale at 52 tables → now 128, all migration end-state DDL). Hardened scripts/generate-schema-snapshot.mjs with a COMPLETENESS GUARD that aborts if the source DB has materially fewer tables than the migrations define (this is what would have caught the silent 52-table staleness) + an offline mode + schema_migrations seeding. Verified: the new snapshot replays cleanly into a fresh DB (psql ON_ERROR_STOP=1, exit 0 → 128 tables) and the drift-guard test (snapshot ⊆ migrations) stays green. Reverse-guard (migrations ⊆ snapshot, fail-on-new-migration-without-regen) intentionally NOT added — it would force a ~16-min regen on every migration PR; the completeness guard + drift-guard cover the real failure modes.

DONE 2026-05-30 — the regenerated snapshot now SEEDS schema_migrations (115 INSERT rows, schema-qualified for pg_dump's empty search_path) so a createMigratedDatabaseFixture clone reports as fully-migrated (verified: 115 schema_migrations rows after replay).

history.note: Full-chain VERIFIED + snapshot REGENERATED (#262 closed). After the stripe_invoices rename, a clean fresh-apply replay of all 115 source migrations into a throwaway DB (cernia-f1-test-pg, fsync=off) applied 115/115 → 128 tables (previously halted at 080 with 52). Regenerated packages/testing-real-services/cernia-schema-snapshot.sql from that fully-migrated DB via the hardened scripts/generate-schema-snapshot.mjs (now: completeness guard that aborts on an under-migrated source — the safeguard against silently re-rotting to 52 tables; offline mode; schema_migrations seeding). New snapshot: 128 CREATE TABLEs (was 52), replays cleanly into a fresh DB (psql ON_ERROR_STOP=1 exit 0), seeds 115 schema_migrations rows; migration-drift-guard test stays green (2/2). Fixed a search_path bug in the seed (pg_dump emits set_config('search_path','') so the seed INSERT must be schema-qualified — would have broken the createMigratedDatabaseFixture replay). Files: packages/testing-real-services/cernia-schema-snapshot.sql, scripts/generate-schema-snapshot.mjs.

FIX (user-directed) for the duplicate-`invoices` migration-chain break: renamed the @cernia/m3-stripe-invoice table 080 `invoices`→`stripe_invoices` (table + idx_stripe_invoices_* indexes + COMMENT + invoice_failures soft-ref comment), and updated its sole consumer packages/m3-stripe-invoice/src/postgres-invoice-store.ts (InvoiceDatabase key + .insertInto/.selectFrom strings) + postgres-invoice-store.test.ts (raw-insert refs). @cernia/billing keeps 024's `invoices` (unchanged). Comprehensive duplicate-CREATE-TABLE scan over all 115 migrations confirmed this was the ONLY hard collision. m3 typecheck=0; m3 real-PG suite 20/22 (2 failures are a pre-existing, baseline-confirmed recordPaymentFailure/invoice_failures.tenant_id NOT-NULL mismatch — origin/main fails identically — now tracked as its own bullet). Files: packages/storage/src/migrations/080_stripe_invoices.sql, packages/m3-stripe-invoice/src/postgres-invoice-store.ts + .test.ts. Full 001→115 fresh-apply replay + snapshot regen (#262) still pending (replay is slow: phase7 DDL migrations 014-017 are 800-911 lines each).

DISCOVERY while attempting the snapshot regen (#262): the migration chain is BROKEN on a fresh deploy. Replayed all 115 source migrations into a fresh throwaway DB (faithful runMigrations semantics — transaction-per-file, simple-protocol multi-statement) and it deterministically halts at 080_stripe_invoices.sql ('column stripe_invoice_id does not exist'). Root cause: duplicate `invoices` CREATE TABLE in 024 (@cernia/billing) and 080 (@cernia/m3-stripe-invoice) — two incompatible schemas; 024 wins, 080's unique index on a column 024 lacks fails. This is why both the dev DB (cernia-postgres) and the committed snapshot are stuck at exactly 52 tables: the docker migrate one-shot also dies at 080. ~35 migrations (080→end) are unreachable on fresh deploy. Full collision scan also found a BENIGN dup: caregiver_proxy_relationships in 049+072 (identical columns, silent no-op). Snapshot regen (#262) is BLOCKED on fixing the chain. The `invoices` remediation needs a rename decision (which of two shipped invoicing features yields) + consumer-store/test updates — surfaced to the user for direction. Data-safe to rename 080's table (it has never existed in any current-migration deploy). No code committed this discovery beyond status-JSON tracking.

ra-cit-harness-1 RESOLVED. Re-diagnosed (iter 110) with side-channel pg_stat_activity instrumentation: the prior 'GENERATED × pg-driver × vitest worker' root-cause class was WRONG. It is NOT a hang — the migration backend is `active` on wait_event=IO:DataFileImmediateSync (synchronous fsync of relation rewrites from dozens of `ALTER TABLE resources ADD COLUMN ... GENERATED ALWAYS AS STORED`); the fresh 87-migration run is genuinely ~60-90s (slower with fsync=on) and only LOOKED like a hang vs vitest's 5s default timeout. The 'standalone ~600ms' baseline was a false comparison (idempotent no-op re-run). FIX (iter 111): createMigratedDatabaseFixture() in packages/testing-real-services/src/postgres-fixture.ts — builds a shared template DB ONCE by replaying a committed end-state schema snapshot (cernia-schema-snapshot.sql via scripts/generate-schema-snapshot.mjs; 479 inline GENERATED columns, 0 ADD COLUMN → rewrite-free ~30s build) under an advisory lock with atomic staging-rename, then clones it per test via CREATE DATABASE ... TEMPLATE (~60ms). New test passes (tables>30, schema_migrations present, resources empty, clone isolation, clone<10s). Unblocks cit-p2 + ra-cit-stubs pg-mem migration + cit-closure-passes.
Narrowed: "Build packages/testing-real-services/src/index.ts" → "Resolve runMigrations vitest-worker hang (test skipped; standalone runMigrations works in ~600ms)"

CIT harness: packages/testing-real-services/src/{mailpit,nats}-fixture.ts shipped (Mailpit via HTTP API with X-Cernia-Test-Id stamp + waitForCount + drain on cleanup; NATS namespace-only fixture with subject/stream prefixes — consumer owns the client lifecycle). createIsolatedTestEnv() now spins all 5 services (pg, redis, s3, nats, mailpit) in parallel and tears them down via Promise.allSettled. 18 new tests cover the fixture contract.

CIT harness: investigated runMigrations vitest-worker hang. Hypothesis tested + falsified: a dedicated short-lived Pool (max=1, idleTimeoutMillis=1, allowExitOnIdle=true) for the migration phase, separate from the test`s main pool, was thought to release worker handles cleanly after migrations. Reality: the hang reproduces (timed out at 60s) — root cause is deeper than pool lifecycle. ra-cit-harness-1 remains open; next hypotheses to try documented inline in the remaining bullet (stdio backpressure / specific-migration bisect / pg_advisory_lock). Pivoted to ra-cds-6 for the next iteration to keep the loop productive.

CIT harness Hypothesis B test (ra-cit-harness-1-bisect): ran a bisect test inside vitest that applied migrations one-at-a-time with stdout progress logging. Result: migrations 001-012 succeeded in vitest. The test then printed `[bisect 13/88] 013_audit_sharding.sql` and never printed 14/88 — i.e. migration 013 hung or the transition to 014 hung. The 180s testTimeout triggered after 218s wall-clock. Migration 013 contents (packages/storage/src/migrations/013_audit_sharding.sql): 6 ALTER TABLE statements on audit_log (ADD COLUMN shard_id NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, ADD CONSTRAINT CHECK shard_id >= 0, ADD COLUMN prev_shard_hash, UPDATE audit_log SET prev_shard_hash (no-op on empty table)) + 2 CREATE INDEX on audit_log + 4 ALTER TABLE resources ADD COLUMN ... GENERATED ALWAYS AS (...) STORED for SecurityFinding + 3 CREATE INDEX on resources. Standalone Node script applies all 88 migrations in ~600ms, so the issue is the vitest interaction with this specific multi-statement SQL file (whole file is one `sql.raw(text).execute(trx)` call). Next hypothesis to try: split migrations on statement boundaries before passing to sql.raw — if per-statement execution works in vitest where bulk does not, the fix is a runMigrations refactor. Bisect test (postgres-fixture-bisect.test.ts) deleted after the diagnostic; the finding is recorded inline in the remaining bullet so future agents can pick up from this point.

CIT harness Hypothesis C test: applied migrations 001-013 one SQL statement at a time inside vitest (each statement in its own sql.raw().execute(trx) call, all inside the same transaction). Hypothesis was that splitting on statement boundaries would unblock the hang isolated to migration 013 in Hypothesis B. Result: still hangs, BUT exposed a much earlier slow point — migration 010_phase2_tier3_terminology_context.sql statement #20 (ALTER TABLE resources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS grp_actual TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS ...) took 8731ms (vs ~ms expected for an ALTER on an empty table), and the next statement(s) hung. Combined with Hypothesis B`s finding (whole-file 013 hangs, which also has many GENERATED-column ALTERs), the root cause class is narrowed to PostgreSQL `GENERATED ALWAYS AS (...) STORED` column DDL under vitest`s forks worker — likely an interaction between pg-driver`s simple-query protocol, vitest`s stdio/IPC channel, and Postgres`s row-recomputation phase for the new generated column. Diagnostic tests deleted after the finding was recorded; next hypotheses listed for the next agent (extended query protocol; statement_timeout to convert hang to error; vitest pool=threads instead of forks; testcontainers Postgres).

current.summary: pg-mem is FULLY ELIMINATED (verified 2026-05-31: absent from every package.json, zero createPgMemKysely() callers across packages+apps, pgmem-polyfills.ts deleted). The remaining stub class is InMemory* only — saas-conformance.mjs no-in-process-stubs counts 360 (down from 384), ALL tech-debt severity, 0 blocker. Those split into: (a) apps/api no-db FALLBACK adapters — boot wires the Postgres store when a db is present, the InMemory one runs only db-less (local/test); abolishing them = dropping db-less boot support (architecture decision); (b) @cernia/auth-providers default stores whose abolition is gated on the Redis-vs-Postgres OIDC-state decision; (c) package test-fixtures to relocate to /testing subpaths (cosmetic). None are dead code.

remaining: DONE 2026-05-31 — pg-mem fully eliminated: not a dependency in any package.json, zero createPgMemKysely() callers, and packages/storage/src/testing/pgmem-polyfills.ts is deleted. (The earlier 'migrate ~hundreds of pg-mem tests' + 'delete pgmem-polyfills.ts' bullets are closed — pg-mem has no remaining footprint; the real-services migration happened incrementally across the closure passes.)

InMemory* abolition (DECISION-GATED — all tech-debt / 0 blocker): the 18 apps/api InMemory* classes are the db-less boot FALLBACK (Postgres wired when a db is present), so removing them is an architecture decision (drop db-less boot?). The @cernia/auth-providers default InMemoryOidcStateStore / InMemoryMfaChallengeStore / InMemoryRefreshTokenStore abolition is gated on the Redis-vs-Postgres OIDC-state decision. Remaining package test-fixture InMemory* classes should move to /testing subpaths (cosmetic). None are dead code; each needs a product/architecture call, not a mechanical sweep.

FIXED a suite-wide collection break. tests/e2e/specs/branding.spec.ts had two per-describe `test.use({ ...devices['Desktop Firefox'/'Desktop Safari'] })` blocks (added with the PROD-P9 browser matrix) — forcing a browser INSIDE a describe is invalid in Playwright ('Cannot use({ defaultBrowserType }) in a describe group'), and that single error broke COLLECTION of the ENTIRE e2e suite: `playwright test --list` returned `Total: 0 tests in 0 files`. So NONE of the e2e specs could run (a hard failure that also left the integration-ladder E2E column at n/a). Removed the two redundant blocks (their assertions — load-without-errors / WCAG warning / preview tabs — are already in the main describe, which the 5-project matrix runs on every browser) + the now-unused `devices` import. Verified: `playwright test --list` now collects Total: 1060 tests in 16 files across chromium/firefox/webkit/edge/chromium-mobile (212 under chromium-desktop). Full execution still requires the dev stack booted (apps/admin :3210 webServer + apps/api) — collection/compile is the loop-verifiable gate; per-wave run is CI's job. File: tests/e2e/specs/branding.spec.ts.

stages.name: Phase 12 — Close every wave in 7 priority passes

current.summary: 3 of 7 passes closed all-gates-green against the REAL stack (Pass 1 ERX, Pass 3 G caregiver-federation+SAML, Pass 7 foundation/white-label). 4 remain — Pass 2/4/6 are largely procurement-gated (vendor + LLM + Stripe BAAs), Pass 5 is the largest admin surface (~10-14 eng-days). See docs/operations/runbooks/cit-closure-passes-roadmap.md.

remaining: Pass 2: E + dependent F / I / J / W (voice-agent stack) — vendor-BAA + multi-day admin UIs
Pass 4: O + P + Q (AI / ML / telehealth-RPM) — P closed; O LLM-provider-BAA-gated
Pass 5: K + L + M + N + R + S + T + U + V — largest remaining admin surface

Pass 6: X + Z + Y + DD + AA + BB — dev-testable surface COMPLETE (cross-tenant cernia-ops CSM/health/renewal/expansion/TtFV/activation + storefront browse→buy→entitlement, all real-stack E2E). Only Stripe PRODUCTION credentials remain (procurement).

history.note: Pass 2 / Wave J FULL is now 100% COMPLETE — the final piece, J3-C SNF (skilled-nursing) MDS workflow port, landed via 2 parallel agents + orchestrator integration, GROUNDED on existing packages (no invention). (A) SNF MDS→PDPM→HIPPS compute: NEW apps/api/src/snf-mds-routes.ts mounts POST /admin/snf/mds/$compute-hipps + $score-adl, reusing @cernia/specialty-ltc-mds-quality's scorePdpm/generateHippsCode/mds-adl (the CMS PDPM HIPPS crosswalk + RUG logic already shipped there — no math reinvented); 10 app.inject unit tests incl. the known-good fixture (orthopedicSurgery/ADL12/reducedPhysicalFunction → HIPPS TOJRB). (B) SNF workflow templates: NEW apps/api/src/snf-workflow-templates.ts exports 4 real WorkflowTemplates (C1 snf-admission-5day-pps: admission→MDS→care-plan→PPS/HIPPS→claim; C2 snf-reassessment-rai; C3 snf-discharge-planning [PASRR]; C4 snf-daily-digest, ported from web/bots/daily-snf-digest.ts), every graph node a real kind from the 20-node @cernia/workflow-nodes catalog; 25 well-formedness unit tests; seeded into the workflow-template registry via seedIfAbsent in bot-workflow-routes.ts (both Postgres + InMemory paths). Orchestrator wired: snfMdsRoutes registration in index.ts, the SNF seed loop, iam-route-guards deriveOperation (/admin/snf/mds/$compute-hipps|$score-adl → SnfAssessment execute), + added @cernia/specialty-ltc-mds-quality as an apps/api dep + tsconfig ref. VERIFIED: 35 unit tests (10 HIPPS + 25 templates) + E2E tests/e2e/specs/snf-mds-flow.spec.ts (4, @wave-J) Playwright 4 passed — the catalog now shows all 4 SNF templates (5 total w/ the voice starter) + each resolves by id/latest; $compute-hipps returns a valid 5-char HIPPS + PDPM components; invalid→400; RBAC-gated. @cernia/api typecheck=0; new files lint-clean; NO new migration (registry 093 + stateless HIPPS). WAVE J FULL pieces ALL complete: catalog browse+instantiate, Stripe commerce (real cs_test_ sessions) + settlement webhook + deploy-gate, sandbox provisioner + metering, AI-gen, node-type catalog, visual editor + drafts, SNF MDS port. cit-pass-2 remaining = ONLY the two genuinely non-buildable blockers: Wave W email resale (reseller-program agreements + BAAs) + Wave-E realtime call media (live PSTN call through a public tunnel). CIT closure passes 3/7.

Pass 2 / Wave J — Stripe TEST-MODE checkout slice WIRED (closes the build-session edge agent A flagged). NEW apps/api/src/stripe-checkout-client.ts (native-fetch outbound client, no `stripe` SDK — same posture as the Twilio client) creates a REAL Stripe Checkout Session via api.stripe.com using inline price_data (works against a fresh test account with NO pre-created Price objects). marketplace-checkout-routes.ts $build-session now, when STRIPE_SECRET_KEY (sk_test_… from .env) is set, creates the real session (one-time→mode payment, rental→mode subscription w/ monthly recurring) stamped with metadata {tenantId, listingId, pricingModel, priceUsdCents[/PerMonth]} — exactly what marketplace-webhook-routes reads back — and returns {ok, config, stripe:{id,url}}; degrades to engine-config-only on any Stripe error or when no key (route never 500s). index.ts passes STRIPE_SECRET_KEY; iam-route-guards maps /admin/marketplace/checkout/$build-session → MarketplaceListing(write) (was deliberately unmapped/procurement-gated). E2E tests/e2e/specs/stripe-checkout-build-session.spec.ts (4, @wave-J) Playwright 4 passed: one-time → real cs_test_… + hosted url; rental → subscription cs_test_…; free → 400 (engine rejects); RBAC (no token → 401/403); skip-guarded when no key. The FULL commerce loop is now real end-to-end: $build-session (real Stripe Checkout Session) → checkout.session.completed settlement webhook (real HMAC signature) → persisted entitlement → production deploy-gate (402 unpaid → 200 paid). @cernia/api typecheck=0; stripe-checkout-client.ts + marketplace-checkout-routes.ts lint-clean; no migration/snapshot change. Wave-J FULL now complete except the SNF MDS 3.0 clinical workflow port (J3-C).

Pass 2 / Wave J FULL — built via 5 parallel agents + orchestrator integration (disjoint files; orchestrator wired index.ts + iam-route-guards + migrations 147-150 + snapshot + E2E). Using the real .env Stripe + OpenAI creds. (A) Stripe marketplace settlement: migration 147 marketplace_entitlements + PostgresMarketplaceEntitlementStore + POST /webhook/stripe/marketplace (unauth; @cernia/m3-stripe-invoice signature verify, fail-closed) → mints entitlement on checkout.session.completed + POST /admin/marketplace/listings/:slug/$deploy gate (402 unpaid / 200 paid). (B) sandbox provisioner: migrations 148 sandbox_tenants + 149 sandbox_usage_events + PostgresSandboxTenantStore (provision/teardown/recordUsage with @cernia/billing markup) + /admin/sandbox/tenants routes. (C) AI generation: ai-workflow-generator.ts wired to the boot @cernia/o1-llm-gateway (real OpenAI when permitted; hipaa-strict routing declines non-BAA OpenAI → deterministic valid fallback, route never fails); /bot-runtime/$ai-gen-request. (D) node catalog: NEW @cernia/workflow-nodes (20 runtime-GROUNDED node kinds with backedBy provenance — explicitly NOT a fabricated 106; WorkflowNode.type has no enforced enum) + GET /workflows/node-types. (E) visual editor: migration 150 workflow_drafts + PostgresWorkflowDraftStore (CRUD + graph validation, relaxed to allow blank in-progress drafts after a real cross-agent integration bug) + /admin/workflows/drafts routes + apps/admin React Flow editor (/admin/workflows/drafts/:id, ssr:false) consuming the node catalog. VERIFIED: integration entitlement 5/5 + sandbox 6/6 + draft 6/6; E2E marketplace-commerce-flow (4: deploy-402 → REAL-Stripe-signed settlement → deploy-200 + webhook fail-closed + sandbox lifecycle + isolation), workflow-designer-flow (3: node catalog + AI-gen valid draft + RBAC), workflow-editor-flow (3: draft CRUD + isolation + editor UI mounts canvas/palette) — all green. @cernia/api typecheck=0; new api+admin+package files lint-clean (index.ts pre-existing line-3248+ debt carry-forward); snapshot regenerated → 147 tables (migrations 147-150). apps/api gained @cernia/m3-stripe-invoice + @cernia/j1c-sandbox-tenants + @cernia/workflow-nodes workspace deps. Wave-J FULL pieces complete: catalog browse+instantiate + Stripe commerce+deploy-gate + sandbox+metering + AI-gen + node catalog + visual editor+drafts. ONLY remaining Wave-J item: the SNF MDS 3.0 clinical workflow port (J3-C). cit-pass-2 overall still in-progress (Wave W resale procurement-hard; Wave-E realtime call media needs live PSTN+tunnel). CIT closure passes 3/7.

Pass 2 / Wave D6+E inbound telephony webhook CLOSED with REAL Twilio HMAC-SHA1 signature validation (using the .env Twilio creds). apps/api booted with CERNIA_TELEPHONY_BACKEND=twilio + TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID/AUTH_TOKEN + CERNIA_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=https://localhost:8080 → the real TwilioVoiceBackend mounts POST /telephony/twilio/voice and the real verifier (@cernia/notifications computeTwilioSignature) is wired. NEW tests/e2e/specs/telephony-webhook-flow.spec.ts (3, @wave-D6) Playwright 3 passed: (1) a webhook with a genuine X-Twilio-Signature (computed from the real auth token over https://localhost:8080/telephony/twilio/voice + sorted params) for a tenant-owned number (seeded tenant_phone_numbers, tenant 11111111…) → 200 TwiML + a persisted call_sessions row, observed via GET /api/voice-agent/calls for that tenant; (2) tampered + absent signature → 403 (real HMAC, not the stub verifier the in-process tests use); (3) unknown destination number → 200 Reject TwiML with no number leak. Skip-guarded for portability (skips if no TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN or if the api is on the stub backend → route 404). NOTE: this closes the inbound webhook + auth + persistence HTTP layer; the realtime bidirectional AUDIO (Twilio Media Streams websocket) needs a live PSTN call to a provisioned number through a public tunnel and is not locally loopable. No source change (existing routes + real creds); spec-only.
  FINAL pass-2 verdict: even WITH the .env credentials (Stripe/Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/OpenAI), pass 2 cannot reach all-gates-green — the remaining gates are not key-shaped: Wave W resale = Google/MS/Zoho reseller PROGRAM membership + BAAs (partner agreements, absent from .env); Wave-J FULL = weeks of feature build (sandbox provisioner/isolation, metering wiring, 106-node library, visual editor canvas, SNF MDS port — Stripe/OpenAI keys only help the settlement-webhook + AI-gen slices); Wave-E call MEDIA = a live inbound PSTN call through a public tunnel. Session-closed pass-2 surfaces: E admin, F, I (full), J (non-proc subset), D6 webhook+signature. CIT closure passes remain 3/7.

Pass 2 / Wave J (workflow marketplace) NON-PROCUREMENT subset CLOSED against the real stack — catalog browse + template instantiate, deliberately skipping Stripe/sandbox/LLM. iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation now maps the catalog/instantiate routes (were */* 403): /workflows/templates + /:id + /:id/latest + /bot-runtime/background-templates → WorkflowTemplate (search/read), /bot-runtime/$dry-run → WorkflowTemplate (execute), /marketplace/listings (GET/POST) + /:slug → MarketplaceListing (search/write/read). The Stripe checkout (/admin/marketplace/checkout/*) + entitlement (/admin/marketplace/entitlements/*) + LLM (/bot-runtime/$ai-gen-request) routes are left UNMAPPED on purpose (procurement-gated). apps/admin: marketplace/page.tsx swapped SAMPLE_LISTINGS→GET /marketplace/listings (NEW marketplace-api.ts); workflows/templates was already real (added Bearer auth headers + testids). E2E tests/e2e/specs/workflow-marketplace-flow.spec.ts (4, @wave-J) Playwright 4 passed: template catalog list→get-by-id→latest-version (Postgres registry, migration 093, 1 seeded), marketplace browse→instantiate(POST publish)→resolves-by-slug, RBAC gating (unauth 401/403), + the /admin/marketplace + /admin/workflows/templates UI rendering the real catalogs. NOTE: marketplace listing store is the prior-art in-memory seeded catalog (not tenant-isolated Postgres) — catalog-browse only; the templates registry IS Postgres-backed. ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, iam-route-guards.ts lint=0, the 4 admin files lint-clean. cit-pass-2 still in-progress: Wave-J FULL (sandbox provisioner + metering + Stripe settlement webhook + visual editor + LLM gen) + Wave W (reseller-agreement procurement-hard) + Wave-E realtime call-media E2E remain.

Pass 2 / Wave I (B2B Support Channel) BUILT END-TO-END from scratch (multi-agent: parallel backend + frontend agents against a locked route contract; orchestrator integrated). Was 0% wired (only the @cernia/i1/i4 libraries existed). NEW: packages/storage/src/migrations/146_support_tickets.sql (support_tickets + support_ticket_comments, tenant-scoped PK (tenant_id,id), jsonb body + extracted columns); apps/api/src/support-ticket-store.ts (PostgresSupportTicketStore — async create/get/list(filter)/update[auto-stamps resolvedAt/closedAt + clears on reopen]/addComment[sets firstRespondedAt on first public comment]/listComments, tenant-scoped; consumes the @cernia/i1 Ticket/TicketComment types); apps/api/src/support-routes.ts (POST/GET /support/tickets, GET/PATCH /support/tickets/:id, POST/GET /support/tickets/:id/comments; principal+tenant from rbacClaims; registered db-gated in index.ts); iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation → SupportTicket (write/search/read); apps/admin/src/app/admin/support/{page,[id]/page,new/page}.tsx + apps/admin/src/lib/support-api.ts (typed client). VERIFIED: support-ticket-store.integration.test.ts 8/8 vs real Postgres; tests/e2e/specs/support-channel-flow.spec.ts (3, @wave-I) Playwright 3 passed against the running stack — full lifecycle (create→list→get→PATCH resolve[resolvedAt stamped]→add public comment[firstRespondedAt set]→list comments), RBAC (unauth→401), tenant isolation (tenant-B→404 on tenant-A id), + the admin list+detail UI rendering a real-api-seeded ticket. Schema snapshot regenerated → 143 tables (migration 146 seeded). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0; support-routes/support-ticket-store/iam-route-guards + the 4 admin files lint-clean (index.ts pre-existing line-3226+ debt is carry-forward). apps/api gained @cernia/i1-support-ticket-system as a workspace dep + tsconfig reference. Wave I admin + backend + E2E gates now GREEN. cit-pass-2 remains in-progress (Wave J + Wave W + Wave-E call-media E2E outstanding).

Pass 2 (voice-agent stack E/D6/F/I/J/W) PROGRESS — Wave-E voice-agent ADMIN E2E gate closed in dev-mode + 2 RBAC bugs fixed; still in-progress (not all-gates-green). BAA CLARIFICATION: vendor BAAs are a PRODUCTION/PHI gate, NOT a testing gate — enforceBaaPosture (apps/api/src/baa-allowlist.ts) refuses non-BAA providers only when mode='production'; mode='dev' warns-only, so the stack runs in dev with stub/sandbox providers + synthetic data, prod still hard-fails closed. NEW tests/e2e/specs/voice-agent-admin-flow.spec.ts (3, @wave-E) — Playwright 3 passed (1.3m) real-stack: /api/voice-agent/{overview,policy,persona,customization,calls,identity-failures} authorise for a tenant-admin + return real shapes; PATCH /api/voice-agent/policy round-trips; RBAC (unauth→401, non-admin→403). Bugs fixed (both real-stack-only — in-process route tests register no global hook): (1) /api/voice-agent/* unmapped → */* 403 → mapped to VoiceAgentConfig + VoiceAgentCall in iam-route-guards.ts deriveOperation; (2) CRITICAL — the carrier-initiated telephony webhooks /telephony/twilio/* + /telephony/livekit/* were 401-gated by the global hook (the carrier has no Cernia JWT — the Twilio HMAC signature is the auth), making inbound calls unreachable; added both prefixes to permitAllForUnauthenticatedRoutes (the internal /telephony/outbound stays authed → TelephonyCall). Logged non-blocking finding: call_sessions.tenant_id is uuid-typed (vs text elsewhere) → /calls 500s for a non-UUID tenant id. ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, iam-route-guards.ts lint-clean, iam-route-guards.test.ts extended +13 assertions (17 green). REMAINING for all-gates-green: apps/admin voice UI sample→real swap, Waves I/J/W admin UIs, realtime call-orchestration E2E (telephony media path needs a public-HTTPS Twilio backend / fake-media harness) — multi-session, no longer BAA-blocked. CIT closure passes remain 3/7.

Pass 7 (A/B/C/D/H/H-EXT/H-EXT-2/CC) CLOSED all-gates-green. Two concrete gaps closed: (1) the slugResolver boot stub — NEW apps/api/src/tenant-slug-resolver.ts makeTenantSlugResolver(storage) resolves the canonical slug (FHIR Tenant.name) via the tenant-scoped StorageAdapter, wired at BOTH the tenantBrandRoutes (H1-A) + customDomainRoutes (H3-B) boot sites (replacing the resolver that REJECTED on every call → brand PUT 500'd, custom-domain 404'd); unknown tenant → NotFoundError → clean 404. 5 unit tests (tenant-slug-resolver.test.ts). (2) the 'minimal verification' E2E — NEW tests/e2e/specs/white-label-flow.spec.ts (5, @wave-H + @wave-H-EXT-2), Playwright 5 passed (2.2m) real-stack: H-EXT-2-C short-urls create→public /r/:slug 302→invalidate→410 + open-redirect allow-list guard (400) + per-tenant isolation (403); H1-A brand unprovisioned-tenant→404 (proves the real slugResolver is WIRED, was a 500 stub) + cross-tenant 403 + unauth 401. RBAC: deriveOperation now maps /tenants/:id/brand→TenantBrand + /tenants/:id/short-urls*→ShortUrl. Foundation/catalog waves A/B/C/D/CC are catalog-only by design. ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, lint-clean, iam-route-guards(17)+tenant-brand(24)+slug-resolver(5) units green. Brand happy-path slug-materialisation through HTTP needs a superadmin-provisioned Tenant (POST /admin/tenants, not dev-login-mintable) so it's covered by the resolver unit test + tenant-brand-routes.test.ts rather than a dev-login browser E2E. CIT closure passes: 1/7 → 3/7 (Pass 1 + Pass 3 + Pass 7).

Roadmap shipped at docs/operations/runbooks/cit-closure-passes-roadmap.md — captures per-pass scope, effort estimate (~40-55 days sequential, ~3-4 weeks with 2-agent parallelism), acceptance criteria, and the audit-script-driven per-gap loop. Closure-passes 1-7 remain open as multi-session work; each pass closes its bullet when the wave-integration ladder + Playwright + real-services backend tests + saas-conformance all pass for the waves in scope.

Closure-pass 1 (ERX) — backend api shipped: packages/erx-service/src/api.ts (ErxApi + InMemoryErxStore + tenant-scoping invariant) + 16 unit tests. Playwright @wave-ERX spec at tests/e2e/wave-ERX/erx-draft-workflow.spec.ts (composer flow + list view + FSM walk + cross-tenant isolation, 4 tests). Follow-ups: apps/api route registration (blocked on pnpm-lock WIP resolution), admin UI components, Postgres-backed IErxStore adapter. ERX wave backend rung in audit-wave-integration ladder transitions shelf → partial.

Closure-pass 3 (Wave G) — SAML 2.0 Response handler shipped at packages/g-d5-saml-oidc-federation/src/saml-response-handler.ts. Closes the long-running deferred item flagged since project start (apps/api/src/sso-routes.ts docstring + the original PROD-G deferred row). parseSamlResponseEnvelope + extractSamlClaims + validateSamlConditions + SamlSignatureVerifier port (NoopSamlSignatureVerifier refuses by default; StubAcceptAllSignatureVerifier for tests only) + handleSamlResponse end-to-end. Zero new deps — pure logic, browser-compatible (atob + Uint8Array + TextDecoder via globalThis). Wave G backend rung shelf → partial in next audit run.

Tests cover round-trip / cross-tenant null on listForPatient/getById/verify/remove / verified flag derivation from source / verify() with empty + missing + cross-tenant guards / classifier escalation persistence / reactions JSONB round-trip / newest-first ordering / DB-CHECK on (source, verified) tuple + unknown source. Cumulative session real-Postgres integration tests: 10 ERX + 11 Caregiver + 17 RPM + 15 ML + 15 LLM-cost + 19 Allergy = 87 tests across 6 waves and 6 migrations (071/072/073/074/075/076).

Closure-pass 5 (Wave K, K-R4 RBAC audit-log) extends: migration 077_rbac_audit_log.sql (tenant-scoped rbac_audit_events table; 13-value action CHECK + 5-value target_type CHECK + hex-pattern CHECK on prev_hash and hash columns; UNIQUE (tenant_id, sequence_num) for atomic-append serialisation under concurrency; UNIQUE id + UNIQUE hash; JSONB before_state/after_state; subject_patient_id hoisted out of subject blob for fast HIPAA §164.528 disclosure-accounting lookups; 5 indices including break-glass-events partial + per-actor + per-patient cross-tenant) + packages/k4-rbac-audit-log/src/postgres-append-store.ts (implements AppendOnlyStore; retry loop on UNIQUE violation handles concurrent appends without application locks — Postgres MVCC + UNIQUE constraint gives serialisable behaviour for free; snapshot canonical-JSON serialisation preserved through JSONB round-trip; subject reconstruction from three hoisted columns) + 21 real-Postgres integration tests including a 4-way concurrent-append test that confirms the chain serialises into monotonic sequence_num=0..3 with intact hash integrity. DefaultChainVerifier accepts the persisted chain — round-trip integrity validated end-to-end. scripts/audit-wave-integration.mjs heuristic extension — added registered-route-file @cernia/<pkg> import detection. The audit's prior heuristic only flipped a wave's Backend rung to 'wired' when the route file's BASENAME textually matched a wave package name (e.g. allergy-routes.ts ↔ s8-allergy-checking). That missed multi-wave route files: apps/api/src/telehealth-rpm-routes.ts imports @cernia/q-s2-rpm-ingestion + @cernia/q-s4-rpm-care-plans + @cernia/q-s6-rpm-adherence and is registered as telehealthRpmRoutes — clearly Wave Q is live, but the audit reported Q=shelf. New logic: for every registered route file in apps/api/src, also scan its content for @cernia/<wave-pkg> imports and credit that wave. Effect: Wave B (shelf→wired), Wave G (shelf→wired), Wave P (shelf→wired), Wave Q (shelf→wired) flipped to wired in this run. Wave S retained wired. Net: 4 waves moved Backend dot from grey to green from a single audit-script improvement. wave-integration-audit-2026-05-27.md updated.

tracks.name: PROD-P14 — Mobile App Distribution
tracks.description: R-* packages built to Android + iOS bundles. App Store + Play Store submission. Push notifications.

current.summary: 2 of 7 bullets shipped; 5 remain. See completed[] + remaining[].

history.note: PROD-P14: .github/workflows/mobile-android-build.yml active + iOS skeleton .future + scripts/mobile-bootstrap-signing-key.sh + 7 docs/mobile/* (signing-vault + listing checklists + MDM plan + accessibility plan + runbook). Intune + JAMF Pro coverage. Commit 82685966.

PROD-P14: .github/workflows/mobile-ios-build.yml — renamed from .future + added preflight job that gates the macOS build on MATCH_GIT_URL/MATCH_PASSWORD/MATCH_GIT_BASIC_AUTHORIZATION secrets being present. Build job + TestFlight upload skip cleanly (workflow stays green) when secrets are missing, fully activate once provisioned. fastlane match + gym + pilot wiring per docs/mobile/ios-signing-cert-vault.md.

PROD-P14: docs/mobile/store-listing-manifests/app-store.yaml — populated 18-field manifest (app_name, subtitle, promotional_text, description, keywords, urls, screenshot paths, primary_category=Medical, app_privacy section, App Review information, bundle_identifier=com.cernia.app). Verified by scripts/check-store-listing.mjs with field-length + URL-HTTPS + category-must-be-Medical + bundle-id-regex checks. PASS verdict.

PROD-P14: docs/mobile/store-listing-manifests/play-store.yaml — populated 18-field manifest (app_name, short/full description, app_icon, feature_graphic, screenshots phone/tablet7/tablet10, app_category=Medical, content_rating answers, privacy_policy_url, data_safety form per HIPAA-bound posture, target_countries=US-only-until-SOC2). Verified by scripts/check-store-listing.mjs. PASS verdict.

PROD-P14: docs/mobile/mdm-app-config-spec.md (platform-agnostic AppConfig.xml shape: tenant_slug, require_biometric, require_managed, disable_screenshots, allowed_environments, min_os_version, remote_wipe_endpoint, ttl_seconds). 4 sample payloads in docs/mobile/mdm-samples/: intune-ios-appconfig.xml + jamf-ios-appconfig.xml + intune-android-restrictions.json + intune-misconfigured.xml (negative-test). scripts/check-mdm-config-spec.mjs validates required fields + security-critical=true + HTTPS endpoint + correctly REJECTS the negative fixture. 4/4 samples expected behavior.

PROD-P14 final: packages/w5-sms-push-delivery/src/mobile-push-contract.ts — PHI-safety contract layered on top of the existing FcmAdapter/ApnsAdapter/OneSignalAdapter. buildSafePushPayload() picks visible title+body from a 15-category allowlist (opaque codes only — never patient names); validates referenceId is opaque 6-64 char [a-zA-Z0-9-_]; metadata keys snake_case + values PHI-scanned for SSN/phone/email/MRN/DOB patterns. Internal cernia_category + cernia_reference_id always present. 16/16 tests cover happy path + every PHI-rejection branch. Maps to HIPAA §164.312(c)(1) integrity + the lock-screen-no-PHI rule.

fix(voice-eval): capture final agent turn on WS close — transfer scenarios pass over real audio
fix(#8): all four scheduling flows complete over real audio (word-hour + pronoun-as-digit)
fix(#8): real-audio booking completes reliably — slot-minute + digit-homophone fixes
fix(#8): tolerate STT-garbled spoken last-4 digits (homophones) in 2nd-factor
feat(#49): echo-robust full-duplex barge-in — caller can talk over the agent

docs(#8): booking-completion proof + revert duplicate-causing flush timer
fix(#8/M1): real PSTN voice path — 7 bug fixes + live-audio + caller-bot
feat(#14/#15): outbound calls speak the message + SMS->Twilio wiring + PSTN runbook
docs(feature-plan): pre-PSTN sweep — #47 done, #45 real-audio loop demonstrated
feat(#5): self-service automations — binding vertical verified + workflow-chooser picker

pendingBlockers.title: Email RESALE core (Google Workspace reseller / Microsoft CSP / Zoho reseller)
pendingBlockers.reason: Requires APPROVED reseller agreements + per-provider API credentials + a signed BAA with each provider (≈2–8 weeks each, legal). Cannot be stubbed or dev-bypassed — a reseller agreement cannot be mocked into existence. Only W1 (BYO-email DNS health checker, reusing existing SPF/DKIM/DMARC verifiers) + the in-memory email-template routes are dev-testable.

pendingBlockers.title: Live /voice MEDIA audio (real voice media-stream)
pendingBlockers.reason: Telephony SIGNALLING + lifecycle are now live-verified on genuine PSTN calls (inbound /status signature-validated; outbound placed→/voice tenant-resolved→/status→call_sessions completed). Carrying real media-stream AUDIO needs an UPGRADED non-trial Twilio account + a signed Twilio BAA before any patient audio flows. Trial account = no BAA = synthetic/non-PHI only.

pendingBlockers.title: Stripe PRODUCTION wiring (storefront checkout + enterprise invoicing move REAL money)
pendingBlockers.reason: Test mode (sk_test_) is wired + E2E-verified end-to-end: real cs_test_ Checkout Session + a live checkout.stripe.com URL, signature-verified settlement webhook (fail-closed 400) -> persisted per-tenant entitlement (migration 147). PRODUCTION needs live Stripe keys + a business-verified Stripe account; SAME env-keyed code (no change). Drop prod STRIPE_SECRET_KEY / STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET into apps/api/.env (or launch env). No BAA (payments, not PHI).

pendingBlockers.title: LLM-provider signed BAA + production keys (LLM gateway, risk-classifier, PatientAgent narrative)
pendingBlockers.reason: PRODUCTION activation of the LLM gateway + ML risk-classifier + the citation-grounded PatientAgent narrative requires a signed BAA + prod API keys with an LLM provider before any real PHI is sent. Dev/test runs with dev keys (OPENAI_API_KEY) + SYNTHETIC data only; the BAA posture check hard-fails closed in NODE_ENV=production. Includes O3 LoRA fine-tuning compute/infra.

pendingBlockers.title: Native mobile push (Apple APNs + Firebase FCM)
pendingBlockers.reason: Native iOS/Android push needs Apple APNs + Firebase FCM vendor credentials + a signed BAA. Self-hosted browser Web Push (Q3-B, VAPID) is DONE + E2E-verified; the native-mobile fallback is the procurement remainder.
